title: Amor Fati Chardonnay San Luis Obispo Coast 2022
body_html:
Fare thee well, Mount Eden! I hope we’ll be seeing you again…but I’ll never forget that beautiful Sunday morning we spent together! But now! 94 POINTS, from even further south down the San Luis Obispo Coast…welcome back, Amor Fati!!!

YES!!! Our love of fate has brought Amor Fati absolutely ROARING back into the Last Bottle Universe…and this little Latin Lover is totally worthy to walk in the shadow of Mount Eden’s killer Chardonnay. Even less available, though, and with a shining bright 94 points from Robert Parker’s Wine Advocate — don’t be surprised to see this vanish FASTER STILL! 

Ok, I got some owls to fly, and hawks to walk at Ireland’s first ever school for falconry. (It’s EPIC, y’all) So I’m going to be quick about things. Amor Fati is a rocking Central Coast bottling, born in a moated castle that was famed for their late-night concerts, their delectable boozy Sunday brunch (yes!), and killer wines just like…THIS! You hopefully snagged some of their Syrah about a week or so back, and this is the encore! 

A classic Cali-Chardonnay weight, and barrel-driven toasty, nuttiness…perfectly honed to a razor’s edge with cool climate brightness, and salty, ocean-born acidity. The fruit hails exclusively from the Norgrove Garden Vineyard — a fantastically EXTREME site that sits just 2 miles and a bit from the roars of the Pacific Ocean. A bevy of orchard fruits, uplifted with floral notes of lemon thyme, and Mandarin blossoms, then this zippy lime leaf and pomelo, all acutely spiced with allspice, and clove, and toasted almond slivers. It’s full, not heavy, round, but not cloying, and all in all — more balanced than a juggler on a unicycle. FOR JUST SIXTEEN BUCKS?!?! You can’t have too much.

While it lasts!

title: Mount Eden Chardonnay Estate Santa Cruz Mountains 2020
body_html:
“Mount Eden Chardonnays have a strong track record for aging, this one is structured to last.” – Wine & Spirits Magazine

SUNDAY FUNDAY starts now! Mount Eden is BACK, good people!!! 95 POINTS, built to last, and clocking in at a MIRACULOUS under $20 price! Say WHAT?!?!? Some of y’all lucky leprechauns scored the winery’s Reserve Chardonnay that we featured a ways back…but for those that missed out, or those that simply can’t get enough EPIC Cali-Chardonnay (guilty!) — this one is for you! Only 935 cases made…EVER — and we pulled a rabbit to snag as much as humanly possible to share with y’all. Barely enough…even for a slow, pre-St. Paddy’s Sunday — so do yourself a solid, take the requisite 60 seconds it takes to fly to your cart, and LOAD UP!

As good as that Reserve was, and is — stylistically, this one is my jam! A bit less oak, and brighter minerality and more freshness as the result — which keeps this absolutely singing like a Lady Gaga encore. A medley of crunchy, juicy apples (Granny’s, galas, honey crisp…oh my) then this warming lemon curd character, with some grassy and fresh herbs, salty minerals, and this gorgeously exotic spice profile. Toasted walnuts and butter-seared brioche complete the package…and HOT DAMN! Sign…me…up!!!

I’m taking a couple to age, and a couple to drink with reckless and feckless abandon in the next couple of weeks — ‘cause when Mount Eden crosses your path for UNDER $20 a bottle…you simply CANNOT have too much around! A collectible California Chardonnay that brings down the house — whether you pop it today or ten years down the line, you WILL NOT REGRET IT!!!

While it lasts!

title: Macedon X Decade Cabernet Sauvignon Red Hills Lake County 2022
body_html:
93 POINTS! Robert Smith is the latest CURE for overpriced, under-delivering Cali-Cabs! Just FIFTEEN DOLLARS and primed to vanish in a flash!

Macedon X, to arms!!! Flee, foul foes of killer vino at utterly INSANE pricing — righteous rage and divine providence carries us on the mighty wings of victory! Like Alexander at the Battle of Gaugamela — history shall remember our triumph today! Man…I am clearly FIRED UP for this…what was in the farmers market coffee? No matter, onward I go! The Red Hills region of Lake County is the most under-appreciated appellation for intensely powerful, high elevation CABERNET SAUVIGNON in the entire world — and if it takes a simply RIDONKULOUS price, and the reputation of an ICONIC Napa winemaker to spread the good word and get you drinking this…so be it!!! Robert Smith is the general behind this worthy campaign — the winemaker that rattles off such notches on his belt like Quixote, Quintessa, Seven Stones, Krupp Brothers, Covert Estate, and more — and the grapes come from the volcanic, obsidian-ridden slopes, and high elevations of the Red Hills AVA just 15 miles north of Napa Valley. 

This BEAST will flatten many a $100+ bottle of Napa Cab — and is a precious gem worth seeking, even at the nearly $90 winery price. But…here? Today? The wine gods smile down upon us, and all the legions of Last Bottle faithful. A simply confounding 83% OFF?!?! My word! 

It’s inky, and unbelievably rich, with MOUNTAINS of structure, and this juicy, decadent core of black and red bramble berries, then roasted plum tarts, and cherries jubilee, and heady kirsch, black pepper, strawberry compote, and a dusting of mocha powder. If you haven’t already guessed — this is NOT FOR THE SHY! Originally started about 15 years back, making 1-2 barrel lots sold direct to their list. After their first decade, they boosted production to a whopping 18 barrels, and crafted this X-factor bottling known as “Decade”. Flash forward to today — and they craft stunning Mountain Cabs from across the North Bay, while their list and presence grows like my favorite dinosaur sponges. For a simply UNBEATABLE $15 — this is the easiest choice you can ever make…

Load up, while we got it!

title: Armida Zinfandel Poizin Dry Creek Valley 2022
body_html:
That girl is POISON!!! Ok, I am somewhat embarrassed that Bell Biv DeVoe — pioneers of the New Jack Swing genre — was the only band that popped into my head when I saw this $10 bottle of sheer, heart-racing joy. What can I say? Child of the 80s through and through. But nevermind that. Did you see this PRICE?! 

TEN BUCKS?! Load up, good people — and if you make haste…you just might be lucky enough to be sipping on this all summer long! Armida Zinfandel has popped up in your inbox a couple different times over the past few years — and every…single…time, we get flooded with consistent praise and a bevy of cool, out-in-the-world, ultra-fun bottleshots tagged to our insta. Now, you ain’t likely to mistake this for DRC, but at ten bucks — this little baddie ain’t trying to be. It’s juicy, spicy, and flat-out fun. Like wicked bomber fun, the sort of fun a Southie might have pickin’ fights with smart kids at a Hahr-vard bar like Grendel’s Den or Shay’s. It’s a forty-five-dollar-drinking Zinfandel at a ten dollar price…and sometimes (especially with the world we’re living in) that’s everything one could want. So, yeah…anywho, this newest drop is POIZIN…and it comes to play! 

Ripe summer plums fresh off the tree? Yes. Cherries of all sorts, spiced with vanilla, and black pepper, clove, and cardamom? That, too. There’s a warm, soul-filling milk chocolate and sweet, toasty oak note that carries through it all, and a lingering zip of pink peppercorns that keeps you coming back for more. Solid, solid stuff — seriously. The perfect companion to backyard BBQs, impromptu gatherings, and peaceful nights gazing up at the stars while you sit around the campfire. All without breaking the bank!

All I can say is…GET A LOT! While it lasts, of course!

title: Broccardo Barolo San Giovanni 2021
body_html:
I TOLD you!!! This disappeared even faster than I thought — but fret not — ANOTHER 96 pointer, from the epic 2021 vintage of Broccardo’s impressive Barolos is here to save the day!

Even better PRICE! Same 96, 95, 94 point scores, this time from the historic San Giovanni Cru in the Monforte d’Alba — and this small production bottling is a LAST BOTTLE EXCLUSIVE!!! 

Wooohoo!!! We’re busting out ALL the Barolo greatness today — and these 2021 Broccardo Single Crus are simply STAR-MAKING efforts across the board. I mean, 99 point, BEST-EVER vintage rating for Piemonte in Wine Spectator history, to boot? Who could say no? Can I pick a favorite? No way, no how!!! Was I planning to buy TWO CASES of Barolo whilst I’m newly landed in Ireland and staying at my favorite (but slightly pricey) 5-star castle? Definitely not. But sometimes you simply have to invoke Joel Goodson, take some Risky Business on, and just go for it!!! 

Ok — the absolute LAST thing I would ever want is for you to miss out on such a stunning and worthy Barolo TWICE. So…we have the EXACT same amount of the Ravera Cru that already vanished. Which means, I strongly suggest a trip to your cart to load up before this baby vanishes as well!!!  (We are the ONLY spot in the entire USA to have it, after all.)

Got some? Good on ya! This is bolder, and richer than the exotic and pretty Ravera — but the pristine pedigree of 2021 is every bit as prominent. Decanter calls it “drop-dead gorgeous” while the good folks at Wine Enthusiast call it “rich and persistent”...and, well…they are both dead on the nose! Roses and hibiscus, with a sanguine minerality, and hints of Damson plums, blood orange, and warm, terracotta clay. Things tighten up considerably on the silky smooth finish — hinting at a long and gorgeous life to come. I mean…96 POINTS, people! And ONLY $36?!?! 

DO NOT miss out twice!!! What a day for EPIC Barolo!!!

title: Broccardo Barolo Ravera 2021
body_html:
Yes!!! Full stop. A TOP Barolo Cru — that Decanter calls “the most renowned vineyard in the municipality of Novello” and we have this explosive, and stunningly elegant 96 POINT bottling to share!!! This is a certified steal even at the full retail price of $75…but today…for those with the fastest finger-twitch muscles…just TWO Andrew Jacksons make this yours!!! 

100% Nebbiolo, of course, from iron rich pockets in the Ravera cru which give it a beautiful minerality and sapidity. This region is among the tiniest in Barolo, with only about 17 named Ravera Cru wines released each year! This might be the best Ravera I’ve tried to date — irregardless of the price! But that KILLER price just makes this all the more tempting...in fact!!! 

96, 95, 94 POINTS — at the sort of price point you can almost NEVER find on delectable, single site Barolo — particularly from a “LANDMARK YEAR” like 2021! I’m gonna give you my notes, of course — but you might want to pull a Fonzarelli and jump the shark to your cart…’cause we’ve only really got enough for a half-offer of this seductive maiden. 2021 stands as the HIGHEST SCORING PIEMONTE VINTAGE EVER according to Wine Spectator…99 points and just about perfect…and this lives up to all the expectations and then some. The violets, the lilacs, with crimson roses, and the sort of evocative, transformative perfume that makes you fall head over heels in love! Then, one sip in, and the power and structure breaks through like a Welsh rugby try from Rhys Carré himself! (I mean, just look at that big man, go!) Red cherries, and crushed raspberries, with flecks of iron, and minty menthol coolness, with the sort of steady intensity usually found in the best deep-tissue massage you’ve ever experienced! (Hurts so good!) Ah man, this is flat-out MARVELOUS stuff! 

Take a bow, BROCCARDO! A Last Bottle Barolo favorite — and you’re gonna absolutely BACKFLIP for this stunning bottling of their single Cru beauty from Ravera! This sacred ground is composed of some of the highest plots in Barolo, protected from the worst of the weather inbound from the Italian Alps, and the conditions in this vintage has produced something that borders on magical.

I got my case — I sure hope you move like a stim-addled fox and make your own claim before it’s gone for good! While it lasts, my Barolo brethren!!!

title: Tempus Fugit Cabernet Sauvignon Napa Valley 2024
body_html:
Over 70% off on a 93 POINT Napa Cab — the wunderkind Weston Edison is back — a Russell Bevan and Jason Moore trained winemaker that continues to take the Last Bottle Universe by storm! 

Napa Cab. Under $30. 93 Points. Impeccable pedigree. BOOM goes the dynamite! Wine Wednesday, let’s do this!!! 

TEMPUS FUGIT! Yes, time flies (that’s Latin, y’all), but a Napa Cab that we scored for a melon-whomping 73+% OFF?!?! That, my friends, is going to FLY even faster!  SR-71 Blackbird, look out! Weston Edison has been soaring high across the pale blue skies of Napa Valley as of late…and we are HERE for it! A more hard-working, endlessly experimenting, focused & driven young winemaker you simply will not find in the Napa Valley today. This Tempus Fugit has often been bottled as a red blend — but with the growth of his portfolio, the opportunity to make a fully-fledged, plush and potent Napa Cab in the excellent 2024 vintage was simply too great an opportunity to miss! 

And we — you, me…US — are the lucky winners! Let’s start a bit with 2024…as these wines are just barely trickling into the worlds. Now, the 2023 was an all-time epic, potential “Vintage of the Century” candidate — and that’s not just me getting all hyperbolic, either. Antonio Galloni, however, is already driving some of the serious hype onto the 2024 vintage, saying, “As exciting as the 2023s are, I have no doubt that 2024 will be better for some, perhaps many estates.” 

He goes on to compare it favorably to the famed 2014 vintage, but perhaps even better, with “more mid-palate intensity and overall fruit than the 2014s...This will be a fascinating vintage to follow.” Umm…mic drop! 2014 is one of my absolute, tippy-top BEST Napa Cab vintages in history in my book…so color me an excited shade of fuchsia and let’s DIVE IN! 

First…the color! Inky dark, like fresh-pressed blackberry juice, and this nearly opaque core that seems to suck in the surrounding light like a collapsing neutron star at even horizon. It’s juicy, and chocolate-y, and simply jam-packed with dark berries of all shapes and sizes. Blackberry, boysenberry, elderberry — you name it. Then plums, and warm black cherry pie, with a dusting of mocha, and sultry vanilla…lush and plush to the MAX! It’s sooo stylish, and so incredibly in the signature style of his mentors — I’d expect to pay $100+ for this (which is what it retails for, by the by…) But…UNDER $30?!?! That’s crazier than your favorite “Florida man” story on a Friday the 13th, with a full blood moon — which is to say…certifiable insanity! No need to wait on this baddie — load up, drink up, and rock and roll like a landslide — ‘cause this one is here for a good time, not for a long time! 

TIME FLIES!

title: Palazzo Rivolo Brunello di Montalcino 2020
body_html:
Brunello, I love you. DOUBLE 93 POINTS and priced to fly — this is the sort of pop anytime Tuscan wonder that we all need on the regular! 

MONTALCINO! The ancient hilltop fortress village, and ancestral home of the beloved, powerful, and stylish Sangiovese Grosso grape! If you’ve been with us here at Last Bottle for any amount of time — you know that Brunello di Montalcino is one of my favorite wines in the entire world…full stop! The selection process, and limited yields, then the rigorous aging requirements — this is one of the more labor/time/$$$ intensive wines you can make in Italy. And the price and scarcity typically reflect all that…typically. But…NOT TODAY!!! We wheeled, and dealed, and absolutely stealed (yes, I know that is not a word…save your emails) to bring you this stunner! PALAZZO RIVOLO in da HOUSE! 

The best part?! ONLY $35!!! Can you even imagine? That’s like pulling up the pump in California and finding the price for a gallon of premium under $3! With critical praise like this baby’s got? INCONCEIVABLE! James calls it “classic and complex” and…yep…can 100% confirm. It’s delightfully tart right out the gate — a perky, invigorating RUSH of cherries, cranberries, and raspberries, too — all delicately drizzled with a fine Modena balsamico. With a few rough and tumble swirls of the glass, these drop-dead gorgeous aromatics of violets, and medicinal herbs come to life, followed by this dusty graphite and terracotta clay, and the lingering note of Luxardo cherries that carries you into a silky yet grippy and indubitably intense finish!

It’s a whirlwind of a Brunello, aged for five years prior to release, and yet — miraculously — as fresh, and vibrant as you can possibly imagine! A beautiful dark ruby, utterly incandescent shade of black cherry to its core…with the sort of delicate weight and surprising power that you’d more often associate with a Cru Barbaresco. MONTALCINO has done it yet again! At our simply epic price…this is a no-brainer of a magnitude usually reserved for a zombie apocalypse. Ok, y’all need to go grab some already. And I need a bit of peace and quiet to enjoy another glass!

While it lasts!

title: Monroy Wines Sauvignon Blanc Kick Ranch Vineyard Sonoma County 2023
Peake Ranch Syrah Peake Ranch Sta. Rita Hills 2022
Mystery Red Spring Mountain District 2017
body_html:
96 POINTS! Over 78% OFF! Classic and traditional Napa in the house...9 years young and ready to ROCK!!!

The 9th of March. 9 years young Mystery Cab Blend. Landing on my desk at 9 am sharp Napa time. The absolutely WORST Beatles song of all time just happens to pop up on my Spotify list. (Number nine…number 9…yuck.) Anywho…there are some powerfully dark forces at work today. Biggie Smalls — died March 9th, twenty-NINE years ago. Little Bow Wow — born March 9th, thirty-NINE years ago. I mean….SPOOKY!!!

All of that is to say…it’s MYSTERY TIME!!! ONLY $39!!! Yes! We’re taking this deal the whole nine yards, and this GORGEOUS, old-school, delightfully structured, and unimaginably complex mountain-cab blend is dressed to the nines and ready to party like it is 1999! Ok, no more nines…promise. Let’s get another number…how about 141? That’s how many dollars you save from the winery’s price today!!! That’s the sort of savings you’d give up your firstborn for, heck…even the very FIRST firstborn if I had to!!!

The long-time winemaker here studied alongside one of Spain’s best ever winemakers, and one of its greatest families, before plying his trade at an ICONIC 1st Growth Bordeaux house, and then working under a man that most historians would say is the single most important winemaker in Napa Valley history! Not too shabby, right? A sprawling property of a few hundred acres atop the hillsides of Napa, planted mostly to the traditional grapes of Bordeaux. Those grapes are blended from the best parcels and the best barrels to make this classic wine — filled with terroir and lively earthiness, with perfectly balanced berries and cherries, wildly exotic spices, and savory herb complements. It’s BOLD, and dusty, with delicious Bing cherries, and sweet rosemary, with an iron-laden backbone of impressive structure, and a wild and savage woodland character that makes me think more along the lines of Gran Reserva Rioja than anything else — but that doesn’t fully satisfy either. It’s akin, yet not quite Bordeaux — but it’s certainly not your prototypical Napa Cab Blend, either. It’s unique, memorable, and stands completely apart — like an Irishman with his hands in his pockets when the British Monarch pops in for a visit!

This is a somm-favorite, high-end restaurant mainstay that is RARELY seen at retail. I’ve been a windmill-sized mega-fan for DECADES — and a recent visit up a long and winding mountain road, way, WAY off the beaten path, reminds me just why this estate is so exceptional. No other vineyard borders it, just an isolated bowl with stunning terraces, and rolling hills, looking out across the valley and, on a clear day, all the way down towards the San Francisco Bay! 96 POINTS, people! Incredible savings, and an ALL-TIME great Last Bottle MYSTERY!

Go, go, GOOOOOOO!!!

title: Nicolas Girard Terres Blanche Menetou-Salon Blanc 2024
body_html:
Menetou-Salon!!! This Loire Valley STUNNER drinks like top-notch Sancerre...minus the price tag! A Last Bottle favorite that is sure to FLY!

Crisp, crunchy, zippy, and utterly ELECTRIC Sauvignon Blanc!!! A stone’s throw from Sancerre proper, and this “Terre Blanche” is the same fractured limestone, ancient and fossilized sea floor that makes the Loire Valley ground zero for the brightest and best white wines in the entire freaking UNIVERSE! For just TWENTY BUCKS??? Epic. 

Alright, newbies — I’m talking to you directly now — ‘cause the second the Last Bottle faithful, those Sancerre Supplicants, and Loire Valley Long-timers saw the name NICOLAS GIRARD…they jumped right to their cart and are loading up every bottle we have in stock as you read this…

So, read FASTER! Longtime Last Bottle favorite Nicolas Girard is our chosen herald of spring this year — and truly, you couldn’t make a better choice. Always exceptional stuff — zippy, fresh, and prone to leave your taste buds trembling — TERRES BLANCHE always seems to fall in my select two or three bottlings of what I’d wager is the BEST quality-for-value Sauvignon Blanc on this PLANET! 

2024 was a cool and wet growing season across the Loire, but while yields were less than ideal, the fruit that weathered the storm has shown the potential for greatness. Famed wine critic Jancis Robinson called the season “a winemaker’s vintage” and I’ll put Nicolas and the entire Girard family in a WWE-style Battle Royale with any other winemaker in the region and let these chips fall with confidence. The grassy, clear, straw-golden juice slithers out of the bottle, and I swear — a spark of struck flint and smoky minerality rises from the glass straight away. Pure and vibrant citrus notes, laser-like precision, tart-but-sweet Granny Smith apples, lemon verbena, and crushed oyster shells, and — LOOK — just trust me here…if you like Sancerre, but don’t like paying 40, 50, 75 bucks to get it these days…consider yourself covered!

Barely enough for a slow Sunday…get it while we got it!

title: The Ablaze Cabernet Sauvignon Paso Robles 2022
body_html:
93 POINTS! For UNDER $20 Paso Cab, crafted by a certified ROCKSTAR winemaker, in an homage to the style of Sine Qua Non – but for way, WAY less scratch! 

Love those light, easy reds? Then DELETE this email!

Still reading? OK then, ‘cause it is time to set the interwebs ABLAZE!! You, my friend, you lover of opulence, of juiced-up and teeth-staining ripeness, with that Go Big side of hedonistic succulence — TODAY is your day!!!! Ablaze Cabernet Sauvignon from Robert Smith (from Quixote, Quintessa, Seven Stones, Krupp Brothers, Covert Estate, and more!) is HERE!!! 93 POINTS, with flawless provenance and drinking AT PEAK form, with a cray-cray price like this??? We do everything we can to make dreams come true. But! Do it quick, the winery had barely enough to sell us!

This is somehow like Christmas in a bottle — all the good bits, anyways, and none of the congested shopping malls, boorish relatives, and shoddy sock-giving aspects. No, no, no! This is 100% HO, HO, HO – with soul-warming spicecake, and blackberry crumble, then cloves, toasty brioche, black cherry compote, and bruleed brown sugar bits…just WOW! All the richness and warmth you generally expect from a Paso Robles Cabernet, but drinking in its prime form despite its young age. This is designed to be – and I quote – “Radically Drinkable” and boy howdy, does this ever deliver on that front! Not a hard edge in sight, just harmonious, juicy goodness that coats the tongue, all while colossally over-delivering flavors considering this wicked-awesome price. I mean, 93 POINTS! Cali-Cab priced anywhere NEAR this is Rhodes Scholar rare – and destined to fly like Dumbo on Red Bull. In fact, ya know what? We’re just going to stop, because there’s not nearly enough – and the price is just TOO GOOD…so there ain’t a snowball’s chance in hell that this lasts for long…

Grab yours whilst you can!

title: Castello di Romitorio Brunello di Montalcino 2021
body_html:
“The 2021 vintage establishes a new benchmark for the wines of Tuscany. It is the best vintage to date...” – Bruce Sanderson, Wine Spectator

And…hold on to your hats!!! The new Last Bottle favorite of Castello Romitorio is most certainly back — and this 96+ POINT Brunello is a certified STUNNER! Better still…we got it at the BEST price in the USA! Over $65 BELOW retail, too??? Let’s go!

First…a warning! As monumentally fantastic of a growing season as 2021 was — a big frost in early April cut yields by as much as 50% across Montalcino…so there is far, FAR less than we’d like…and me, and every buyer, and most of the staff in the office are already loading up on this — so load up that cart first, and then come on back to read all about it, ok?

OK! Welcome back. Man, I hope you got enough! The wonder that is Castello Romitorio — and the budding legend of the young, charismatic Filippo Chia — makes this THE estate to watch in Montalcino, and this baby is flat out GORGEOUS! I tasted a special sneak preview in Montalcino back in October, and it was one of the highlights of my Italy trip. So good, in fact, I immediately bought another bottle to enjoy with the porcini mushroom three-course menu at Il Leccio after we left the estate! (If you’re ever in Montalcino…go here!)

But…THE WINE!!! A supremely elegant and aromatic style of Brunello, with laser-like precision, and layer after layer of complexity. The northwest corner of Montalcino is considerably wooded, and the estate here benefits from the cooling insulation that the surrounding woods bring. High-toned, but simply packed with ripe and plush fruits…this baby is SINGING! Violets, and peonies, with red currants, and plump cherries, punctuated with pristine minerality, and delicately roasted herbs, and…AND!!! The wine really comes to life with a few swirls of the glass and dances across your senses hypnotically. A bright lift of orange zest, and earthy tetherings of sun-baked clay, and above all else…BALANCE. This is built to last, but man-oh-man…is it ever tempting now!!!

While it lasts!!!

** Romitorio Fun Fact: Head honcho Filippo Chia’s dad traded 3 of his prized paintings for these 200 hectares of land and a castle that would become Castello Romitorio (Hermitage Castle). Sandro Chia (who also designs all their stunning labels) is a principal member of the Italian Neo-Expressionist movement – and his art is nearly as stunning as the wine inside! **

title: Beau Vigne Cabernet Sauvignon Old Rutherford Napa Valley 2023
body_html:
Old Rutherford has landed! The newest bottling from the seemingly endless hit makers at Beau Vigne — with 100 point winemaking LEGEND Julien Fayard blending fruit from some of Rutherford’s most iconic sites...including the famed GEORGES III vineyard!!!

Get this…and FAST! Simply classic Rutherford Cab, with 94 POINTS, and built with a juicy, crazy complex, core of GEORGE III Cabernet Sauvignon…well, surely we’re looking at $90 plus…right? I mean, the winery itself retails this for $150 a pop, so maybe, ‘cause you know…we’re Last Bottle, it’s closer to $60? Still not low enough? OK…how’s a full 72% OFF, and a price tag just over $40…final offer!!! 

Pretty freakin’ LEGIT, right? Beau Vigne are Napa Cab specialist, and those in the know (like Robert Parker when he called these folks “one of my favorite wineries”...) just KNOW. A bastion for incubating young Napa winemaking talent on the verge of superstardom (ahem…Kirk Venge, Dave Phinney, and now Julien Fayard, anyone?) and when the sourcing is this incredible (and the 2023 vintage?) Fughetaboutit!!!! 

Concentrated, inky black, with gorgeous crème de cassis, and a pretty little bouquet of lilacs and violets, then some serious intensity of blackberries, and black cherries, with blueberries, and molten chocolate, with cinnamon red hots, and this warm, earthy spice of cardamom, and mace, and charred rosemary…YES!!! Minty freshness and fresh bay leaves keep this cool and lengthy on the silky finish, and those barrel notes throughout give this roundness, and top-notch, cult-worthy polish.  All THAT for UNDER $45?! Just insanity!

Not as much as we’d like, so load up on your HUMP DAY stash, and shine on you crazy diamonds! While it lasts!

title: Spring Mountain Vineyard Red Blend Elivette Spring Mountain District Napa Valley 2008
body_html:
What a day to be ELIVETTED! The single greatest score in Last Bottle HISTORY (so far, at least) and we've uncovered a secret stash in the warehouse that you won't want to miss!!! 97 POINTS! And priced soooooo dang low, you can't even fathom the sort of discount we're dealing with today! Seriously...86% OFF — are you kidding me?

The low-yielding, immensely concentrated, simply STACKED 2008 vintage is here — and ready to PAR-TAY!!! This intensely dark, and brooding beast most certainly deserves the good ol’ NOT FOR THE SHY sticker of approval! 75% Cabernet Sauvignon, 13% Cabernet Franc, 6% Merlot, 5% Petit Verdot, 1% Malbec — and one sultry MONSTER of the Spring Mountain District! Powerfully bold with simply EPIC cigar box notes, then inky black and blue bramble berries, with wild fennel, star anise, roasted hazelnuts, and sweet little percolations of brandied cherries…IT…IS…BANGING! If there was a nearly TWO DECADES young wine today that just might outlive us all…

Here. Ya. GOOOOOOOO!!!

title: Grand Mirande Berthonneau Saint-Émilion Grand Cru 2023
body_html:
“A young visionary from Saint-Émilion is captivating the new generation of Bordeaux lovers” – George Hindle, Decanter

95 POINT Saint-Émilion Grand Cru! From a 6th-generation Saint-Émilion winemaker who also trained at Louis Latour with Boris Champy AND Harlan estate (under the legendary duo of Cory Empting and Bob Levy, no less!). I mean, C’MON!!! 95 points, crazy small production, and the tippy-top BEST part? 

OUR PRICE!!! Way, way under $25 a bottle, coming from a single lieu-dit that stands along the border of the revered vines of the one and only Château-Figeac…this is an absolute MARVEL! The warm, rich clays, sitting atop a monolithic sub-stratum of pure limestone…it is utterly CLASSIC Right Bank Bordeaux — loaded with earthy spice, plump dark berry fruits, and sweetly juicy plums, dark chocolate, graphite, and tart little bursts of sour cherries…WOWZA! I popped this bottle first thing Monday morning, was blown away…but then sagely hid it under my desk for the night…and guess who’s ROCKING now??? Simply amazing levels of polish, but bright and vibrant acidity, and no shortage of structure either. One of the best QUALITY to PRICE wines I’ve tasted from Bordeaux in a while — you could easily slip this into a line-up of $75-$100 Bordeauxs, and I assure you — this little beauty will win, place, or show like a prized filly in a stakes race! 

The family’s impressive history, the global wine-trotting training under some of the world’s best, the remarkable estate and quality vines — we’re looking at a perfect storm, people!!! Add in our simply unbeatable price…and, welp…WHAT are you waiting for?

While it lasts!

title: Amor Fati Syrah Santa Maria Valley 2023
body_html:
93-95 POINTS! This Nietzschean wonder of 100% single varietal, single vineyard, Santa Maria Valley Syrah is a BEAST of a discovery — especially when you can snag it at OVER 70% OFF and bring it home for less than TWENTY BUCKS!

Welcome to the worlds, AMOR FATI! Y’all know I LOVE me some Latin, and this flagship bottling makes me want to break out the toga, recite some Aurelian meditations, and assemble the legions to drink all the good vino like we’re at Maximilian Circus! Hooo-RAH!!! This inky, boldly hedonistic Syrah will certainly be there…how about you?! 

Now, Amor Fati means “to love one’s fate” — basically, no matter what good, what evil, whatever the gods of fate hurl your way…be grateful for it. It forges you, educates you, and your responses to such challenges fashion who you truly are. I love that. And the fact this deeply savory, wildly aromatic, and plushly layered Syrah cuts you to the core…all the better! Murmur Vineyard sits close to the Pacific Ocean, in the Santa Maria Valley that lies roughly halfway between Paso Robles and Santa Barbara. For this wine, we’re looking at 100% Clone 887 Syrah, fermented mostly in concrete, then aged in large format oak vats and foudres. The floral freshness, the salty/volcanic minerality, the smoky herbs, and peppery meatiness are all brilliantly preserved, accentuating a core of luscious bramble berry fruits, deeply rich plums, and baked cherry tarts. I mean…YUMMM!!!

The winery shut down in 2025 (it seems they stretched themselves a bit thin with their moated castle tasting room that also doubled as a music venue, wedding grounds, and locally-infamous Sunday brunch spot) but this wine is simply too good to not share with the worlds. That, beloved readers, is where we come in! Normally $69 at the winery — we CRUSHED a staggering deal to bring this into your hands at UNDER $20! Not a ton to go ‘round, but you’re gonna dig it — provided you get a move on and grab your six-pack NOW!

93-95 POINTS for UNDER $20?! Go for it!

title: Angles Cabernet Sauvignon Reserve Calistoga Napa Valley 2022
body_html:
70% OFF 94 POINT Napa Cab?! Let the Sunday Funday commence! 

ANGLES, not angels. Math, science, and sacred geometry — not faith and fantasy. Normally, that’s completely out of my comfort zone, but one glass of this, and I’m like Rainman counting toothpicks! It just clicks! I absolutely BUZZSAWED through a glass of this classy,  richly layered, and quintessentially concentrated BEAUTY of single vineyard Napa Cab — and at OVER $90 OFF — you’re gonna want some!!! 

Two words…Jericho Canyon. That culty phenomenon of Napa wine that has made the canyons of Calistoga’s eastern hills some of the hottest real estate for artisan, high-end, tippy-top shelf Napa Cabs. That’s the utterly PRISTINE vineyard where this unheralded, 94 POINT Angles RESERVE Cab was born. It’s also where winemaker and proprietor Nicholas Bleecher was born, too. Nick’s the youngest of three, born to Marla and Dale Bleecher – who started Jericho Canyon back in 1989. Nick grew up amongst the vines, working at least 3 hours a day in the vineyard almost as soon as he was able to walk. After cutting his teeth at one of Napa’s most idyllic properties, he left to study winemaking in college, and tour the world’s top wine regions, before returning as the prodigal son to take his place on the Jericho Canyon crew alongside such legends as Aaron Pott and Michel Rolland!!! He’s been Jericho Canyon’s winemaker since 2015, and ten years working with Michel Rolland is the sort of pedigree you can TASTE!!! Surely, this is one of the most romantic child labor stories since Dickens?  

Ok, I digress…THE WINE!  It’s Marion Cotillard-level GORGEOUS — crafted of precise and pristine proportions, with the smolder and strength of a caged tiger. Floral notes of lavender, lilacs, and deep, red roses, followed by whispers of dusty blueberries, and vanilla beans — all delightfully intoxicating and seamlessly balanced. Once it hits the tongue, an explosion of blackberries, plum tarts, black cherry cola, and toasted walnuts, with a fine, gravel-y minerality, and this chalky, ultra-fine grip of intense beefiness! WOWZA! You saw the 94 POINTS, right? And you like saving a full 70% OFF the winery retail, right? Welp, the time is now to snag the last of what’s left…so whatever are you waiting for??

Going…going…GONZO!

title: Château de Corneilla Red Blend Cavalcade Côtes du Roussillon Villages Les Aspres 2023
body_html:
Côtes du Roussillon for da WIN!!! A stunning bottle that RARELY leaves Europe...in fact — we’re the ONLY spot in the USA to get it! All for way, way UNDER $25? Yes!!! 

Y’all know what a Cavalcade is, right? It’s a parade…on horseback — and that is just the sort of vibe this wine is bringing home! It’s celebratory, it’s regal, and dignified, with history, and a defined sense of importance, a little bit of wild, unruly stallion element — and, most importantly — you simply cannot help but stop in befuddled wonderment as it passes on by…

Well…SNAP TO, good people! You do NOT want to let this little 95 POINT baddie pass you on by…and when it is priced as absurdly low as this…expect it to FLY!!! 

Half Syrah, then a quarter each Grenache and Mourvèdre…from a family that goes back TWENTY SEVEN generations of tending vines and making wines at this historic château — all the way back to 1485!!! Horses, vino, and a little bit of world-class organic olive oil are their specialties…and while I’ve yet to taste the olive oil, and I can’t imagine they would even consider letting me ride one of their ponies — this wine? It satisfies. Seriously satisfies. Côtes du Roussillon is the southernmost wine region of France, near the Spanish border, and nestled alongside the Mediterranean Sea. It’s a massive amphitheater of sunshine, created by the merger of three mountain ranges — The Corbières to the North, the Pyrénées to the West, and the Albères to the south — delivering a kaleidoscope of terroirs that can produce exceptional and long-lived wines...just…like…THIS! 

Jeb calls it “Les Aspres at its best” — and I’m ready to line up in that queue like it’s the world premier of Star Wars in 1977! These “arid lands” sit at the edge of the French Catalan region, where terraces of yellow and red clay enjoy warm days, cooling ocean breezes, and cold nights. The result is something spectacular — darkly roasted plums and blackberries, with smoky accents and plenty of spice, and this Northern Rhône-esque meaty-savoriness and playfully floral finish. It’s big, and beastly — yet oh-so smooth! Tastes like something you’d happily pay $75 plus for, if it were labeled in either the Rhône or California, but at our downright silly $22 price?!

It SLAYS! My advice? Get some for drinking now, and a few for tucking away for another 7+ years. With 95 POINTS and these sorts of savings…you truly cannot have too much!!!

While it lasts!

title: Mystery Pinot Noir Russian River Valley 2021
body_html:
Let the MYSTERY commence! 99+ POINTS, good people! The miraculous 2021 vintage, from one of the greatest to ever make Pinot Noir in California, and a new(-ish), absolutely cult-worthy and highly-coveted bottling from the top sites in the Russian River Valley!!! 

You are not, cannot, shall not be READY for this simply EPIC mystery! The greatest Pinot Noir mystery deal in Last Bottle history? I’d say so! Normally priced like Grand Cru Burgundy (and you’d better be on the list!) but today, for however many MINUTES this lasts — ONLY $79!!! Can you even imagine?!?! That’s like…way, way over 65% off! Ok, let’s do this…and while I’d normally just toss out a few snippets of heavily redacted quotes to raise the stakes a bit — A.I has absolutely ruined that possibility. 

So we’re getting EXTRA obscure here — and essentially, if you waste precious seconds trying to beat my evil genius riddlings and unravel this guarded and elusive mystery — you’ll severely risk missing out on the utterly compelling wine that could be yours at an ALL-TIME banger of a price! So choose wisely, friends — ‘cause I sure wouldn’t want to miss this!!!

  • A coterie of performers and the scent of excitement— all that’s missing from the equation is us!
  • “Trust me, they don’t know it yet, but they’re going to love you.”
  • My brother works for UPS. I like his uniforms.
  • Old-school banknotes

 

Ok, GAUNTLET thrown, you A.I.-addicted cheaters! That’s ALL you get! For the rest of y’all, here’s what it tastes like, and here’s why you are gonna BACKFLIP when a few bottles arrive on your doorstep. It’s GORGEOUSLY FLORAL. Like distilled essence of roses, violets, lavender, and peonies, with a dash of hibiscus, too. Absolutely dense, and gloriously concentrated — but without any heavy sappiness somehow. The fruits are soft, and supple notes of cherries and raspberries, with strawberry rhubarb pie, and even more cherries. The spices enrobe the fruits but never dominate — just perfectly judged accents of cinnamon, and cedar, with some roasted Herbs de Provence bundles…you know, the really fancy ones you get at the farmers market. The finish is bold, silky as a spider’s web, and ENDURING! Wowza…this is intense! Sooooo good, but even more potential to impress over the decade to come — I’d suggest you treat this like prized Grand Cru Burgundy, ‘cause in California terms…that is exactly what you’re getting here. Well-pedigreed, impeccably made, highly allocated Pinot Noir for a fraction of the regular price...don’t miss out on what may well go down as one of the GREATEST Mystery Wines EVER!!!

While it lasts!

title: AXR Proprietary Red Napa Valley 2021
body_html:
94 POINTS! The glorious 2021 vintage, and an utterly STUNNING Cabernet blend from ROCKSTAR Jean Hoefliger for UNDER $30! Let’s do this! 

You are not ready!!! Give me an A! Give me an X! And a R! A-X-R, good people! Under $30 and simply MESMERIZING! AXR has rapidly made a name for itself in Napa Valley and beyond…in no small part thanks to 100-point winemaking legend, Jean Hoefliger. The winery might still be a relative newcomer to the Napa scene, but the historic property has cranked out LEGENDARY wines for over 150 years. Now, with Jean leading the charge, the wines are the best they have EVER been – and to score this Prop Red (technically, it COULD be called a Napa Cab – 75% Cabernet Sauvignon leads the way here…), but this Prop Red way, way less than half price is a screaming banshee of a deal, y’all!

Adding in 15% Malbec, 7% Cabernet Franc, and 3% Petit Verdot from vineyards in the Oak Knoll District, Yountville, and Coombsville – this medium-weight beauty is Mariana-Trench level depth combined with a James-Joyce worthy complex masterpiece that artfully blends tons of boisterous and BOLD Napa Cab character with the peppery personality of Left Bank Bordeaux. A racy and vibrant expression awash with juicy blueberries, blackberries, and ripe plums, then tinges of wet stone, dried herbes de Provence, creme de cassis, and shavings of rich, dark chocolate. Flecks of pipe tobacco and cool graphite are infused throughout every layer to bring this all to a long, silkier-than-suede finish that is altogether entrancing and soul-warming in every way. Proper Napa juice for a price that feels impossibly budget-friendly…this is your chance to LOAD UP big on a wine you’ll be finding excuses to open for all of your friends in the years to come! 94 POINTS and ready to ROCK...YOUR...WORLD!!! 

While it lasts!

title: Meteor Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon Perseid Napa Valley 2017
body_html:
Over $110 OFF this gorgeous, entrancing, 94 POINT wonder! Meteor’s Perseid is back and ready to rock...your...world! 

No — you haven’t lost your grip on reality, time-travel is not yet real, and yes, good people…this is actually happening!

Ahhhh...Napa Cab fiends, collectors, enthusiasts, and DRINKERS…it’s HIGHLY suggested you do anything in your power to bust a move like In Living Color’s Fly Girls and snickety-SNAG this 94 POINT gem. Homie DOES play dat! 

OK. Here’s what you need to know:

  • A staggering $111 OFF current retail pricing. Yes, no typo. Hey, we know, we’re floored, too. These kinds of things only happen because we’re at ground zero here in the lush, verdant Napa Valley!!
  • The superb winemaking talents of Bill and Dawnine Dyer (local Napa legends) combined with the legendary vineyard manager Mike Wolf (Harlan, Bond, too many to name here…)
  • The vineyard is a longtime favorite selection for Andy Erickson, Tony Soter, Philippe Melka, and many of Napa’s top winemakers!
  • We said 94 POINTS, right? Galloni calls it “...rich, dense and expansive, with tons of depth and pure volume...” and straight up “scrumptious” — sounds about right!!!

 

OK — that's the condensed version, if you’re not already on the checkout page — what are you waiting for? A Fire Marshall Bill safety lecture?!?! Get a move on already!!! We'll NEVER SAY how we pulled off an astounding score like this, but just go with it (while it lasts...). Meteor Vineyard's “Perseid” Cabernet Sauvignon is revered for its Bordeaux-esque style, but here in the warm 2017 vintage, it’s added a whole ‘nother level of ripe fruit depth, and silky, powerful weight to kick up the amplitude. This will age another decade, but I’m drinking this up right now — one meager tasting portion simply wasn’t enough!!! Violets and rose petals, then lavender, and mocha, black plums, sweet tobacco, blackberry pie, spice cake, cigar box….just crazy good! If you scored any of this — you already know — 94 POINT Napa Cab with this sort of pedigree is a treat! This is one of those all-time, can’t miss, only at Last Bottle kinda deals...just carpe diem that stuff until it is gone...!

** Yes, I obviously stumbled across an old In Living Color greatest hits clip on YouTube…and relived a very consequential facet of my young adulthood. It’s probably for the best that they just don’t make TV like that anymore… **

title: Beringer Cabernet Sauvignon Reserve Knights Valley Vineyard Knights Valley 2021
body_html:
Guess WHO?!?! 97 POINT Beringer KNIGHT’S VALLEY Cab is back! One of the world’s great values here in California — for UNDER $30!!!! 

The KVR is sort of the secret weapon in Beringer’s entire portfolio. Everyone knows the estate’s flagship Private Reserve Cab, and food lovers and restaurant hoppers around the country are highly versed in their incredible (and incredibly priced) Knight’s Valley Cab from the wine list at their favorite Thursday night steakhouse…but this KVR magically snags the best of both. I mean, James Suckling gave BOTH this normally $70 KVR and the normally $175 Private Reserve — in the very same 2021 vintage — matching 97 POINT scores! Can you say, ALL-TIME-DEAL or what?

KVR!!! The utterly glorious 2021 vintage. Selected from a SINGLE block, and then whittled down from a selection of only the BEST barrels in that block to craft what just might be the dollar-for-dollar and pound-for-pound Cabernet CHAMPION of all California. Sorry, Napa…sorry, Paso…but it simply HAD to be said! And that PRICE?!?! What more could you want? 

Perfumed and floral notes of violets, lilac, and lavender pull you in like a Parisian patisserie window display, and then…POW! In your face flavors of sheer power and depth — blackberries, and plums, with black cherry kirsch, and mulberries, warm mocha, and graphite, and toasted baking spice, and…AND! It’s so incredibly expansive, and so delicately polished — it is simply unfathomable to score this at such a crazy low price. I LOVE Private Reserve — y’all KNOW that I do. But load up on this, and tuck those PR baddies away for another decade — ‘cause this flat-out DELIVERS — even now. 

That cooler Knight’s Valley freshness ensures this has another decade plus of prime drinking, certainly — but I don’t know a TIBETAN MONK with the will and training to resist drinking this on the regular!!! We got all we could…YOU’RE UP!

While it lasts!!!

title: La Massa La Massa Toscana 2023
body_html:
95 POINTS! OVER 60% OFF! La Massa’s newest release of the BEST pound for pound, dollar for dollar Toscana Rosso is here!!! YES!!!

Delectable finish? Dark, brooding, and muscular? Full-bodied and generous? Check, check, CHECK! La Massa rides again — and this iconic bottling never fails to impress! Our favorite Chianti Rogue — born in Naples, inspired in Bordeaux, and perfected in Panzano — is BACK, and all I can say is…Giampaolo, oh how I have missed you! We’ve been direct importing this little legend for a few years running — and to be completely honest, I’m running out of hyperbolic turns of phrase to describe just how EPIC this wine is…year in, year out! For our uniquely STELLAR price of way, way under $30, you get an iconic Super-Tuscan pedigree, from an incredible part of the famed Conca d’Oro in Panzano, which regularly pops to the forefront of my brain when I am asked about what the most glorious, jaw-droppingly beautiful, and absolutely mesmerizing wine region is my personal favorite. This ROCKS like you wouldn’t believe people — but now for the bad news…

2023 was tough. Low yields and lots of work went into making this the best possible wine it could be…but far, far less than previous years. Luckily for La Massa’s many, many die-hard fans — Giampaolo is one of our best friends in the wine biz…even joining us on a recent trip to Champagne, where we toured Krug, and Maxime Blin, and feasted and drank bubbly across every spot in Reims until the wee hours of the morning. The things we do to keep the good juice flowing stateside!!! One day, I’ll write a book about it. But here? NOW? All I can do is implore you to get as much as you can, as fast as you can…and then thank me later. 

This 2023 is certainly brooding, and Giampaolo’s signature concentration and texture is as rich and polished as ever. But 2023 is perhaps a touch lighter, with higher tones and greater vibrancy than more typical years. I LOVE it…and to me, it simply means I am going to drink MORE, and EARLIER than my 2021s and 2019s. Rough problem, right? But the blueberries! The black cherries! Succulent plums and blackberry coulis, with flecks of iron and roasted herbs, with punctuations of star anise, and fresh mint…just delicious! It’s layered, and focused, with uplifting acidity, and plenty of structure — yet swaddled with a softening sweetness…I need another glass, STAT! Mostly Sangiovese, then Cabernet Sauvignon, and Merlot, with the tiniest dashes of Petit Verdot and Alicante Bouschet…and just about the most perfect marriage between Bordeaux and Tuscany that you will EVER taste for under $60…much less the CRAY-CRAY price we are boasting here! 

Go get some, and be sure to LOAD UP…you might not get the chance again!

** EASTER EGG ALERT: Take a look at the back label for a little jab at the Chianti Classico Consorzio. When Giampaolo opted to leave the organization in 2002, he added a little graphic of the region’s famous Gallo Nero (black rooster) tied-up and spit-roasted over an open flame. Cheeky, right? **

title: Domaine des Herbauges Muscadet La Roche Blanche 2024
body_html:
Sell out alert!! How about some more white from Loire!!!

OVER 57% OFF! 93 POINT MUSCADET for the win! The wonder of the western Loire, crafted from a textured and zippy grape that was just too COOL for Burgundy (literally and figuratively) at an UNBEATABLE price!

Are you ready for a glass of searingly electric two-step of hyper-charged vibrancy and the ULTIMATE vinous ambrosia? I know I am!!! MOOSE-CA-DAY for the win!

If you love Chablis, or Austrian Riesling, Sancerre, or any luminously bright, floral, zesty, and zippy white wine, loaded with aromatics, and full of creamy, luscious texture…DON’T MISS!!! Domaine des Herbauges is one of the TOP estates in Muscadet, making some of the region’s best since 1864, with the fourth generation, Jérôme Choblet, crafting the wines. This is from a SINGLE PLOT, and vines up to 70 YEARS OLD, all aged for a whopping 14 months on the lees to build that textural richness, and classic Muscadet BITE on the finish. GET ME OYSTERS, STAT! The BEST part?! ONLY $15!!!

Anywho, these wines are absolutely BANGIN’ — awash with floral notes of honeysuckle and jasmine, with whiffs of lemon verbena, all rolling into this titillating core of white grapefruits, lemons, and orange oil, with this tingling, river rock minerality, and crunchy oyster shell finish. It’s piquant, and spicy, and terribly, fascinatingly EASY to knock back a whole bottle. Case buys are the way to go, no question — especially at this simply ROCKING price!

This is the wine you need on hand at all times, for all occasions. Go get ‘em, friends!

title: Nellcôte Cabernet Sauvignon Rutherford Reserve Napa Valley 2021
body_html:
96 POINTS! Nellcôte is back – and with winemaker Benoit Toquette at the helm, this is a Napa Valley Tour de Force!

Step one…GET THIS! I literally cannot stress this enough, good people. Heck, yeah! I popped a bottle of this over the weekend – and in a line-up of Guado Al Tasso, Pintia Toro, and a cult-y, shall-not-be-named $200+ Napa Cab – this was a favorite! (And the least expensive of the bunch – by quite a ways!) Over $60 OFF and under $90 for a stunning, cult-worthy RESERVE Rutherford Cab?! Well, well, WELL! The Benoit Toquette Effect strikes AGAIN!!! REALM, Teeter-Totter, Fait-Main, and MORE – this man turns Napa Cab into liquid gold like some Dionysian deity. Nellcôte is ascending, friends – and we’ve got it for the BEST PRICE anywhere!

Nellcôte is the name of the French Villa where the ROLLING STONES lived, partied, and occasionally worked while cutting their iconic “Exile on Main Street” album. So when I boldly proclaim that this winery – and this stunning new Rutherford Reserve release ROCKS…I mean it in a multitude of ways. The price! The intensity of that luscious Rutherford fruit! The silky, polished, and simply LUXURIOUS bearing…it’s beyond EPIC.

Oh yeah – it’s ALL from one single, ICONIC Rutherford vineyard. Now, I’ve been pre-emptively pulled into the drab and soulless office of our in-house legal counsel and told – in no uncertain terms – that I am NOT allowed to use this named, incredibly famous vineyard in any way. I’m not even supposed to allude to it strongly…but life’s just more fun in the grey areas, isn’t it? And so…on a completely unrelated note…who’s seen Hamilton? Man, that ROYAL antagonist in Act I? With that famous line in the musical of "I will kill your friends and family to remind you of my love..." POWERFUL stuff, right?  Welp, so is THIS!!!

Other top wineries like Schrader, Myriad Cellars, and Purlieu charge $200++ for cabernet sauvignons from this iconic location, set just west of the Silverado Trail on the eastern edge of Rutherford (and planted nearly 130 years ago by the Rutherford family)! But we’re Last Bottle – so you’ll pay a mere fraction of that today – and score a bottle every bit as delicious! Those classic Rutherford dusty tannins! That bold and silky crème de cassis character! The oodles of blackberries, and currants, with mee-maw’s strawberry jam, smoky black pepper, and this gorgeous note of peonies and red roses…DANG! The Nellcôte team mentioned a bit of rhubarb pie and smoldering sage influence, too…and I’m so on-board with that call it’s almost spooky. Get out of my brain!!!

Anywho, this is downright sexy and sultry Napa Cab to the absolute MAX, good people. The simply befuddling greatness of Napa’s 2021 vintage is flexing like some 70s era Schwarzenegger – and I honestly can’t decide to drink this all up right now, ten years from now, or both. Toss in that super-secret LEGENDARY vineyard I absolutely did not and shall not allude to directly. Absolute MADNESS – just like a certain King that lost the colonies. Sorry, must be dusty in here, my left eye keeps blinking twice…weird. Anywho…we grabbed all that we could – and now the chance is yours! Phenomenal winemaker, iconic vineyard, high-scoring, Rutherford Cab goodness – all at a simply UNBEATABLE price!

While it lasts!

title: Marston Family Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon Spring Mountain District Napa Valley 2015
body_html:
“Absolutely riveting wine as of early 2026 when I tasted this 11-year-old Cabernet from Spring Mountain. It is in the perfect drinking window.” – Jonathan Cristaldi, Decanter

Welp…I’m sold! And THE PRICE!!! Over 67% OFF…with $135 in savings — and by far and away the tippy-top BEST PRICE in the universe! Heck, the only two other spots in the USA right now that have this little baddie are selling HALF BOTTLES for more than Last Bottle’s EPIC price on full-size, 750s!!! Hmmm…no brainer, much?! Seriously, the only thing baffling about the GLOWING and gushing praise in the recent review is the final 93 point score. I mean, that’s solid, sure…but as a learned scribe, who can read in two living languages (and one dead one) — that certainly doesn’t READ like the sort of review you give to a 93 point wine! More like 97 points at least!!!

Anywho…I’m scoring this at 97 and that’s where I’m sticking it. But there’s sooooo little available, and the price is sooooo good, it’s probably a moot point anyways. So let’s jump right in, shall we? Marston Family is in da HOUSE! This family-run property has been growing ultra-premium grapes since the late 70’s, when it was a TOP-SECRET source for two of Napa’s most ICONIC winemakers – André Tchelistcheff and Philip Togni! Then, the late great Bohemian revolutionary Sean Thackrey used 100+ year-old Petite Sirah from this site to craft his 1992 Sirius red that was awarded “Best Red Wine in the World”...so yeah, this site has been STELLAR for a while now, my friends. Once the Marston’s started bottling their own wines, things didn’t miss a beat – first with rockstar winemaker Philippe Melka at the helm, then Sierra Leone native, Marbue Marke. (Caldwell, Italics, many more!)

If you scored the 2018 that we BLITZED through on Christmas Eve — you likely already know what a Defcon 4 STUNNER this baby is. Not quite as GARGANTUAN as the 2018 (likely just a bit more softened and supple thanks to the ever falling sands of time…) this 2015 is CRUISING! Cedar, and crushed raspberries, with black currants, and orange peel, with silky chocolate truffles, and charred rosemary, with a little POP of vanilla bean and cardamom coming to life on the finish. It’s a little tight on first pour, but a bit of swirling gives this the weight and substance that a 100% Cab from the upper reaches of Spring Mountain deserves! The structure is still quite impressive — and this will continue to impress for another decade — but HOT DANG! It is soooo luscious, so deeply complex, and soul satisfying…I simply can’t summon up the willpower to age this any longer!!! Much like the 2018…powerful aromatics, powerful fruit, powerful finish. For $135 BELOW the winery price…how could any Napa Cab lover resist?!

While it lasts!

title: Domaine Albin Jacumin Châteauneuf-du-Pape Rouge La Bégude des Papes 2022
body_html:
92-94 POINT CH TEAUNEUF! Jacumin’s La Begude Des Papes is a longstanding Last Bottle favorite that we scored for a full 50% OFF!

Who’s ready for CdP?!?! Ever since I tasted these components from both barrel and concrete tank, deep in Jacumin’s underground cave with Albin and Agnès (and their ghost grey Weimaraner pup) back in February of 2023, I’ve fallen madly in love with the precision and depth of Jacumin’s wines. This 2022 is somehow, miraculously, even slightly better than last year’s stellar 2021 – and if you scored some of that beauty…you KNOW! 

55 year old vines, infused with this gorgeous, cherry compote note, then lavender, and this beguiling note of almost tropical dragonfruit and rambutans…WOW!! Crazy minerality too, with this flinty, dusty, fine-grained and laser-focused character and precision…just exceptional! So layered and complex – it’s gotta be at least $60, right? No?! $45? Go LOWER. $30 – yes, I’m fo’ real, fo’ sho’...only THIRTY DOLLARS! Insane price and just everything you could want in top-notch CdP – at a decidedly un-CdP like price!

Even more flavors of black plums, and raspberries, even more black cherries, with exotic mulberries, milk chocolate, black pepper, strawberries…DANG!!! Velvety smooth, with a gentle grip that makes it linger on the finish. Powerful, but delicate, with ripeness that is never heavy or over the top…just PERFECTION!

70% Grenache, 15% Syrah, and 15% Mourvedre – all from some of the most INCREDIBLE vineyard land in Châteauneuf. Picture this – gnarled, haunted-looking old vines shooting up through the cobbled stones of Châteauneuf’s highest sun-baked plateau as a kaleidoscope of warm, refracted light paints the horizon...and all just a stone’s throw from Chateau Rayas, and Chateau Vaudieu, not to mention the 12th-century ruin of Pope Clement's palace in the backdrop – just...freakin’...MAGIC! The newest release of one of the greatest values on CdP we’ve ever found! Take me back, oh mighty and kind Bacchus! 

Until then…drink this! 92-94 POINTS and 50% off! While we got it, good people! 

** Ever since my visit to Albin and Agnès' incredible house, tucked just under the ruins of Pope Clement’s abode, I’ve been trying to match Albin’s impressive selection of digestifs. I’m not much further along than I was last year… every time I get a good bottle it disappears! **

title: Cave De Tain Hermitage Blanc Nobles Rives 2019
body_html:
He, DJ Khaled...what's that line again???

ANOTHER ONE! And...ANOTHER ONE! Shut. The. Front. Door!

Y'all ROCK! And so does this! HERMITAGE BLANC! 95 POINTS! For half...HALF(!) what this wine normally sells at! This is an all-time coup, my friends! It has been FOREVER since we found a Hermitage Blanc that we could share – these wines account for such a TINY portion of an already MINISCULE appellation in the Northern Rhone...and most whites from here START in the triple digits, and climb faster than Maverick in Top Gun.

HOW...just how do you evoke the beautiful essence of ripe white peaches, Asian pears, golden apples, Ruby Red grapefruits, plus honeysuckle, gunflint, kaffir lime, Tahitian vanilla, toasted hazelnuts, and lavender from grapes? How do you make it so utterly fantastic to drink? And HOW ON EARTH do WE scoop up a 95 POINT bottle of uber-delicious Hermitage greatness at 50% OFF?!?! It’s just...well, INCONCEIVABLE!! 

Seriously, this is wonderful, compelling, and utterly complex vinous perfection – shockingly good at this price, too! Huge aromatics and a rich creamy texture, but it's not heavy – it's crisp and pristinely balanced. Mostly Marsanne, with about 5% Roussanne to round things out. A touch of new oak gives it depth and spiciness to soften and integrate all the astounding fruits going on. Awesome. Can age a few years, like up to a decade plus too. We only wish we had more!

title: Domaine Paul Jaboulet Aîné Domaine de Thalabert Crozes-Hermitage 2020
body_html:
WOW!!! CdP's went FLYING out the gate this morning! We had 6 bottles lined up...and y'all hungry hungry Rhone Rangers gobbled up every...last...one! So let's head north, shall we? Bring on Jaboulet – one of the Northern Rhone’s BEST for two centuries!

We missed out on the 2019, but 2020, at long last, has ARRIVED!!! Domaine de Thalabert’s treasure OLD VINES at this planet-shaking price? Scores from the pros running 92-94 POINTS!?!? We still get that rush of warmth in the belly just thinking about it!! WOW! Big red Rhone drinkers, this is ALL about you! Last Bottler Fan Favorite vino for years and years! YES!!!

CHARISMATIC. TERRIFIC. EXPRESSIVE. JUICY AND ENERGETIC. GREAT FINISH.

Not our words (that’s the pro critics talking there), but let us say this: This is INTENSE! If you like watching Olympic Skeleton races or hopping into a rusted-up ZIPPER ride o’ death at your local county fair each summer...THIS is definitely your wine – bold, daring, and ultimately as rewarding and satisfying as other Rhones at TWICE the price!

The price!! Hair-raising. Mouth-dropping. Only $29?!?! For single-parcel, signature JABOULET prestige, power, and extreme elegance worthy of aging two decades?! Insanity. Now, Jaboulet, of course, is one of the greatest Northern Rhone producers, and we scored a SUPERB deal on the new-release, absolutely palate-rocking Thalabert Crozes-Hermitage. As with most of the top Jaboulet wines, you’re going to need to sit down when you take that first whiff (and keep sitting down because it swirls and whirls and changes DRAMATICALLY with air) – INHALE the freshly crushed, ripe, picked-at-peak-perfection blackberries and blueberries, the lush and plush, creamy baked tart notes, smoky bacon, crushed granite minerality, that sweet violet and lilac perfume, heady incense, and savory spice…just AHHH! 

Read up on the storied history (the 1961 Hermitage La Chapelle is often argued as one of the greatest wines ever made, for example) HERE, if you like...but WHATEVER you do, get this into your cart as fast as possible. One of the great wines of the world at a simply outrageous price! While it lasts…!

title: Domaine de la Janasse Châteauneuf-du-Pape Chaupin 2022 (Magnum 1.5L)
body_html:
DOMAINE de la JANASSE, people! One of Châteauneuf-du-Pape's greatest stars at an unbeatable price! 98 POINT MAGNUMS for the win!!!

Just when I thought I had reached my peak, and this day of wonder and bewilderment couldn’t possibly get any better — a little 98 POINT Janasse CdP MAGNUM comes bursting into my life! Just in case you’ve been living under a rock…Janasse is one of the world’s foremost champions of Grenache, not to mention one of Châteauneuf-du-Pape's greatest stars, and this remarkable Cuvée Chaupin just might be one of the best CDPs I’ve ever tasted! Maybe it’s the OVER 100-year-old Grenache vines in La Crau? Or maybe it’s the taut precision and cool, James Bond elegance of the less-than-ideal 2022 growing season – a cooler vintage that is hallmarked by floral prettiness, and zippy acidity, with angular structure. Whatever the case – this ain’t no juicy jam box of a Cdp, that’s for sure! But still…absolutely LOADED, like an 80s stockbroker, with frenetic energy, and furious intensity. This guy KNOWS how to party. Soooo goood!!!   

Violets, and black tea, rose petals, lavender, strawberries, blackberries, and raspberries galore! There’s this salty, iron ore and crushed rock minerality, and this beautiful freshness of raw cedar, and camphor, with tingly orange zest, and this uplifting essence of star jasmine. Uber cool, incredibly pretty stuff for CdP, that has plenty of ripe fruit character, without any sun-dried heaviness or jammy aspects. This is such a baby that I’m looking for that hypnotic scent on the soft spot on top of the cork – but really, it’s here in the glass. Decant this, or lay it down for drinking over the next two plus decades, or just pour a glass and swirl…and swirl…and keep swirling until this beauty whispers in your face and tells you she’s ready. 

It’ll be worth the wait…I promise you! 98 POINTS! While it lasts!!! 

** The legendary Aimé Sabon started the estate in the 70s, handing it over to his children – who have continued to impress – the critics, other CdP estates, the whole of France, and grizzled ol’ wine slingers like us! They’ve cracked the 100 point mark more than a few times, and just seem to be on a never-ending ROLL of ginormous proportions. Collectable stuff, indeed! **

title: Domaine Giraud Châteauneuf-du-Pape Les Galimardes Rouge 2020
body_html:
DOUBLE 95s! TRIPLE 94s! A veritable cornucopia of critical praise for this pedigreed, limited production bottling, AND free shipping?!?! Let’s do this!!!

Domaine Giraud’s Les Gallimardes, good people! Is this an EPIC day of CdP greatness or WHAT???? Organic farming, long-reaching family pedigree, simply exceptional lieu-dit sourcing — and a WHOPPING $51 OFF??? Yes, please!

The critics call it “remarkably silky and fresh"…with “grace and polish, balance and finely cut texture…” that’s “creamy and well put together…” and “a forward, charming, sexy profile…” that finishes with “hedonistic appeal and age-worthiness…” I mean, C’MON!!! 

This utterly SPELLBINDING wine was crafted from a plot just south of the CdP village, in the Les Gallimardes lieu-dit, where 100-plus-year-old vines sit atop the region’s iconic “rolled stones” the size of a baked potato. 90% Grenache and the balance Syrah fully STUFFED with loads and loads of red berries and plums, plus black cherries, a smattering of Herbes de Provence, black truffle, mocha, damp earth, charred wood, and a lick of licorice and peppery spice…BOOM! Rich, herbaceous, hedonistic, and epically complex enchantment abounds in every…single…sip! This is one CdP you don’t want to pass by!

Load up!

title: Le Clos du Caillou Châteauneuf-du-Pape La Reserve 2021
body_html:
“The 2021 Châteauneuf-du-Pape La Réserve turned out magnificently and is one of the highlights of the vintage.” – Antonio Galloni, Vinous

BOO-YEAH!!! Clos Saint-Jean may have gone the way of the dinosaur (I told you it would!), but instead of tissues, tears, and pats on the back – I’m here to offer something truly salvatory. 

La Réserve 2021!!! Cooler than Arthur Fonzarelli, Steve McQueen, James Bond, Thomas Shelby (ok, that’s pretty damn cool) — this Clos du Caillou flagship was blessed with TRIPLE 95 POINTS — and I’m here to say…totally worthy! (And maybe deserving of a couple more, but I get it -– lean, elegant, and precise CdP’s can’t seem to get those same really HIGH scores as the jammy, spice and fruit bombs from warmer, more classic vintages.)

But as fascinating as 95 point scores are – this wine is all about the aromas and flavors, baby! That, my friends, is where this wine SHINES!!! Impressively floral, with roses, kirsch, and violets, some creme de cassis richness, more ripeness than their Les Quartz, but still incredibly elegant...and POWERFUL!!! This La Réserve carries a less traditional blend of nearly equal parts grenache and mourvèdre, and the latter really showcases its intensity here. Blood orange, and then juniper berry, with Herbs de Provence, fennel, and a tang of salty ocean air give this incredible depth and a hold-on-to-your-hat rollercoaster ride of sensations. This is youthfully powerful, with an intensely structured finish that is really going to STRUT over time. Put this one down a couple of years if you can, and watch the magic happen! 

This flagship for Clos du Caillou hails from some gnarly 70-year-old vines in the lieux dits of La Guigasse and Pignan — the latter sandwiched between the enviable vineyard of Chateau Rayas and Vaudieu, with Guigasse sitting just to the north of there. This is the CdP Northeastern plateau — a higher-elevation exposure studded with those famous potato-sized stones, and known for the most-expensive, longest-lived, treasure-worthy CdPs on the planet! This is just such a wine — and we’ve got it for one heck of a great price!!! Only a handful to share – but what a steal…while it lasts, that is!

title: Clos Saint-Jean Châteauneuf-du-Pape Vieilles Vignes 2022
body_html:
Man, can it get any better? Of course! How about this 94 POINT Clos Saint-Jean CdP! 100-year-old Grenache, blended with some of the region’s best sites, in a TOP CdP vintage – for the BEST PRICE in the entire world!!!

CLOS SAINT-JEAN, good people!!! This little baddie flat-out MOVES. Like Brock Bowers after first contact, or me…when it’s Mardi Gras and the third Sazerac of the evening takes hold. (True story…got the beads to prove it!)

Point is…go fast, my friends. I mean, C’MON! Read this excerpt from the 94 POINT review: “jumps at you with perfumed orange rind, fresh violets and ripe and red black fruits, all paired with licorice and spice box aromas. Neatly maintaining balancing freshness, velvety tannins envelop the 2022. It checks out with a refined and juicy conclusion.” And it truly IS juicy — coating every nook and cranny of your mouth with deep, ripe, warm blackberry pie and strawberry jam, plus these spicy, savory, and gorgeously opulent notes of roasted herbs, fennel, and...AND!!!

2022 has been described by Wine Spectator as “a ripe, powerful vintage” with reds that are “lush, richly styled, silky and balanced,” and I’m quite certain that they were swirling a glass of this exact wine as they wrote that. Hot DANG, this is good! Thanks to a rather isolated but intense maelstrom in the La Crau — one that hit the century-old vines of Grenache that are so often the backbone of this cuvee – very few critics have gotten to taste this gem — but in this case, that means more…for…US!!!

We are talking Clos St. Jean, after all — this winery can do no wrong — just Google the masses of huge scores and critical acclaim over the years. For UNDER $50?! That’s a simply INSANE price — and you simply cannot, will not, SHALL NOT beat it anywhere on this planet! What ARE you still doing here?! Go get ‘em while they last...and more KILLER CdP to come!

** “This may well be one of the most exciting addresses not only in France, but in the entire wine world.... In short, these wines are not to be missed!” – Robert Parker, Jr. **

title: Domaine de la Janasse Châteauneuf-du-Pape Rouge 2021
body_html:
DOMAINE de la JANASSE, people! 93 POINTS and UNDER $40 for a vinous icon’s EPIC and hyper-fresh 2021 CdP!

“Domaine de la Janasse is one of the great winemaking estates of not only France, but of the world." – Robert Parker, Jr.

Right? RIGHT???? The simply legendary Sabon family has been revered as Châteauneuf-du-Pape princes, pioneers, and perfectionists for DECADES! Grenache masters, with access to some of the tippy-top BEST plots of old vine, gnarly soils, and cobbled stones of CdP’s upper plateau — and the sort of traditional winemaking to make it all SHINE! 2021 may have initially been considered a “difficult” growing season for CdP…but I say bring…it…on!!!  A cooler year in the new normal of Southern France’s already warm climate — giving way to leaner, meaner, and racier than ever! It’s old vine Grenache, with bits of Syrah and Mourvedre, sooooo succulent and juicy, but definitely not shy on horsepower! Imagine if a top Burgundy house got a chance to play with world-class Grenache grapes…and you’re entering my orbit of what my friends at Janasse are cooking.

And it’s straight FIRE! 

From just 15 hectares in 1967 to nearly 90 today — this is no longer a small producer — but a true CdP JUGGERNAUT that sits as the cream of the crop for Châteauneuf-du-Pape. Each of the over 60 plots is farmed sustainably, and harvested painstakingly by hand, with each lot vinified separately — then blended with the utmost care and precision. The result is FLAT OUT spectacular…and quite honestly, my touch-blue-make-it-true FAVORITE iteration of this cuvée to date! We don’t have much, but at our simply jaw-dropping price — and FREE SHIPPING — you’d be savvy to score a few!

While it lasts!

title: Château de Nalys Châteauneuf-du-Pape 2020
body_html:
CdP Day 2026 edition starts...NOW! 95 POINT Château de Nalys at the lowest price in the USA! 

Who’s ready for Châteauneuf-du-Pape Day?! THIS GUY! We’re charging into the weekend blowing out a whole HEAP of top-notch CdP’s — one right after the other, mini-marathon style. These beauties are CRAZY limited, and at the absolute BEST prices in the USA, so whatever you do…DO NOT hesitate! Oh yeah…FREE SHIPPING ON EVERY ORDER! Huzzah, my people! Now, down your quad espresso and STAY ALERT! THIS IS GOING TO BE A BLAST! Starting…Now!!!

Château de Nalys in the house! This is one of the oldest estates in CdP — and it just got a MONSTER glow-up! Purchased by the one and only GUIGAL family in 2017, this has become the Southern Rhône JEWEL in the Guigal crown…and this flagship wine is seriously turning some heads! Old vines, stony soils, world-class winemaking…YES! 95 points from Wine Spectator is a stunning achievement, and the instant a drop of this hits your glass, the perfumes EXPLODE upwards, delivering brain-altering amounts of heady fruits, and TONS of spice, with the richness and polish that has become a Guigal signature…WOWZA! 

Grenache, Syrah, Mourvèdre, Counoise, and Vaccarèse — mostly from the fabled plot of La Crau — this is the sort of lip-smacking, mouth-drooling CdP juice that makes me want to cry out with sheer, unadulterated pleasure. Winemaker Ralph Garcin continues his meteoric rise, and with stops at JABOULET, and the cult-y Château La Nerthe…his penchant for crafting world-class Rhône goodies continues. ONLY 500 CASES were allotted for the USA…we got every last bottle we could…but this is going to FLY!

More CdP to come, but with FREE SHIPPING on every bottle, now is the time to beef up that collection!!!

title: Field Recordings Red Blend All the Rage Santa Barbara County 2022
body_html:
93 POINTS! For UNDER $13?! What have we here...? My newest house red — that’s what!!!

How far out is this? SO FAR OUT!!! That’S the name, and most definitely the vibe we got for you on this fine Thursday! We blew it out yesterday with a bold, powerful, and utterly hedonistic Napa Cab — and so today, we’re playing the role of KitchenAid…and MIXING IT UP! 

Ooof…bad pun…deserves a paddling, fo’ sho. But I popped this little baddie not more than 30 seconds ago, and I…am…deliriously GIDDY! Seriously trembling with pure, unbridled pleasure — ‘cause that is what this seductive ambrosia is all about!!! It SOARS out the glass with bubbly, wild notes of elderberries and blackberries, then slightly carbonic black cherries, and this homemade strawberry jam character. But don’t forget the wild herbs! Sage, and sassafras, with tingly pink peppercorns, and rhubarb, and tomato leaf…just, DANG!!! It straight up dances across your tongue, with this almost pop-rocks-esque frenetic tension, and clean, pure berry goodness that feels like you walked out of the wilderness after five days without food, only to stumble into a berry patch right at its glorious, end-of-summer peak of ripeness. Somehow — despite all that — this is neither sweet nor tart…just the perfect balance of both. And…93 POINTS!!! 

For a decidedly hip, totally counter-culture wine like this…that just shows how exceptional this little beauty is! Cool blend of 65% Cabernet Sauvignon, 23% Merlot, 6% Syrah, and 6% Marselan, too. I’d call it a unifying style between two warring factions. If you like them au naturel and funky — you’re gonna love this. If you like them traditional, fruit-forward, and polished…SURPRISE! You are REALLY gonna love this! It just WORKS! Medium-bodied, with a silky texture punctuated by little pops of tannin — serve this on a warm day (they’re coming back soon, right?) with a slight chill and watch the absolute MAGIC that ensues. For just TWELVE DOLLARS?!?! Insanity.

Go BIG is all I have to say. Not nearly as much as we’d like, so grab yours whilst the getting’s good!

title: Edict Cabernet Sauvignon Collinetta Oakville Napa Valley 2023
body_html:
94 POINTS! From 100-point winemaking phenom, Julien Fayard, this glorious OAKVILLE Cabernet Sauvignon is ready to rock...your...WORLD!

Julien Fayard. Mark Herold. Jay Bouncristiani. Umm…starstruck much?!?! This magical high-powered trio are the wizards behind the curtain at Edict — and at our simply MIND-MELTING 67+% OFF price — you’d be wise to throw your name in the Goblet of Fire for this deal! Why, you ask? You know, other than the cray-cray, UNDER $40 price? Or the high-flying 94 POINT score that calls this a “distinctly Oakville” GEM? Or the fact that we are talking about the simply MARVELOUS “as perfect as any Napa vintage in living memory” 2023 growing season (thanks, Karen MacNeil, for the quote!) Hmm… I can’t imagine why…at all! 

PSYCH!!! This slays like Ser Duncan the Tall in a seven-on-seven tournament to clear his name…and it tastes like the quintessential OAKVILLE CAB that it is — without the $120+++ price tag. Seriously, if you love the gorgeous violet and lilac aromatics, with that grounding minerality of volcanic soil, and crushed gravel, interspersed with that wild bramble berry character, and a medley of roasted bay, and thyme, and all manner of culinary herbs — your ship has come in!!! 

I mean…Julien Fayard, people! This guy’s CV reads like a 3 Michelin-star wine list! Lafite Rothschild, Gemstone, Covert, Smith Haut Lafitte, Somnium, Beau Vigne, and his own Purlieu, among many others. He’s one of the biggest winemakers in the world right now, and this beauty demonstrates WHY. Then there’s Mark Herold — a long-time ICON of Napa’s boldest and most unapologetically MASSIVE Napa Cabs, and this fall in-step like a perfectly coordinated free skate performance from Riku Miura and Ryuichi Kihara.
