Delicate yet substantial, immediately gratifying yet structured enough to provide all kinds of pleasure for years to come. WOWZA!

We got every last bottle, every last case that the Antinori family would share, and we’re bringing it to you at over $40 LESS than the winery retail…and if you love Antinori’s Tignanello, Solaia, or Guado Al Tasso – you simply MUST try a few bottles of this Brunello Baddie!!! Just a couple miles southwest of the ancient Montalcino fortress, the Pian delle Vigne plot of vines sits near the village of Camigliano, less than 15 miles from the Tyrrhenian Sea. Sangiovese HEAVEN, I say! You’re gonna love it…but you better HURRY!

** If you read our nerdy details – you’ll see I paired this with a classic Tuscan pasta…Ragú al Cinghiale. Now, if you don’t have your own wild boar supply – pork shoulder works in a pinch, and to get that wild, game-y, umami-rich funk, here’s my pro tip: Marinate chunks of pork with a bit of chopped anchovies and some fish sauce (Roman is better than Asian, but use what ya got). Just a bit goes a long way, but the flavor is incredible! **

title: Cordella Brunello di Montalcino Riserva 2019
body_html:
We blew through the Il Poggiolo in a flash—now the spotlight shifts to the 95-point Cordella, and it’s ready to steal the show!

2019! Perhaps the greatest Tuscan vintage of the last century, and my word, did we score a RISERVA to remember! 

CORDELLA in da casa!!! STUNNING, racy, lip-smacking, and heart-thumping BRUNELLO RISERVA making up the second leg of this Monday two-step…and WOWZA, what incredible moves this baby has! I couldn’t even DREAM of this kind of swagger – even in the olden time of two good knees and a carefree attitude towards public shaming. But one sip of this and I am straight dreaming of a different sort! Sunshine, and rainbows, the rolling Tuscan hills, the sweeping tall and golden grasslands, with ancient stone fortifications rising high in the distance…take…me…BACK! 

This absolutely DELICIOUS Riserva takes everything I love about the 2019 vintage (the depth, the balance, the absolute purity of fruit, and spice) and distills it down to its very essence in this inky, concentrated, and lush bottle o’Brunello goodness! At the BEST PRICE in the entire USA…who could resist?! 

Not ME! Cordella is a long-time favorite here at Last Bottle – but we never, I repeat, NEVER get Maddalena’s best of the best RISERVA!!! There’s simply never enough to share, and Maddalena only makes it the tippy-top best vintages. It’s been several years since her last Riserva bottling, and all I can say is…WORTH THE WAIT! The roses and violets, with black tea, and crushed cherries that pull you in, then your mind reels from the waves of heady spice, and sweet balsamic, then plums, and hibiscus agua fresca, and terracotta, and…AND! Completely coats the mouth with a single sip, and then hangs on like a banshee of pure vinous delight – for easily a full minute or more!  At UNDER $60 – this is a STEAL of killer, outrageously delectable Brunello…we just wish we had MORE! 

95 POINTS on a 2019 Brunello Riserva falls smack dab in that “no brainer” territory – but at our EPIC price? Simply INCONCIEVABLE! 

While it lasts!

title: Il Poggiolo Brunello di Montalcino 2019
body_html:
95 POINT Brunello!!! For the absolutely stark-raving MAD price of UNDER $30!!! A legacy Brunello, from one of the wine’s first producers, grown on their high-elevation estate...what a way to kick off the week! 

Monday…Montalcino…Madness! I call it mmm, for short – and boy howdy, is it ever DELICIOUS! Brunello di Montalcino is the top dog of Tuscan wines in my book, and it’s crazy to think that despite hailing from a top estate – and just the 28th producer to make Brunello over 55 years ago – this FLAGSHIP wine can be yours at such an incredible price! A chest-crushing 450 meters above sea-level, just about a mile southwest of the ancient Montalcino fortification, the incredible Il Poggiolo is in da HOUSE!

Only a stone’s throw from a handful of other Montalcino greats like Le Potazzine, and just across the valley from Castello di Romitorio, Rudy Cosimi has a prime 65-hectare parcel passed down from his father. Rudy has become rather infamous in Montalcino as a rather polarizing character – with a sharp tongue and no internal filter – but when the wine is this good, you tend to make allowances! And, my word…is it ever good! Incredibly vibrant and pure notes of black cherries, and sandalwood, followed by a medley of raspberries, peonies, red currants, roasted coffee beans, and vanilla. The sweet red fruit sensations carry seamlessly as the wine’s core, but spice accents, zippy acidity, and grippy, powerful tannin structure extend this through into a long and lasting finish. The 95 POINT review suggested waiting until after 2025 to start drinking…and guess WHAT?!?! 2026 is here, baby! Plenty of life to come on this tightly-packed baller of Brunello – and with a price like this, you’d be wise to get AT LEAST six bottles. 

Not nearly as much as we’d like…so make haste, and snag top-notch Brunello at OVER 60% OFF! 

While it lasts!

title: Adroit Carignan Vincenzo Vineyard Redwood Valley 2022
body_html:
And…so long Ribolla Gialla! I hardly knew ye! (Two cases should take care of that, though.) Up next…even more Adroît! This 92 POINT red – for the same EPIC price, is guaranteed to turn some heads!!!

Three cheers for CARIGNAN! An ancient grape, and one that deserves way, WAY more love from everyone! Originally migrating up from northern Spain, taking root in southern France, and then becoming one of the most important and widely planted grapes in all of France! In fact, until Merlot dethroned it in the 1980s, this was the NUMERO UNO grape in France, and had already migrated around the world as a staple grape in nearly every New World wine region.  Napa and Sonoma plantings go back to the mid-1800s, and RIDGE is widely believed to have crafted the first ever varietal bottling for the Golden State back in the early 1970s. The grape has trucked along quietly ever since, and I think it is LONG OVERDUE for a chance on the main stage.

So…here…we…GOOOOOOO!!! UNDER $13 for a Wine Advocate 92 POINT red from a single vineyard originally planted back in 1918…I mean, that’s a STEAL!!! Most of these vines are 65-70 years old, with an infusion of new plantings in 2015 to give some bright and robust fruit burst character, but dagnabit…is this ever DELICIOUS! Master Somm turned winemaker Chris Miller has done it again – and this beauty sings with wild blueberry and strawberry fruits, followed by dusty, lightly scorched herbs like rosemary and thyme, and tart little plums, then dried cherries, and a kiss of grapefruit peel, and compressed watermelon, with a final dash of hibiscus tea. Crazy crushable, but complex enough to both age and match perfectly with some hearty fare…anybody wanna head to Yountville’s Mustards Grill for their famous Mongolian Pork Chops?! This is gonna CRUSH with that little bite of heaven. Aw man, that’s it…that’s my plan for Sunday night. See you there!

Grab more than you think…this will FLY!!!

title: Adroit Ribolla Gialla Basalacchi Vineyard Lodi 2022
body_html:
Italian wine lovers! One of the rarest plantings in California comes roaring to life – at a price that cannot be beat! 92+ POINTS from the good folks at Robert Parker’s Wine Advocate...and we SCORED it for UNDER $13!!

Adroît…welcome to the Last Bottle Universe! Single vineyard, rarer than a Pittsburgh Blue steak grape, critical praise, pedigreed winemaking, jaw-dropping price…let’s see…did I miss anything?!?! Oh, YEAH!!! Another bottle! Between my content team, and myself, and one of our intrepid buyers that just happened to be walking by as I popped the cork – this beauty is DONE-ZO! Like the green flash of a San Diego sunset…if you blinked…you missed it! Ribolla Gialla, good people! No, that’s not the name of some up-n-coming Italian starlet…it’s an ancient grape, nearly 1000 years old, and grown almost exclusively in northern Italy’s Friuli/Venezia region, with a bit bleeding over the border into Slovenia. Here, in California – you’re looking at LESS THAN 20 ACRES…total!!! After a bottle of this, and another already chilling on ice – I have absolutely NO IDEA why it isn’t more widely planted…and if you’re fast enough to snag some of this, I have no doubt you’ll be jumping on that bandwagon right alongside me soon enough. This…ROCKS!

Seriously, lip-smack, scrumptious, and textured, zippy, salty, fruity, waxy, and floral…and MORE still! Chris Miller – the same Master Somm turned winemaker behind Seabold Cellars – has done a doozy here with this gem. 45 days ON THE SKINS, neutral oak barrels for aging, and an utterly unreal level of complexity. Orange blossoms, and beeswax, with a dusting of crushed, raw corn, and orange marmalade, with powdered chalk, and lemon balm, sage, and saltwater taffy, too! Dang!!! Golden, almost amber in color, with the weight of a full-throttle chardonnay…but none of the buttery heaviness. Bright, fresh, and intoxicating…where HAVE YOU BEEN all my life?! 

UNDER $13?! What a deal…what a wine! While it lasts!

** Ribolla Gialla was famously called out by the 14th-century Italian poet, Giovanni Boccaccio, as being a sinful wine that leads its lovers to gluttony. I must confess…GUILTY on all counts! **

title: Peake Ranch Pinot Noir Sta. Rita Hills 2022
body_html:
92 POINT Pinot Noir on the board! Peake Ranch is BACK – and how!!! 

“The 2022 Pinot Noir Sta. Rita Hills is terrific…spicy…complex…floral…incredibly satisfying…” – Jeb Dunnuck

Time for that hound-dogging, wolf-whistling golden age of cartoon moment…AHHHH-WOOOO-GA!!!! Oh, baby – pick your jaw up off the floor, roll your tongue back into your mouth, and (gently) re-insert those bulging eyeballs back where they belong! PEAKE RANCH rides again!!!! Y’all have snatched up every last bottle of Peake Ranch we’ve ever featured…ravenously. Today? We’re rolling out this never-before-offered bottling of their excellent 92 POINT 2022 Santa Rita Hills Pinot. Only $23, too?!?! YES!!!! 

Just to re-cap in case you forgot – Peake Ranch is an ELITE S.R.H. winery, with an impressive array of UC Davis alums running the show like a full-time Masters Class symposium. It’s an impressive think tank of vinous talent…blending fruit from two famed sites: The legendary John Sebastiano vineyard and their own Peake Ranch estate, with a near equal split of California heritage and Burgundy clones (667, 115, Calera, Hyde, Pommard, and Swan)...simply spellbinding! Check it out!

Mike Anderson: winemaker. He served as a researcher, author, and educator at UC Davis for 30+ years — including running their Oakville Station program (you know, the heart of TO KALON vineyard that was handed off for research purposes!) 

Dr. Maria Nikolantonaki: consulting enologist. She has degrees in winemaking from Greece and Bordeaux (literally a doctor of wine) before post-doc work at UC Davis that got her wrapped up in this project. Currently a professor of wine at University of Dijon.

I’m not even covering their assistant winemaker, Max (Master's in Vit & Enology) or their owners, John & Gill Wagner who — when not being simply badass philanthropists (Google them), also have a UC Davis winemaking certificate (that’s how John met Mike). Soooo…I think I’ve established these folks know what they’re doing, right?!?!? 

One sip of this PROVES it. Absolutely DECADENT bouquet of raspberry tea, and violets, and peonies, with lavender and wild strawberry too…YES! There’s some serious spice, and cool blueberry cobbler things going on, with this powdery pastille candied note, and the cooling, hyper-freshness of spearmint and rhubarb…WOWZA! As spicy and coastally cool as Santa Rita Hills should be, with the power and darkness that has made SRH the HOTTEST name in the Pinot game over the last decade. 92 POINTS, people! Just $23!

This will FLY – no question. How much can you make your own before it’s gone forever?

I’m getting a full case just to be safe…while it lasts!!!

title: Bouchaine Vineyards Pinot Noir Estate Carneros 2022
body_html:
One of the tippy-top BEST wineries in Carneros is BACK – and their estate-grown, utterly ROCKING 93 POINT Pinot Noir is here for a grand ol’ time...but not a long one!!!

Give me a “B”! Give me a “U”! Give me a “Shane”! What’s that spell? (Not really…just roll with it.) BOUCHAINE!!! Who’s ready for glory? For unending joy, and guilt-free sipping on Carneros Pinot that seriously, vigorously, OVERDELIVERS!!! Yes, sir! Bouchaine is back…and how – with a straight-up jaw-dropping 63% OFF this 93 point, simply titillating ESTATE Pinot Noir…you better FLY!

We’re talking 100 acres of absolute prime, Napa Carneros vineyards, overlooking the San Francisco Bay, and all the fog, wind, and cooling influence that comes along with it. It’s one of the best sites for Pinot Noir in the entire Napa Valley – and Bouchaine is one of Napa’s longest champions of Carneros for good reason. This CLASSIC beauty – and I’m talking Audrey Hepburn, Roman Holiday levels of classic – soars in at a totally balanced 13.8 abv, and a freshness-preserving 10 months aging, with only 20% new French oak in the process. The result???

Hypnotic. Exotic. Elegance and power perfectly intertwined, with a lift that is matched by depth, supple structure, and a long, delicate finish. WOWZA! Black cherries, and sweet smoky herbs, with vanilla bean, milk chocolate, and a tangy summer strawberry compote. A kiss of cedar keeps this hanging around like my golden retriever begging for a walk – and every bit as heartbreaking! UNDER $20 makes this an EPIC steal for pedigreed Napa Pinot Noir – but get a move on…only so much to go ‘round! 

While it lasts!

title: Domaine Eden Cabernet Sauvignon Santa Cruz Mountains 2020
body_html:
Double 94 POINTS for one DIVINE Cali-Cab that we’re bringing to your doorstep for a full 64% OFF! A Santa Cruz Mountains ICON has landed!

DOMAINE EDEN is BACK, y’all! You begged, then we begged, and wouldn’t ya know it – quite unlike poor Oliver Twist – hot dang, did we EVER get some MORE!!! And THE PRICE? Ludicrous. UNDER $20 for this double 94 POINT, Wine Enthusiast “Cellar Selection” from one of California’s most historic wineries…YES!!! Mount Eden, my people! This completely magical Santa Cruz Mountains stalwart is beloved by wine drinkers the world over, and just when I thought I couldn’t love them any more, they added a sister. In 2007, they bought the old Cinnabar winery (another legend) just down the road and renamed it Domaine Eden. Same attention to world-class farming and winemaking, even more attractive pricing, and, on occasion…in certain vintages – every bit as good (OR BETTER!) than their sister property…

In 2020…we’re talking about one of those special instances. We tasted the two Cabs side-by-side, and Domaine Eden won…unanimously for sheer, upfront pleasure, and delightfully Cab-centric deliciousness. And this goes beyond our illustrious tasting panel of grizzled wine-industry veterans and burnout line cooks with creative writing degrees…Wine Enthusiast came to the exact…same…conclusion!!! 92 points for the $110 Mount Eden 2020 Cab, and then a 94 POINT review for the (normally) $50 DOMAINE EDEN 2020 Cab. Ummm, mic drop much?! That’s not even factoring our LUDICROUS under $20 price – a full 64% OFF the retail!!! 

So yeah…LOAD UP is what I’m saying – ‘cause you can be certain this ain’t sticking around for long! And WOWEEEEE…is this ever rocking! Plums and kirsch, ripe blackberries with smoldering sage, briny and savory black olives, with a smattering of bay leaf and cinnamon, then this truck-load of crushed rock minerality ushering in the finale…this is highly complex, old-school accents with a new-school core of plush, lip-smackingly ripe fruit, and plenty of polish. WOWZA – so good!!! The winery has long been a personal favorite for off-the-radar Cabernet Sauvignon production…and this simply UNBEATABLE value makes this the sort of no-brainer, multi-case pick-up that nobody…NOBODY… should miss!!! Any of y’all that scored a much, much tinier drop last spring know what a deal this is…and we backed up TWO truckloads of freshies just from the winery’s warehouse this time around. I mean, C’MON!!! A living legend amongst California wineries, a DOUBLE 94 POINT, cannot miss round of critical praise, and the BEST PRICE you will EVER see at our fantastic UNDER $20 mark.

Who could even THINK of missing out??? Let’s take home some UNICORNS today – while they last!!!

title: Le Mistral Red Blend Joseph's Arroyo Seco 2021
body_html:
Le Mistral blowing into the Last Bottle wind tunnel today!!! 93 POINTS, over 60% OFF, and ready to toss a Cali-coated gauntlet towards the good folks of Châteauneuf-du-Pape!

Let’s go!!! From the Petaluma Gap to Monterey, Châteauneuf-du-Pape and beyond – a bit of a windy disposition can really make a region special. And wind is what makes this wine a winner, folks!!! For decades, Le Mistral was a project headlined by Napa’s famed Joseph Phelps Vineyards – and just as INSIGNIA was their flagship Bordeaux blend, Le Mistral was their pinnacle of Cali-Rhône – an homage to Châteauneuf-du-Pape from a cool, windy vineyard in the Arroyo Seco corridor of Monterey. In the heart of the Rhône Valley, and especially in CdP – everyone knows the infamous Le Mistral – a furious and freezing wind that blows over the region every winter and spring (up to 80 mph!) and is a vital characteristic of the vineyard’s terroir. For this wine, Le Mistral is an actual plot in the Arroyo Seco, where old vines of Grenache and Syrah make some seriously CdP-reminiscent wines…just…like…THIS!!!

Wild and savage with floral notes of rose petals, and earthy camphor, roasted herbs, and warm saddle leather – still dusty from the trail. Yeehaw! Just crazy smooth, and intensely spiced, with a luxurious finish…this beauty is redolent with fennel fronds and juicy blackberries, then red currants, and roasted plums, freshly baked baguettes, and a kiss of sweet sassafras cola. I love it…a true homage to delightfully old-school CdP – at a Last Bottle swoon-worthy price!

Get excited, everybody! And get some Cali-Rhône that’ll rock…your…WORLD!

Just…GOOOOOOOOOOOO!!!

body_html:

Yaaaaaaaaaayyyy! (C’mon, Monty Python’s Quest for the Holy Grail is the most quotable, re-watchable film ever… no cap!) Anywho… back to the UNDER $13 ZINFANDEL! This is some straight-up Cali-magic from a handful of Dry Creek Valley’s TOP vineyards, infused with a healthy 10% of inky, wildly lush Petite Sirah. 

It simply *POPS* with pure and vibrant Zinfandel goodness, those spicy bramble berries, that old leather, and earthy cola sensations, all with TONS of juicy, ripe, and simply mouthwatering fruit character. title: Tilth Red Blend Old Vine Sonoma Valley 2023
body_html:
Over 66% OFF! Tilth’s newest 93 POINT red is an old vine, utterly spectacular red that boasts the talents of famed 100-point winemaker, Keith Emerson!  At UNDER $25 – this is your perfect winter sipper!

Joltin’, jumping, Jebidiah Simpson! This TILTH Old Vines is rocking like grandma’s favorite chair – and if you scored some back in September – you KNOW that granny’s chair just…don’t…QUIT! Anywho...TILTH continues to roll with a steady flow of both critical and peer-to-peer praise…not to mention y’all never-ending requests to re-order!!! The value, the quality, the undeniable DELICIOUSNESS of it all! KEITH EMERSON, you da’ man!

Vineyard 29’s longtime winemaker (and CEO and Presidente, too), who also can boast Robert Craig, Martin Ray, and Gundlach Bundschu in his hall-of-fame resume, Keith is known for polished, concentrated, age-worthy, and downright LIP-SMACKING gems – be they Bordeaux, Rhone, or any other style of Cali-perfection. This luscious, inky-dark, and simply cranium-melting red blend is a not-quite classic Cali-Rhône – with a core of Syrah, and old vine mixed blacks (Grenache, Carignane, Zinfandel, Mourvedre, Alicante Bouschet, and who knows what else), plus a little dash of extra BOLD, utterly NOT FOR THE SHY Petite Sirah from Sonoma’s Moon Mountain! The black cherries, and blueberry compote, the muddled blackberries, and white sage, and wild spearmint, with pulverized gravel, trodden violets, and end-of-days fire & brimstone…this is INTENSE! The luxurious oak profile frames all, and despite the grapes involved – don’t be thinking this is a pizza and pasta, soft and stylish medium-bodied red…

You’re looking at UNTOLD depths, and lunatic-levels of power, and persistence, and heady, luscious concentration – this ROCKS!!! The long, ample, and nearly perfect 2023 vintage gives this legs to run for YEARS, while the completely ripe fruit profile and softening edge character of the oak profile’s vanilla bean and mocha sweetness make this wondrously approachable right…freaking…NOW! 93 POINTS and OVER 66% OFF…what more could you hope for?!?!

Last chance to re-stock. This WILL NOT LAST LONG!

body_html:

Wooohoooo! A MAGICAL new Napa release from VERDON ESTATE! If you like them Richy Rich rich, with plump and purring bushels of black cherry, and plums, and black currants, with heady and luscious spice accents of mocha, and dark chocolate, all framed with a fine and grippy pencil lead structure, and uplifted by entrancing floral notes of violets and hibiscus – well, then!!! title: Château Gracia Saint-Émilion Grand Cru 2018
body_html:
Three Bordeaux sell outs, thank you!......Let's keep it going!!!

97 POINT Vin d’Garage STUNNER! Château Gracia has returned to help kick off 2026 properly! The 2018 is singing – especially at our JAW-DROPPING 54% off!!! Let’s GOOOO!!!

Château Gracia!!! Did you score the 2016? Then you already know. Missed out? Don’t make that mistake TWICE! One of the very first “GARAGISTE” wines of Bordeaux’s Right Bank that forever shifted the balance of power in the Bordeaux Class system…and despite the absolute CULT status of this wine – we scored some for a CRAY-CRAY price! To have ANY of this is a miracle – but at a full $96 OFF??? Très incroyable!!!

Remember Michel Gracia? A Saint-Émilion contractor and stone mason, who’s laid stone at properties like Angelus, Cheval Blanc, and Petrus…and now makes wines that rival (and some say surpass) them all! In 1994, after encouragement from Jean Luc Thunevin (ahem…another garagiste who launched all this craziness with Château Valandraud back in the early 90s), alongside his pal, Alain Vauthier of Ausone – Michel purchased just under 4 acres of prime vineyard next to Troplong Mondot. The rest, as they say, is history.

Savvy Bordeaux collectors and wine lovers are known to sneak these in on the regular with FIRST GROWTH Châteaus in blind tastings – and then revel in the absolute PANDEMONIUM that erupts when these come out on top. After the absolute BLITZ we had in 2016, we begged our source for another parcel – and BOOM! Wish granted. The pricing is simply breathtaking – UNDER $80 for this is like finding out you’re actually the only son and heir to an eccentric billionaire with royal blood, who’s set to become the next King of Monaco. CaaaCHING!!!

The 2018 is just breaking that Bordeaux barrier and stepping into its “let’s turn some heads” phase – and goodness gracious, Greta Garbo – this is now worthy of the silver screen! Silky and smooth aromas of kirsch, and crème de cassis, with brooding elements of roasted fennel, and fresh-poured asphalt, then baked plum tarts, and black cherry compote, with salty, savory roast beef, and flecks of iron and graphite, with this strong and heady finish of crushed violets, and dried rose, and a faint, sweet whisper of English toffee. Just like the 2016 – this is full-bodied, full-throttle Right Bank righteousness – and you won’t find it anywhere else at anywhere near this EPIC price!

97 POINTS and as cult-y collectible as EVER!!! While it lasts!

title: Skylark Red Blend Red Belly North Coast 2019
body_html:
It’s a bird! It’s a plane! It’s a RED BELLY?!?! 92 POINTS, under $13, and making its GLORIOUS return to the Last Bottle arena...SKYLARK!!!

What’s it been…two years? Three? However long has passed…Skylark, I have missed you! This has been a personal favorite, cellar-defending, over-delivering, simply KILLER Cali-Rhône for YEARS – made with clonal material sourced from Château de Beaucastel in Châteauneuf-du-Pape, for Pete’s sake! John Lancaster and Robert Perkins are two Bay Area sommeliers who catapulted the famed Boulevard restaurant in SF to superstardom, and when they got together to make a bit of wine from some VERY old vines and singularly great vineyards some years back…they flat-out ROCKED our world! For almost 25 years, this partnership has thrived, producing a portfolio of delectably fresh, stylistically pure, and unimaginably food-friendly iterations of many of California’s top vineyard sites. They never make a ton, and they always focus on sustainable and thoughtful family growers, and their prices are downright tasty! 

We’ve done what we do, and sprinkled some magical Last Bottle MSG on this deal to make it even TASTIER! I mean, UNDER $13?! Say WHAT?!?! Three vineyards, spread across the cool climates and high elevations of Mendocino, with each site providing a single varietal for the blend. 45% Syrah, 32% Carignan, and 23% Grenache, and just downright DELICIOUS!

The smoky, cool Syrah notes of blueberries and violets, complemented with meaty, roasted herb wildness, and juicy strawberry, and red raspberry that just pulses and vibes across your bewildered tongue, finishing with this cooling minty-ness and pulverized gravel minerality – and then hanging around with tingly peppercorns and spice notes for far, FAR longer than any TWELVE DOLLAR bottle of California sunshine has any right to do!!! Huzzah, good people! This is drinking absolutely RIDONKULOUS here and now – you’d never guess it was so ridiculously priced…and none of the dozens of people you sagely pour this for again and again over the next several months will ever guess either!

An all-time MEGA-STEAL that once again shows why Skylark’s RED BELLY belongs in YOUR belly! While it lasts!!!

title: Conn Creek Cabernet Sauvignon Special Selection Napa Valley 2019
body_html:
95 POINTS! This “Special Selection” Napa Cabernet Sauvignon is back, and as beastly as ever! OVER 60% OFF...let’s do this!

This “Special Selection” Cabernet (take that, Caymus!) is an unbridled, run-for-the-roses thoroughbred of elite Napa Cab – selected from some of the ORIGINAL and historic Napa sites that put Conn Creek on the map back in the 1970s Golden Age of Napa Valley. Saddle that with the expressive and heavily-acclaimed 2019 Napa vintage…and you’ve something special indeed! A WHOPPING $91 LESS than the winery price, to boot?!?!

Huzzah, good people! You might have noticed, but we’ve taken a long-held, slow-burning CRUSH on all things Conn Creek and let it EXPLODE! I’m talking full Fatal Attraction style crush…bordering on manic. We may need counseling. But group counseling for our ENTIRE buying team would be hella expensive, and at the end of the day…the wine is just TOO GOOD! Can you really blame us? It’s not like we went full Glenn Close and boiled a pet rabbit or anything – we simply pop bottles, and share them with y’all at every possible occasion. Totally harmless…especially when we can offer a 95 POINT 2019 Napa Cab for over 60% OFF! YES!!!

The nearly black, vibrantly deep purple wine oozes silkily into the glass, revealing beguiling aromatics of violets and rose petals, with blackberry syrup, and hints of potent kirsch. Chocolate cream pie with vanilla Chantilly cream blossoms on the mid-palate, enveloped in surrounding fruits like baked plums, more blackberries, and a dusting of chocolate truffles. Some woodsy notes of cedar and green tobacco lend further depth, and this crushed rock minerality keeps this pulsing into a crazy-long finish. Flashy, layered, and just undeniably SEXY – this is Napa Cab as it should be…we just so happen to have it at the BEST price anywhere!

Under $60 makes this impossible to ignore…get your fill, and add on more than you think you’ll need – you’re gonna be crushing these bottles in 2026 and for YEARS to come!!!

title: Emeritus Vineyards Pinot Noir Hallberg Ranch Russian River Valley 2021
body_html:
94 POINTS! Plus triple 93s, one heck of a track record, and one duly famed plot of Pinot Noir…all for UNDER $20!

Did you miss this in the post-BLACK FRIDAY madness? Welp, suit up, Maximus – ‘cause Fate smiles upon you today!!! I love, love, LOVE seeing the name Hallberg Ranch when I crack open a bottle of California Pinot Noir...and boy, howdy...does THIS ever deliver! Hallberg Ranch is one of my very favorite parcels in all of Russian River – an old apple and peach orchard from Prohibition until the year 2000 that was originally used for winemaking back in the late 1800s. Classic RRV power and deeply fruited intensity, with a long finish, and all the twists and turns of complexity along the way…it just swings and swings and almost never misses – like Shohei Ohtani at batting practice.

We’ve featured Emeritus before – and if they weren’t on your Pinot Noir radar before…you need to remedy that IMMEDIATELY! Emeritus was founded by Brice Cutrer Jones (the same year he sold his famed Sonoma-Cutrer winery) and has been quickly hailed as "one of the Russian River's most accomplished Pinot Noir producers" by Tom Hyland of Forbes -- and I'm here to say...you better believe it!!! This estate vineyard bottling is stellar – and if you have ever tasted any of the famed bottlings of this vineyard from the likes of Gary Farrell, Etude, Joseph Jewell, or Martin Ray – you're already loading up your cart! These 750s are sold out at the winery, but don't fret, you can still get 375mLs from them for $25. Or...OR!!! You can bust a move and get the full 750s for UNDER $20 from Last Bottle. Choose wisely!!!

This 2021 is sleek and exuberant with fresh strawberry, black cherry cola, ripe boysenberry, goji berry, rose petal, and decadent chocolate cake. A kiss of coffee bean torrefaction (I love that word) with a whisper of petrichor that lingers long after each sip makes this incredibly unique and stop-you-in-your-tracks delectable. Easily competes (and outclasses) some $100++ Russian River Pinots that have the big names and long waitlists (you know who they are). At our RIDONKULOUS price?! This is a no-brainer, folks.

title: Massimo Rivetti Froi Barbaresco 2021
Solar Ash Cabernet Sauvignon Knights Valley Sonoma County 2022
body_html:
93 POINT debut Cabernet Sauvignon, from a single vineyard a stone’s throw from the famed Peter Michael winery, in the warm, sunny, and volcanic haven that is Knights Valley...for WHAT price????

I mean, talk about kicking off the week with a BANG! Yes, I know it is Tuesday, but after a long weekend, I’m just settling back into this cruel existence of working for a living. I mean, I’ve opened three bottles of wine already, and it’s not even 9 am! Jeez, what misery! Ok, yes, I am kidding, and yes, I am blessed with a pretty sweet gig of popping bottles and telling stories…but still, getting back into the swing of things is tough! Luckily, I got a sweet proposition for all of the many, many Cabernet Sauvignon lovers out there in the Last Bottle Universe. So here it goes: 1st ever release, boasting a solid 93 POINT score, with a full 70% SLASHED off the winery retail, from a fully-pedigreed 2nd generation of some big-time Sonoma grapegrowers – and your buy-in is a paltry $22…TWENTY TWO DOLLARS!

If your heart didn’t just skip a beat, it is time to see a cardiologist, my friends…’cause this Solar Ash Cab is the real deal! This is a Taylor Swift, Fab Four, Led Zeppelin 1 worthy debut…absolutely JAMMING with rich and deep berry liqueur, and ripe black currants, with cherry compote, and sticky tar, uplifted with chocolate truffles, and roasted herbs, some fresh mission figs, and even a bit of mint and menthol for good measure. 100% Cabernet Sauvignon, all from The Bavarian Lion vineyard that sits across from Peter Michael, tucked just under the shadow of Mount Saint Helena, and not two miles from the Napa border. And…IT ROCKS!

Decanter called it “full-bodied and effortlessly quaffable”...and yep, I see no lies there. The bottle on my desk disappeared in about 3 minutes flat – thanks to some colleagues who are also wishing that long weekend lasted just a weeeeee bit longer. The rolling hills and warm, volcanic soils of Knights Valley (arguably the BEST spot in Sonoma for Cabernet Sauvignon) grant this a depth and unctuousness that is more reminiscent of Napa than your typical Sonoma Cab – but when it comes to the PRICE??? That’s a deal that’s rare even in Sonoma!

Over 70% OFF?! I’ll line up for this like 3-day Bottle Rock VIP tickets – I mean, who saw that line-up?! Anywho…this is a wine to score early and in great store…’cause who knows if we’ll ever get this chance again! Not as much as we’d like, and sure to FLY!

** I swear to everything holy, if they cut the lights and sound on Foo Fighters, right in the crux of “Everlong” again...I am done with Bottle Rock. For real, this time… **

title: Montagu Pinot Noir Charles Heintz Vineyard Sonoma Coast 2024
body_html:

Pinot-philes, lend me your ears! (Or eyes, rather.) Montagu is BACK! A timeless love of Pinot Noir – that runs deeper than any six-day infatuation for some haughty Capulet! (And fully skips the tragically depressing ending…)

Plus…95 POINTS! And a price that could quench even Mercutio’s tempestuous rage – clocking in at UNDER $30!?! Huzzah! Ok, I’m about Barded out, I swear. Just saw Hamnet, so I’ve been thinking in iambic pentameter and dreaming about Midsummer-esque bacchanalian adventures nonstop! But this wine?! It’s pure poetry, to be certain…starting with the hallowed soil of the famed Charles Heintz vineyard in Sonoma. A California Grand Cru if ever there was one, set on the Occidental Ridge, and producing prized fruit clamored and claimed by notable wineries like DUMOL, WILLIAMS-SELYEM, LITTORAI, CERITAS, and more! Their single vineyard offerings tend to fetch THREE TIMES the price we have today – and our over 65% OFF savings make this impossible to ignore!

After some globe-hopping in Chile, South Africa, Bordeaux, Veneto, and more...Weston settled in the Napa Valley in 2012, cutting some razor-sharp teeth working under the legendary Russell Bevan (with too many 95-100 point scores to count), and Jason Moore of Modus Operandi (who nabbed some pretty awesome 97+ and 98 point scores himself recently). He’s paid his dues, shook all the right hands, and now…THIS!

If you tasted the 2021 we offered up a couple years back – you’re already filling up your cart…this is one absolutely STELLAR deal for world-class Pinot Noir! Full, plush, and bursting with perfumed fruits and florals – from black cherries, to crushed raspberries, elegant rose, and intoxicating lilacs, and with this silky, and gorgeously layered sensation of milk chocolate, and subtle baking spice that just lingers for DAYS! AMAZING to get a little bit of this, please don’t miss – this is a Pinot deal that doesn’t come along every day! While it lasts, good people!!

** Happy Martin Luther King Jr. Day! Stand up for what’s right, stand up for each other, and let’s create a future where our dreams can come true! **

title: Alberto Voerzio La Serra Barolo 2021
body_html:
Sell out alert!! More Barolo in the house......

96 POINTS for a singularly ROCKING new Barolo, by the supremely talented Alberto Voerzio, hailing from the ICONIC La Serra Cru atop of La Morra...YES!

Expressive. Seductive. Captivating. Commanding. And that’s just a sliver of what the critics have to say! If you’ve been buried under a football field of rubble for the past several months, let’s just say we’ve been LOVING these Voerzio family wines for some time, but a chance encounter about a year and a half back has changed the PIEMONTE game for all you Nebbiolo lovers FOREVER here on Last Bottle. We’ve been snatching and grabbing, hoarding and sharing all things Voerzio like they are going out of style – when in truth, they are on the rise like never before!!!

CRU BAROLO… from a site revered for its aromatic elegance, and chalky, age-worthy, simply CLASSIC Barolo texture…that we just so happen to have at an INSANE deal of UNDER $60!!! La Serra is the very highest of the high of La Morra’s hillsides, and the wines just might be the metaphorical peak of the mighty Cru as well. I popped this little beauty at 9 am in the office on Friday, and the explosion of roses, lilacs, and peonies jolted me to life like a quad espresso injected into my eyeballs! I came back an hour later, and it was even better, with luxurious sandalwood, and red cherries, milk chocolate, orange zest, and even some spiced raspberries coming to life. The first sip was EPIC, with an intensity of fruit and florals that all perfectly settled into a sweet, dusty earthiness, where crushed gravel and pulverized chalk give this a foundation of immense complexity. The finish is regal, and long-lived, and I had a sneaking suspicion…it wasn’t done yet!

So I corked the bottle and snuck the rest home…and a full day later…BINGO! It’s everything it was on day one…just…MORE. The tension had relaxed gracefully, like the muscles in my back after a beer at the 19th hole. Just…aaaaahhhh! I’ve no doubt this could last twenty or thirty years, easily…and at this price, you’d be sage to snag a few extra for cellaring, but if you just can’t wait (life is short, after all) you’ll still be rewarded with the best Barolo I’ve tasted so far this year. Sure, that’s only out of about 30 Barolos in total – but trust me…22 of those we’re bangin’ and this one was still the unquestioned winner!

UNDER $60 is a STEAL on this beautiful Barolo – especially when almost anything with the name Voerzio on it typically sets you back $150 or more. Grab more than you think you’ll need, and revel in your wisdom for years and decades to come!

Go get ‘em!

title: Adversity Cellars Cabernet Sauvignon Catastrophe Napa Valley 2022
body_html:
Russell Bevan? Check. 96 POINTS? Check. Over 66% OFF? Checkmate, friends!

Who’s ready for a CATASTROPHE?!?! Wait…that sounds kind of ominous. Strike that. OK, let’s try again…the only CATASTROPHE would be missing out on this MONSTER of a Napa Cab! Meh…it’ll do. But wordsmithing aside, let’s focus on what REALLY matters for a moment! How about the ROCKSTAR winemaker that is RUSSELL BEVAN?! How about the BEST PRICE ANYWHERE! (And it ain’t close…not enough for horseshoes, not enough for hand grenades, not by a country mile…) YES!!!

Let’s dig in, real quick – the winery’s suggested retail is $225, the NEXT best price on the planet is $150, and we’re absolutely MASSACRING that price by cutting it in half…HALF! For a 96 POINT, new Napa Cabernet from the visionary team of Heidi and Russell Bevan, this is lunacy! If you like BIG, polished, layered, utterly hedonistic Napa Cabs…Russell is your winemaking spirit guide. This beauty flashes and pops, and gets gloriously up close and in your face like paparazzi – and with every sip, you feel more and more like the celebrity you are! The fruit hails from three stunning Napa vineyards – the legendary Haynes vineyard in St. Helena, the prized Sugarloaf Mountain on the southern end of the Vaca range, and the Phelan Ranch. Now, the first two are certified legends, but if you haven’t heard of Phelan Ranch…you will. Just planted in 2019, this sprawling hilltop site atop Mount Gregg overlooks Lake Hennessey and completes a lovely little powerhouse triangle of Howell Mountain and Pritchard Hill, sharing similar volcanic soils and impressive elevations. The result is all-in-all INSANE – with plush and lush bramble berries and cherries, loads of spice, tobacco, and warming, soul-filling notes like cardamom, black pepper, Cuban cigar, and vanilla, then rounded out with a rigid spine of graphite and crushed gravel, flecked with cedar shavings and exotic black truffles. A Cult-Cab in the making, no question – but with a total production of only about 150 cases, don’t expect to find this just hanging around your local grocer.

It’s RUSSELL BEVAN, for Pete’s sake! And with 96 POINTS, and a full $150 in savings on EVERY…SINGLE…BOTTLE – you better bet your bottom dollar, this is gonna FLY!

While it lasts!

title: McKahn Family Cellars Grenache Morning Glass Arroyo Seco 2022
body_html:
Morning Glass is BACK, y’all! A longtime favorite Cali-Rhône here in the Last Bottle Universe – backed by a solid 91 POINT review from Wine Enthusiast, and absolutely PORCH-POUNDER worthy at our insane price of UNDER $15! Those of you who’ve scored this before already know...this is one to back up the truck on!!!

We did our voodoo magic to keep 2026 rolling and scored this beauty at the BEST price anywhere, folks, and I tell you…it ain’t even close! Nearly 70% OFF makes this unmissable, and with the McKahns’ typical Cali-Rhône blend moving to 100% Grenache for 2022…this is an utterly EPIC wine that I’ll be drinking with feckless and reckless abandon – as though I had a back-up liver in a cryo-freeze case that says “break in case of overindulgence”. HUZZAH!

If you’ve missed out on this homage to the pristine conditions of an untroubled lake at first light – y’all need some Morning Glass in your life, STAT! My boy, Chuck McKahn, is a phenom...I call him the anti-stormtrooper – ‘cause he simply can’t MISS! Chose winemaking at 15, graduated from Cal Poly’s famed Enology program while simultaneously working harvests before moving up to Napa and catching a prime gig as a cellar hand under the LEGENDARY Phillip Titus at the historic CHAPPELLET Vineyard on Pritchard Hill. In a scant 5 years, he had worked his way up to Assistant Winemaker. Phillip's recommendation saw Chuck taking over as head winemaker at nearby William Harrison soon after that, and Chuck was, as they say, on his way. He more recently ascended to the head-honcho position at TITUS Vineyards (plus an Associate Winemaker title at Acumen) – and if you love things precise, baby-bottom SMOOTH, and densely packed with flavor – Chuck is your man!

For UNDER $15?! This CRUSHES plenty of Cali-Grenache at 4 or 5 times that price! Simply STUNNING aromatics of lilacs, and dusty rose petals, with flecks of white pepper, and summer strawberries, and this minty/menthol coolness holding it all together…WOWZA! It explodes on the tongue with red raspberries, and smoky sage, then some cherry and other bramble berry notes, alongside this exotic vanilla, cardamom, and star anise blend that keeps this driving like Rory McIlroy – long, precise, and flat-out impressive! The banana-belt valley floor region of the Arroyo Seco AVA in Monterey gives this a perfectly balanced weight and ripeness, with fantastically integrated acidity that keeps this bright, cool, and perfect for your table! Do yourself a solid and grab a BOATFUL…then thank me once you start popping one or two bottles every week! At this price, you simply CANNOT get too much.

While it lasts!

title: Une Femme Sparkling The Betty California NV
body_html:
Bubbles. 91 POINTS. Under $13. I mean...do I even need to say anything further?! Let’s do this!!!

We almost never – NEVER – score primo Cali-bubbly for south of $20 – at least any that we’d actually drink!!! But this woman-owned, woman-made wonder of California gold is a certified BANGER…especially at our wicked price!

First off…any product… literally ANY project, that tips the cap to the late, great Betty White – well, sign…me…UP!!! 80% Pinot Noir and 20% Chardonnay, sourced from Certified Sustainable family-owned vineyards in Napa, Sonoma, and Lodi – all to express the glorious California sunshine in a fizzy, hyper-fresh, and absolutely CRUSHABLE bottle o’bubbly. Floral flourishes of jasmine and honeysuckle, then Korean pear, and mandarin blossom with yuzu…DANG, Betty! Each greedy sip simply ROCKS with purely focused flavors of white nectarines, golden apples, and unabashed liquid sunshine. The bubbles are frenetic and zippy, with a freshness and a softness that leads to these bottles disappearing lightning fast. But hey, at ONLY TWELVE DOLLARS a bottle – drink two, or three!

Founder Jen Pelka knows a thing or two about Champagne and the world of sparkling wines. As CEO and founder of The Riddler – an all-women funded NYC and San Francisco based Champagne Bar – she’s seen, tasted, and explored tens of thousands of bottles of bubbly over the years…and Une Femme started as the by-the-glass pour here. But when the Champagne Bars shuttered (thanks, Covid!). She refused to be conquered – and Une Femme took on a life all its own. This beauty is all about a time, and a place – with the emphasis being here…and now. Lip-smacking, delectably tasty bubbly that should be shared widely and often with others – what’s not to love?!?!

So, I say again…Bubbles. 91 POINTS. Under $13. Go get your case, people!!!

title: Spring Mountain Vineyard Red Blend Elivette Spring Mountain District Napa Valley 2013
body_html:
96 POINT phenom! The powerfully-built, and all-in-all amazing 2013 vintage and Last Bottle’s simply RIDONKULOUS price…let’s go!!!! 

2013!!! What could I possibly say about this vintage that you haven’t heard for years?! The power, the depth, the intensity that made this one of the biggest, and BADDEST vintages in Napa history – with the sheer life force to survive and THRIVE for decades!!! The polish and complexity of the Elivette 2013 is just staggering – and while we basically received an entire TRUCKLOAD of this beauty…don’t think for a second that this will last for long! (On the site, I mean – in your wine fridge or tucked safely under your bed – this just might outlive us all!)

Anyways, I’m going to be straight with y’all (as ALWAYS!) When I tasted this on release, at the winery back in early 2016…I didn’t love it. It was just TOO MUCH. Tighter than a drum, with tannins that felt like a lead pipe to your molars. But I taste EVERY wine I get to share with y’all – and (thankfully) sometimes more than once – so I gave this a go yesterday afternoon…and WOW! It’s in its HAPPY PLACE, no question, and even Shooter McGavin and a hired heckler couldn’t rattle this absolutely profound and POPPING Cabernet blend. 57% Cabernet Sauvignon leading the pack, then Cabernet Franc clocking in at 17%, and rounded out with 10% Merlot, 8% Petit Verdot, and 8% Malbec…and my word, give it the coveted Gold Jacket already! The plums and blackberry coulis! The myriad of herbs, from sage to thyme, with lavender and lilacs, and this bold and brooding current of graphite, and toasted black peppercorns, and a silky, dark chocolate and espresso finish! It still has YEARS of prime, character-building development ahead of it, so don’t fear the decanter on this one…but wow, oh wow – how far it has come along already! 

HUZZAH, good people! Even at the winery’s $220 MSRP, this is a relative STEAL for high-end Napa Red from the near-perfect 2013 vintage. But at our UNDER $40 price?!?!

Stop IT! Grab yours while it lasts!

title: Cabernet Sauvignon Reserve Napa Valley
body_html: STOP. WHAT YOU’RE DOING. Because this is THAT Cabernet—the one our customers hoard, text their friends about, and then get irrationally upset when it sells out (which it always does). Big, bold, unapologetic Cali Cab vibes here: cassis, black cherry, a little mocha swagger, and tannins that flex without being a jerk about it. It’s the kind of wine that makes steak better, Tuesdays feel like Saturdays, and your wine rack look smarter than you actually are. Normally this bottle struts around at a much higher price point, but we’ve pulled some warehouse wizardry and — POOF — it’s here, it’s stupidly underpriced, and it will absolutely vanish. Blink and you’ll miss it. Don’t be the person who reads this twice and still doesn’t add it to cart.

title: Etude Chardonnay Grace Benoist Ranch Carneros 2022
body_html:
Sell out number 2……let’s keep the Chardonnay rolling!!

Etude’s single vineyard Carneros Chardonnay is BACK! You begged...we DELIVERED! Still UNDER $20!

Hit you right in the feels, doesn’t it, Chardonnay lovers? Kinda makes me want to quote Maya Angelou even…

“On Saturday afternoons when all the things are done in the house and there’s no real work to be done, I play Bach and Chopin and turn it up real loudly and get a good bottle of chardonnay and sit out on my deck and look out at the garden.” 

Right? RIGHT?! The sun is back to shining, winter seems to be in full retreat, and LIFE is ready to resume, good people! In the garden, at the lake, sitting on the deck — just grab this and GO! ETUDE, y’all! 94 POINTS, best price on this planet, and an unquestioned California classic that you will be straight-up LOVING all spring and summer to follow!! Not as much as last time — so load, or re-up, just don’t miss out! 

Founded in 1982 by the legendary Tony Soter (he has also made wines for the likes of Dalla Valle, Spottswoode, Shafer, Araujo, Chappellet, Viader, and so many more rock stars), Etude is a classic for rock-solid, delicious, and, when you are hip to Last Bottle, occasionally face-meltingly priced vinos — like today. I mean, UNDER $20 and FREE SHIPPING on 4 or more bottles! Just bonkers. Made in a non-heavy/fresh and zippy style (as opposed to really rich and oaky) that allows for unabashed, joyful consumption, there’s hella-clean and pure apples and crunchy-crisp pears, lemon meringue and candied citrus peels, with lip-smacking acidity and bright, laser-focused flavors. No ML means this goes full fruit and floral, and even mineral, without the butteriness. Rich, yet restrained…and just perfect for the sunny days of spring. It flat-out DELIVERS, y’all.

This is one to back up the truck on, good people. As before, this will go fast…while it lasts!

title: Weingut Bernhard Ott Gr?ner Veltliner Fass 4 Wagram 2015
body_html:
2015, people! One of the tippy-top BEST vintages for Austrian whites in the last 30+ years, and packaged up here for the ultimate is CRUSHABLE, delectable, and wondrous JOY!!! The legendary FASS 4 barrel does its magic once again, and great googly moogly, this 91 POINT beauty has really come around with time and patience! Under $20?

The gorgeously ripe honeydew melons, wrapped in salty-savory slivers of Parma ham, with this exotic star anise, and brimstone-ian, end-of-days primordial depths…all softened up with a juicy, warm core of roasted pears and toasted hazelnuts. Cool stuff…while it lasts at least!!! 

** All orders will be combined and shipped from our West Coast warehouse, so give it a little time, East Coasters! **

title: Due Fontane Cabernet Sauvignon Alexander Valley 2022
body_html:
Alexander Valley continues to climb the ladder as one of California’s best spots to grow KILLER Cabernet Sauvignon with this UNDER $30 wonder!!

Mitchell Masotti in da house! What? You don’t know him? You WILL. He’s already had his hands in nearly a dozen 100-point wines, working alongside Russell Bevan first, and now Jesse Katz. He’s also gone global – working for New Zealand’s cultiest of cult producers, Craggy Range, as well as another 100 point icon of global winemaking…Paul Hobbs at his premier winery, Viña Cobos. Toss is some time spent at other greats like Howell Mountain’s O’Shaughnessy Estate, plus a couple Pinot/Chard luminaries in Dutton-Goldfield and Freeman out in Sonoma…and…well, DANG! Pretty much the PERFECT resume, ain’t it?

So…I suppose it shouldn’t have been TOO much of a surprise that this wine nearly separated my brain from my body like some demented Tarantino kung-fu/samurai mash-up. But WOWZA! This 95 POINT beast is that plush, juicy juice, y’all…the sort of inky, silky, decadent, and hedonistic marvel that 110% bears the undeniable imprint of both the Bevan and Katz signature styles! Black fruits, blue fruits, luscious mocha and truffle dust, with savory, roasted herbs, and a wee morsel of spiciness that showcases this volcanic rock/graphite minerality. This baby is pumping like 70’s-era Schwarzenegger, and finishing stronger on my scorecards than Nadia Com?neci in ‘76. 95 POINTS ain’t too shabby for this new, completely off-the-radar winery (just TRY to find this anywhere else – not even a website yet!)

To have this for UNDER $30…it just addles the brain. If you’re looking to score that next big, “before they were famous” star…look no further!!!

While it lasts!

title: Giusti Umberto I Montello Rosso Superiore 2022
body_html:
96 POINTS! Incredibly rare, unimaginably delicious CABERNET & MERLOT blend from SASSACAIA’s revered oenologist, Graziana Grassini!!!

YES!!!! Giusti’s UMBERTO I is BACK, y’all — and when the critics croon on that “this bold, plush, juicy and inviting flagship red is the freshest and most electric to date” you better listen! We’ve shared Joe Giusti’s flagship Veneto red before — and not only is it the best ever…this stands apart as the BIGGEST…BADDEST…most utterly delectable red from Veneto I have tasted…EVER!!! Just a gobsmackingly EPIC 96 POINT Venetian beauty that will siren-song you right into the nearest canal if you’re not careful. The roses and violets! And smoky meats, with sanguine intensity, and blackberries, and brandied cherries, with roasted plums, and savory herbs and spices! This bold and layered STUNNER is all poise and balance, and this tightly wound complexity that is going to stretch out like a morning fern over time. $170 at the winery (Sassacaia’s winemaker don’t come cheap!) but today — we’ve got it at a mind-melting 67+% OFF!!! Yee-haw!!!

I had my mathematically inclined colleague do the math…we’re talking about $115 in savings, good people! Joe Giusti is one of the most influential vineyard barons in all of Veneto — bubbles, whites, reds...you name it, we’re in! He’s one of those LARGER THAN LIFE kinda guys — and we LOVE visiting his incredible estate whenever we’re in Italy! His estate-grown Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot blend refuses to stand in his larger than average shadow, and with the help of Sassacaia’s LEGENDARY Graziana Grassini — this powerful wine illuminates the void quite impressively all on its own! If you LOVE unapologetically BOLD Napa Cabs — you simply have to try THIS!

PACKED, stacked, and fully racked up with flavor — it just goes on FOREVER! 96 POINTS, over 67% off, under $60…hmmmm, what’s left? Oh yeah…WHILE IT LASTS!

title: Swiftsure Sauvignon Blanc Marlborough 2023
body_html:
Let the KIWIS rain from the sky! 93 point New Zealand Sauvignon Blanc at a terrifically tempting price!!!

As everyone knows, you have to drink Sauvignon Blanc – especially this time of year. Like icy, cool water, Beastie Boys tunes turned way up, birria tacos, and NHL Playoffs – it is essential for the very prospect of human life to continue. So why pay $25 for a 93 POINT New Zealand bottle of crisp and zesty goodness? When you can get it NOW for less than HALF THAT! I mean… TEN BUCKS for this?!?! Yet one more reason to do all your important shopping here at Last Bottle – hey, maybe we’ll start selling $1 tacos too!

Master of Wine Bob Campbell (he’s like the Robert Parker, Jr. of the Aussie/Kiwi wine scene) called this “A bright, fresh wine in a classically Marlborough style.” And who am I to disagree? Those perfectly tingling notes of oyster shells, and fresh-cut grass, with the zippy and electric POP of lemon and lime, and then a lovely little medley of passion fruit, guava, and papaya to give it that exotic and hypnotic flair that has me dreaming of white sand beaches, sunny days, and an endless horizon of crystal blue water!!! YESS!!

It’s juicy, lip-smacking, citrusy-zippy, and hits all the tropical staycation buttons – and especially the whoppingly awesome PRICE button!! Right?? How do we do it?

So – make haste to secure your favorite bi-valves, or maybe some fish tacos, and crack this open with no delay! We promise life will instantly be improved. A sixty-dollar SIX-PACK delivered to your door of 93 POINT liquid lightning – C’MON!!! BUT!! Ya gotta go quick...we don’t have much of this to go around…while it lasts…!

title: Upsell Product 2
TEST 5: Hoopes Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon Oakville Napa Valley 2019
body_html:
CAB ALERT! 

Not just any cab...OAKVILLE CABERNET! OVER 60% OFF!!! What a kick-off to the week!

Oakville single vineyard cabernets typically go for $200, $300, $400+...but...NOT TODAY! 94 POINTS! Let’s go!

A whopping $76 OFF and sure to FLY! 100% cabernet sauvignon from the oldest pre-phylloxera vineyard in Oakville. This juicy spot is only one of two vineyards in all of Oakville where the vines grow on this rare vein of volcanic soil. Want to guess the other one…? Ever heard of a little spot called TO KALON? (We sure have!) Superstar Aaron Pott came on board with Hoopes shortly after this harvest was barrelled down – and his signature plush, round, and expressive style is unmistakable in the final blend...easily the best Hoopes we’ve tasted yet! Simply a can’t-miss recipe for stellar, inky, and unctuous vinous ambrosia!

Seriously, I can’t get over this deal! Alpha Omega’s Tench Vineyard cabernet took home an IDENTICAL 94-point score for their 2019…which sells for $250+!!! But...THIS? At UNDER $50?!?! Load up the truck, good people! I’m vibing this morning – deep, greedy whiffs of that classically elegant Oakville aroma – simply chock-a-block with freshly picked blackberries and blueberries, plus spicy oak notes of cinnamon, clove, and black pepper, and faint earthy accents of forest duff, and porcini mushrooms. I LOVE porcini mushrooms!!! Meaty, savory, and impressively layered liquid goodness with metric TONS going on. Powerfully structured, but smoother and far more approachable than their 2018. Drink now, or two decades from now...you’re gonna be BLOWN AWAY! 94 POINTS! We mentioned the whole $76 OFF too, right? Single vineyard OAKVILLE CABERNET SAUVIGNON for UNDER $50? What madness is this?!?! While it lasts!

title: Domaine Besson Chablis Montmains Premier Cru 2022
body_html:
Who’s ready for CHABLIS?!?! Montmains, PREMIER CRU Chablis at that! And all crafted by long-time Last Bottle favorite Camille Besson! Let’s go!!!

YIPPEEEE!!! We’ve been singing the praises of Camille Besson – a master of Chablis’ top crus and Last Bottle uber favorite – for YEARS, and she’s enshrined in the Last Bottle Hall of Fame without question. Her Chablis flat-out delivers, and her Premier Cru site of Montmains is a personal favorite of mine. The 4th generation winemaker continues to craft the pound-for-pound, dollar-for-dollar, tippy-top BEST Chablis out there…and we’ve scored some for JUST $30!!!

2022 has become known as the SUNSHINE vintage for Chablis, due to the dry, warm conditions that allowed for excellent fruit set, and good yields – that nevertheless maintained Chablis’ prized acidity and intense minerality. The result here is just JAW-DROPPING good…a perfect medley of ripe orchard fruits, exotic tropicality, and that signature oyster-shell and lemony, grassy freshness that is so unmistakably CHABLIS! Premier Cru Chablis from Montmains regularly fetches $60, $70, $100+ sort of prices – but Camille gives us a special price for being her biggest fans ANYWHERE – so we can share the savings and offer this out for a simply UNREAL $30 a pop! That’s just EPIC, y’all.

The name “Montmains” refers to a medium-sized mountain that is lower than two surrounding peaks. Generally characterized by the presence of the precious Kimmeridgian marls under the clays – this means complex, intense aromatics, cooling minerality, stony freshness and salinity…mmmmm! How’s that sound to all you White Burg lovers out there?!?! Get four, get six, get two dozen…just get MOVING! We can never get enough, and pedigreed Chablis like this is sure to FLY!!

...while it lasts!

title: Castello Romitorio 'Romitorio' Toscana Rosso 2021
body_html:
93 POINT Tuscan! The BEST price ANYWHERE! Castello Romitorio in da’ HOUSE!!!

YES!!! What a wine! What a Wednesday! The absolutely EN FUEGO Castello Romitorio winery has been blowing up on a global scale these last few years. Their simply cult-like, wake-up-in-a-bathtub-full-of-ice-missing-a-kidney just to get one Brunellos have been snagging TONS of praise from all the top critics – 97, 98, 98 again, 97+ – and on and on…just fiercely hip and having their “It Girl” moment. We tasted this at VinItaly back in April – and honestly…we considered canceling the appointment. We KNEW beforehand the wines were epic, with crazy high scores, and a cult-like following. But the production size is small – what are the chances they even had enough? At these sort of prices, we insist on getting the all-time great deals our loyal Last Bottlers demand. It seemed a herculean task…and well, this was day four of “networking” over negronis and Champagne until 3:30 in the morning out in Verona’s Piazza delle Erbe.

But we really, REALLY wanted to try the wines! So we soldiered onward…and my word, are you gonna be glad we did!!! Somehow – I was barely conscious at this point – our crack team of buyers SCORED an all-time deal on this 93 POINT Romitorio Toscana Red. It was one of our favorites from all THIRTEEN wines we tasted from this great estate – with a quality for value that simply couldn’t be beat! A price like this for a wine of this pedigree is just ga-ga…plus, it’s got a friggin’ BULL on the label! (Everybody loves animal labels, right?) I digress…the wine!

Stunningly pretty, with loads of wild berries and flowers – from blueberries to violets and lilacs, with accents of black plums, red currants, and black cherries – and followed by a structured and lusciously rich mouthfeel that evokes molten milk chocolate and cafe mocha. Whispers of wild fennel, and orange zest complete the sensations – a velvety smooth, and wild ride that simply SINGS with freshness and depth! Huzzah, indeed! This will smackdown wines three times the price – a stunning marriage of power and elegance not often found at these sort of daily drinker price points. Get a case, get two, just GET MOVING! Not enough, and you have to know – the office is loading up on this for their personal stash! While it lasts!

** ROMITORIO!!! Fun Fact: Head honcho Filippo Chia’s dad traded 3 paintings for these 200 hectares of land and a castle ruin that would become Castello Romitorio (Hermitage Castle). Sandro Chia (who also designs all their stunning labels) is a principal member of the Italian Neo-Expressionist movement – and his art is nearly as stunning as the wine inside! **

title: Patz & Hall Pinot Noir Burnside Vineyard Russian River Valley 2018
body_html:
And…POW! Coming at you like a BOMB CYCLONE from the Pacific…Patz & Hall! It’s pouring down sheets of rain, streets are flooded, my skylight sprung a leak, but DA HECK with all that! Nothing but sunshine and rainbows today, good people, ‘cause…we scored THIS! Russian River Pinot in da HOUSE! 93 POINTS! From the one and only Burnside vineyard, a gem in the iconic portfolio of one of Sonoma’s greatest growers – the Martinelli family! YES!!!

All for UNDER $30?! Man, we NEVER get enough Patz & Hall to do a full offer! And this 2018, single vineyard pinot noir – crafted from two blocks, and two clones, in the coolest corners of Russian River is a straight-up vibe for me on this rainy, dreary day. Wisps of smokey cedar, and cinnamon red hots, with this luscious strawberry jam, and heady camphor, finishing with this cooling mint element. And that’s just the aromas! One sip to seal the deal…and BOOM! Black cherries, rosemary, Assam tea, pomegranate molasses, even more strawberries, and exotic spices...HUZZAH! This is big, brawny, and beautiful stuff – a classic RRV pinot noir through and through.  

The folks at Patz & Hall are legends in the Cali-pinot game – nearly 40 years of top-notch Sonoma pinot magic (and some chardonnay too…which you might see here on Last Bottle in the future...**wink, wink). We got every last bottle they’d sell us, and I’m here to say…you’ll be kicking yourself in the keister if you miss this EPIC deal! In their 93 POINT review, Wine Enthusiast has this release with a full $85 retail…so to nab some at a whopping 68+% OFF?! Just larceny!

While it lasts!

** Ok, dad joke time…if you’re Russian going into the bathroom, and Russian when you leave the bathroom…what are you while you’re IN the bathroom? EUROPEAN, of course! Here all week, folks, tip your server. **

title: Bibi Graetz Testamatta Toscana 2020
body_html:
The “Crazy Head” of the Tuscan wine scene, Bibi Graetz, is back at it – with his newest release of TESTAMATTA! Sure, it’s got the 97 POINT pedigree, and yes, we scored Bib’s flagship at an exceptionally drool-worthy price, but here’s the kicker…you can snag some at 50% OFF!!! 

Let’s GOOOOOO!!!! Y’all that snagged the last two vintages we featured already know – this is one of the TOP cult wines in all of Italy (with the prices climbing every year it seems – ELSEWHERE, at least!) Graetz exploded onto the Tuscan wine scene back in 2000, buying up old vine plots at high elevations in some of the tippy-top best spots throughout Tuscany. Testamatta is his self-portrait, naming the wine “crazy head” after himself, naturally. Hailing from a family of artists, his rise to the very top of Tuscany’s wine scene over the past 20 years has been unconventional but unquestionably meteoric. Slews of 97+ point scores prove it – his break-all-the-rules and howl-at-the-moon style has paid off in a momentous, Tuscan landscape-altering way! The 2020 is his best yet – alluring scents of roses, vanilla, and plenty of graphite kicks things off – then roasted wild herbs and savory smoked meats, sour cherries, and ripe plums, with a tangy twist of blood orange peel, lemon thyme, and a salty, sanguine finish of delicate tamari shoyu. This is brighter, and leaner than this cuvee in years past – and I’d say all the better for it! Lush – but incredibly taut and focused, with a lifted precision that bodes well for increased complexity and vigor in the years to come. The flagship just got taller, folks – yet dances in the winds as gloriously as ever! 

100% sangiovese, hailing from 6 of his best vineyard plots, with vines up to 50 years old, fermented and aged mostly in used barrels...this is a purer, elegant, and more thoughtful style – with less flash, but greater depth and longevity than previous vintages. The new parcel near the winery just outside of Florence seems to have a hot, new stamp on this wine – and I am HERE for it! There’s no two ways to say it…this wine ROCKS!  At 50% OFF...you’d best be scooting a few bottles into your cart! Not much of this to go around, and with the critical praise already latched on to this baby…get yours while it lasts!!! 

** Bit ticked off at the Florence Post Office...I sent 4 precious bottles of my house-made hot sauce to Bibi’s cousin, Emmanuelle (the BEST guy to know in Italy), and they mysteriously disappeared…arrived to Florence, went “out for delivery”, then “redirected”, then POOF! Gone. Grrr. **

title: Bennett Lane Maximus Red Feasting Wine Napa Valley 2019
body_html:
Are you not entertained?! Is this not why you are here?! 94 POINTS. Over 63% OFF…Maximus…Maximus…

Oh, baby…I guess it’s past time I watched Gladiator again! Preferably with a bottle of this, and a simply hedonistic array of olives, charcuterie, cheeses, maybe a chariot race on the TV – good times. Ok…let’s get after it…fabulous people, you awesome humans – we HEARD you!! And we HOOKED you up today, oh yes! Bennett Lane has been on our “most re-requested” list since the first time we ever offered it some years back – so any of you who know this wine probably aren't reading anyway, as you’ve already got your 6-pack!

For the rest of you – well, just HURRY is all we can advise, here sorry. Beautifully drinking, 94 POINT cab-dominant deliciousness, rounded out with 9% merlot, 6% syrah, 5% malbec, and 2% petit verdot at...well, let’s find the right phrase...reality warping prices, like, you just went through a black hole to a dimension where Cabernet at this level costs about 63+% less than you ever thought possible. Oh, and also you have 2 heads and telekinetic powers. Who knows? It’s possible.

This might come from another dimension as well – huge, concentrated, and more plush than an emperor’s lion-skin cape – pulsating with lavish blueberries and black cherries, plums, and blackberries, all complemented extravagantly with smoky cedar, and finely coated with molten dark chocolate. Roasted herbs, and silky tannins, with a hint of rocky minerality – there’s sooo much to discover here, at a simply RIDONKULOUS price! I said 94 POINTS, right? For the nigh-on unbelievable price of just $20?!?! What are you waiting for??? Drink this tonic!!!

** So…Gladiator Two, huh? Will it be a masterfully handled continuation long overdue…or a money-grabbing soulless effort that will only serve to enrage fans of the original? And can it really be worth the price of admission without Djimon Hounsou’s Juba making a return? Time will tell… **

title: Peju Proprietary Red Wine Winemaker's Select Napa Valley 2018
body_html:
Huzzah!!! Peju is BACK! Just $20 for this 94 pt. BEAST of a Napa red blend – your new fall CRUSH is here!

One sumptuous, NOT-FOR-THE-SHY monster of a Napa Valley red blend for all your fall drinking pleasures…the prodigal PEJU has returned! We’ve got a few friends in the valley that keep us rolling in spectacular deals for y’all, but these past few WORLD-BREAKERS from Peju have sent shockwaves across the Last Bottle universe. Welp, here…we…GO AGAIN! 94 POINTS, 60% OFF, all on this Right Bank-style Bordeaux blend (boosted with a spice and complexity multiplying 2% zin, 1% pinot noir, and 1% sangiovese – ‘cause we’re the U.S. of A. and we do what we want!!!). 

Oh me, oh my, does it ever work out here – with layer upon layer of ripe raspberries, and spiced cherries, with raw cedar shavings, and toasted oak laced with vanilla bean, then cocoa powder, tartly sweet pomegranate syrup, black pepper, roasted thyme and lavender, and…AND! Big and beautifully round, with tannins that have perfectly softened over these last six years – simply a JUGGERNAUT of Napa Valley goodness that shatters all price expectations in the way that only Last Bottle can! Good luck finding this beauty elsewhere – but I promise you, even if you could…it won’t be anywhere near our impressive 60% OFF!

2018’s legend grows ever larger, good people, and the bold, decadent flair of the vintage is alive and well in this baby! Get six and you’ll wish you got twelve, twelve and you’ll kick yourself for not getting twenty! You simply can’t have enough of this in your collection – especially for the incredible price of just $20! I said 94 POINTS, right? Go, go, GOOOO!!!

title: Waypoint Zinfandel Bell Road Vineyard Amador County 2019 (Magnum 1.5L)
body_html:
Amador on the board, people! 93 POINT MAGNUMS are here to SLAY! Editor’s Choice – no less – from the always reliable, and eminently lip-smacking Bell Road vineyard! Zin-possibly delicious stuff – for an INSANE $42 price!!!

High up in the Sierra Foothills, from a single block overlooking a picturesque tableaux of a mountain creek flowing down into a pristine lake, this OLD VINE zinfandel is enough to make a grizzled, old wine slinger tear up. This…THIS(!) is what zinfandel is supposed to be. Juicy, robust, absolutely jam-packed with cherries, blackberries, blueberries, currants, and plums – and interspersed with bountiful accents of black peppercorns, roasted herbs, exotic ginger, and Thai basil…simply never-ending complexity for a $42 magnum of zinfandel!!! Over 50% OFF too? You Z.A.P. heads need to grab a boatload of this!!!
