title: Eden Rift Pinot Noir Estate Cienega Valley 2019
body_html:
96 POINTS! How’s that for an encore from former CALERA winemaker Cory Waller? This is just SILLY good pinot – even at the full retail…but at OUR price? UNBEATABLE!

EDEN RIFT, good people! This is a new project, from an OLD vineyard (California’s oldest continually farmed vineyard – 1849!!!) headed up by a winemaker at the very PEAK of his powers. If you LOVE Calera or loved them before they sold to Duckhorn, or just love epic Cali-coastal pinot noir – then this is your CHANCE! Dodge, duck, dip, dive, and dodge your way to your cart…before Patches O’Houlihan gives you the wrench treatment for missing out! I’ve locked in a case – this is one you simply don’t want to MISS! 

$48 at the winery is just GONZO for this – easily competes with pinots TWICE this price – but our dizzying, completely disorienting price of ONLY $25?!?! That’s a whole new level of insanity. The juice? JAMMING! Deep and floral, with rose petals, cranberry, rooibos tea…just SEXY as all get out in that classic cool-climate Cali-coastal pinot style. This beauty is ho-ho-hopping with Christmas spice notes of clove, and cinnamon, nutmeg, dried cherries, orange peel…and…AND! Soft and silky, too – but it glides across the tongue with enough warmth you just might imagine you’re next to a crackling fire with Nat King Cole humming away in the background. It’s a VIBE – fo’ sho’! Don’t let the delicate color and gentile demeanor (like a proper Belle of the South) fool ya – we’ve got some sass, spice, and bless-your-heart persistence here! Crazy long finish, too – giving those Damson plum and blackberry flavors, with even a touch of dusty mocha on the finish. I drained my glass in three gulps, then went back for more. You saw the 96 POINTS from the good folks at Wine Enthusiast, right? California’s oldest vineyard? Calera’s former winemaker? What MORE do you want?! Go, go, GOOOOOO!!!
 
** The Eden Rift Estate is 125 acres in a valley tucked just under a mountain range, a stone’s throw from the Pacific, just outside of Monterey, and right on top of the San Andreas Fault. Mt. Eden, Swan, and Calera heritage clones, as well as some Pommard (clone 4) and a smattering of Dijon clones…this is straight Pinot Shangri La! **

title: Artesa Winery Pinot Noir Estate Los Carneros 2019
body_html:
Who loves world-class Carneros pinot? Yeah, me too…most especially when I can snag it for over 60% OFF! Don’t get befuddled by our EPICALLY low price – this ain’t your grocery store’s Artesa pinot – but rather a small-production, ESTATE VINEYARD bottling you won’t find anywhere else! Two options…the winery (at more than double this price), and US!!!! 

Let’s gooooooo!! Hell, our simply GOBSMACKING price is even over 50% less than what Artesa’s own club members pay for this beauty! Way, way, WAY less than their best price – we’re talking UNDER $20, people! Lock, stock, and sop this one up, friends…quick-like!

The ROCKSTAR 2019 vintage, the cool, wind-swept climes of Napa’s Los Carneros district, tucked up under the shadows and forestry of Mt. Veeder…this seductive and sultry pinot noir has it all!!! The smells! The flavors! I swoon…and I never swoon on a Tuesday. (Least favorite day of the week, always has been…until TODAY!) Floral and pretty, with fresh rose petals and bright pomegranate tea, then hints of spiced cranberries, and smoky cinnamon, with sweet red bell peppers, and dusty little crunches of cocoa nibs. It’s rich, but never heavy, with a delicately interwoven acidity that is really only perceptible on the long, enduring finish. Wowza…this is a HECK of a pinot for Napa, for California, at ANY price. I say again…UNDER $20? Go. Just…go. No scores on this baby (smaller production, remember?), but as their base-level pinot (that combines purchased fruit with estate fruit at a nationwide production capability) got a solid 92 points – I think it’s fair to say this is deserving of a 94, at least! 

This is ready to go, the moment it hits your hot little hands. Wicked-delish! These Barcelona-born folks are Spanish royalty in the wine world – been at it for generations, with the top spot in Spain added to their impressive portfolio since 1551! They’ve been killing it with the estate Artesa wines for almost 30 years – but most only know them for the larger production chardonnay and pinot noir. Do yourself a solid and sneak a peek behind this elegant and elaborate curtain…you’ll be stoked you did! OVER 60% OFF on this baby? What are you waiting for?!?!

** As a former architecture student (before I discovered just how much MATH was involved), I can say this – the winery is freaking spectacular! Designed by Barca-born architect Domingo Triay – the pools, the pyramids, the monolithic and grand style of the site – it’s worth a visit, even without wine! Check it out next time you come visit us here...in the meantime drink it, at by far, the best price on the planet!! **

title: Reynvaan Vineyards Bordeaux Blend The Classic Walla Walla Valley 2018
body_html:
Walla Walla Bing BANG! OVER 60% OFF! 97 POINTS! The good people of Reynvaan have done it AGAIN! Cabernet sauvignon, and franc, and petit verdot and merlot…that Jeb Dunnuck called one “rock star of a wine” – at a price you can’t find ANYWHERE else!

Game on, broham! This is one epic Bordeaux blend, and as any of you that were fast enough to snag the syrah we featured a couple weeks back know – Reynvaan is one of Washington State’s tippy-top best producers! Matt Reynvaan took on the winemaker role in 2010, learning the craft and making wine hand in hand for YEARS with the one and only Christophe Baron, you know – the GODFATHER of Walla Walla and creator of the iconic CAYUSE (of the uncountable 100 pt. scores). Christophe last consulted in 2017 – after 12 years working alongside Matt – but as this vineyard called “In The Rocks" stands adjacent to Baron’s own “En Cerise Vineyard”...you can only imagine a little healthy competition among neighbors, that continues to this day!  And I’ll tell you what – pound for pound, dollar for dollar – these Reynvaan wines stand toe-to-toe with the best Cayuse wines I've ever had...and that's saying something!

I mean, C’MON! This price?! This will absolutely SMOKE a truckload of $100++ Cali-reds and Bordeauxs – and clocking in at just under 14% abv, this perfectly straddles the balance of ripe fruits, uplifting energy, and brightness, with that gorgeous kiss of toasty oak. Crème de cassis, and lilacs, with this inky, purple-y fruit character of plums, and boysenberries, some brandied cherries, with this exotic tropical flourish of passionfruit. Hints of chocolate, with rose petals, and bay laurel, too, plus this lingering smooch of cherry bitters, and a soupçon of Tahitian vanilla…just EPIC y’all! We don’t have enough…at least of this particular wine (hint, hint.) 97 POINTS! 60+% OFF! Go, go, GOOOO!

** More Reynvaan to come? Possibly. Stay tuned, friends! **

title: Reynvaan Family Vineyards Syrah Estate The Contender Walla Walla Valley 2017
body_html:
The Contender – climbing up the ranks, a raw, tangled, powerhouse of talent, pedigree, and gumption…fully worthy of a title shot for best QVR syrah in North America! OVER 67% OFF, good people!!! 95% syrah, 5% marsanne – co-fermented together (a textured, aromatic, and powerful Rhone white grape that really makes this whole deal POP)!

Oh, BABY!!! This is just some smokin’ DELECTABLE goodness, y’all! Bursting with fresh-picked raspberries and blackberries, some gloriously smoky SPECK ham, with this beguiling stone fruit note of nectarines and plums, all heightened with this floral note of star jasmine, and hibiscus, and then grounded just as quickly with this crushed gravel and scorched earth minerality. It’s a roller-coaster ride of awesomeness – simply classic syrah that you won’t believe for such a stupendous price! Nearly 70% off? Barely any to share – so to the fastest fingers go the spoils, friends!

Let’s gooooo!

title: Peju Winery Cabernet Sauvignon Legacy Collection Napa Valley 2021
body_html:
Napaaaaaa caaaaab! Sing it loud, let it echo from Diamond Mountain down to Coombsville! WAY, WAY over 60% OFF, this is a day to crank up your favorite song, tell your secret crush how you feel, spoil your pup rotten with extra treats – just whatever you do, do it with vim and vigor – ‘cause we’ve scored one EPIC deal on 94 POINT Napa cab!

This will FLY!

** Seriously, tho. You might want to lock some in and read the rest of our exuberant joy about this later….**

Ok…PEJU! Here…we…GO! 94 POINTS from that eccentric winery that you can't help but love! Sure, they stripped trees naked, and then bent them into candy canes with tension wires to craft what is surely the oddest, not-quite-tree tunnel in the world…but HEY! Art, man…art. For me, this is all about what’s in the bottle – and man, does this ever DELIVER! Wildly aromatic – pulsing with lilacs and violets, some crushed black cherries, black and red currants, with some roasted herbs and sharp, savory smokiness – layered, long, and yes, one more L…lovely! 2021 is already developing an aura of mystified, best-vintage-since-the-last-best-vintage hype…with wines that are cool, compact, elegant, structured, and remarkably pure. This has ALL of that and then some – inky blackberries, cool balsam wood, saturated mocha, and espresso notes, plus this gorgeously fresh and enduring acidity, paired with these supremely velvety tannins that just GLIDE across your tongue. Exotic and lusty vanilla on the finish, too…with this sort of umami hoisin sauce thing going on. ORGANIC grapes too, all from their estate fruit…and our silly price is more than HALF OFF our next closest competitor…so yeah, neener-neener and all that.

It’s just a BOMB.com juggernaut of crowd-pleasing, rip-roaring Napa cab goodness, people – and man, oh man…THE DEAL! 94 POINT Napa cabs simply don’t exist for this WAY under $30 price outside of the Last Bottle Universe…so get six, get twelve, heck…get twenty! You simply can’t have too many of these beauties for all your considerable cabernet drinking needs to come! 

While it lasts!

title: Conn Creek Sauvignon Blanc Estate Napa Valley 2021
body_html:
Man, who else needs a palate cleanser? Wait, am I the only one drinking? Well, consider this is a perfect start to your Saturday too, people…join me! CRISP, cool, boisterous, and bountiful sauvignon blanc, my friends! Get THIS…only FOURTEEN DOLLARS!!! I know spring is really just getting started, but can I make like Nostradamus and throw out bold(-ish) predictions – “On the six month, in the year of the great election, golden, zesty, and quenching juice will rain down upon the masses like water from heaven. The chosen will save their coins, and share the juice with their friends – but drink evermore for themselves.”

Wow…spooky, right? But the wine? Stunning. Lemony and bright, but with a nice kiss of richness, and a whisper of vanilla to make all that pear, citrus, and stone fruit flavor skew exotic. Weighty, but never flabby, this is a beach-body-ready sauvignon blanc that just drains from your glass in record time. Case purchases would be wise here – just saying.

title: Eco Terreno Cabernet Sauvignon Old Vine Lyon Vineyard Alexander Valley 2018
body_html:
You know what’s better than paying $100 for inky, structured, mouth-coating, and downright sexy Cali cabernet sauvignon? Paying less than $20!!!

I mean…DUH!!! OVER 80% OFF?!?! Say goodnight, John Boy! Organic. Biodynamic. Epic. Toasty, velvety. Not a lot. That is all. (Oh, and HURRY!!!)

Wowza! Details matter on this one, folks! Eco Terreno is a stunning Alexander Valley winery in Northern Sonoma, just over the hills from Napa’s Diamond Mountain. Owner Mark Lyon had an epiphany moment over a decade back when he saw a songbird run for the hills from a man in a white jumpsuit spraying pesticides on the nearby vines. It changed his life...probably the bird’s too…and now, his OLD VINE cabernet sauvignon just might change yours (but you gotta hurry)! 

** Disclaimer Alert!!! Other retailers across the USA have Eco Terreno’s wholesale only “Lyon Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon” – but apart from the winery itself…you simply CAN’T find this rare, “Old Vine” cabernet ANYWHERE! (Even Winesearcher got it wrong…)* *

If it doesn’t say it smack dab in the center of the bottle…it’s the wrong wine! The winery sells this Old Vine beauty for more than 65% higher than their standard Lyon cabernet – and our absolutely INSANE price would STILL beat every competitor out there! But trust me, you WANT the Old Vine Cab…you NEED the Old Vine Cab – one sip and you’ll understand…

80% cabernet sauvignon, 11% petit verdot, 9% merlot – all from the truly EPIC 2018 vintage, and grown on a vineyard that is certified Bee Friendly Farming, CCOF Organic, and even Demeter Biodynamic! Of course, none of that would matter if it wasn’t stark-raving DELICIOUS! (It is.) Toasty, and sumptuously decadent, with barrel-inspired notes of vanilla bean, and clove, with luscious dark chocolate, heady kirsch, momma’s mixed berry pie…just lip-smacking, teeth-staining goodness through and through. Black cherries and plums linger in the mouth, with a cooling uplift of bay laurel and graphite that carry this into a velvety and silky finish. It’s big, but focused and precise too! Killer stuff, for real.

What are you still doing here?!?! We don’t have enough…seriously…I’m already working on the backup offer to go up right behind this – and the clock is ticking! OVER 80% OFF Cali-cabernet!!! Under $20!!! What a world!!! 

…and stay tuned!!!

title: Lobo Cabernet Sauvignon Wulff Vineyards Oak Knoll District Napa Valley 2020
body_html:
94 POINT, single vineyard, NAPA CAB goodness on the board today! Just wait until you see that price!!! Normally $80 at the winery…but how’s OVER 60% OFF sound?

Pretty damn good, am I right?!

BOOM! It is ON big time today...but hurry!! Napa cabernet lovers – pull the car over, get out of your meeting, and hang up the phone, because TODAY is the day! Napa cab at over 60% OFF!  If you buy anything from us this week, BUY THIS ONE. We pulled off a sensational deal on this killer 100% cabernet sauvignon (which we would favorably compare to wines like Turnbull, Stags’ Leap, and Hall...stylistically and qualitatively, at least…). Anywho read on for the story (besides the utterly breathtaking 63% OFF price we scored).

Lobo means “wolf” and this is a wine to set you howling! It’s primal, it’s inky dark, and kinda fierce (in a good way!) Victoria Coleman (who’s made wine everywhere – from China to First Growth, Mouton-Rothschild in her storied career) is at the helm here…and…BRAVO! Not only is she a general bad-ass who stands as Napa's first black woman winemaker (and first black woman to graduate UC Davis’ revered oenology/vit program), she has a rare talent for coaxing elegance, power, and purity from this Oak Knoll site. She’s been leading Lobo’s winemaking for 17 years…and if this house isn’t on your radar yet – consider yourself officially ON NOTICE!

Silky, powerful, plush stuff, singing to the cheap seats with all the ripeness, spice, and unbridled hedonism of your classic, no-holds-barred Napa cab. Juicy and full-throttle right out the gate, with crushed blackberry pie, crushed blueberries, and perfectly ripe plums, whiffs of espresso and toffee, dark cocoa, and a chef’s kiss of Madagascar vanilla…HUZZAH! Rich, round, and fleshy mid-palate – totally irresistible and dangerously delicious. It will keep a few years, but it is literally drinking perfectly TODAY. Maybe your willpower is greater than mine…but my bottles will be gone before Independence Day…guaranteed! I said 94 POINTS, right? And the PRICE! Completely off-the-hook for a single vineyard Napa cabernet at this level – but you know the deal, we will probably sell out quickly (cabernet always FLIES out of here) – don't miss!!

** Victoria got her foot in the door working as a receptionist at Stags Leap Wine Cellars…and just look at her now! All kinds of cool stories in the wine industry like that, but I never get tired of hearing more! **

title: Handley Cellars Pinot Noir Anderson Valley 2019
body_html:
Over 50% off 94-POINT Anderson Valley pinot POWERHOUSE!

Handley is a well-established pinot powerhouse just a few miles from the Pacific, in Anderson Valley’s infamous Deep End. We worked our magic and got some jaw-dropping pricing…ONLY $19!!! This is singing and ringing with black cherry cola, black plums, sweet earth, cooling minerals, and a voluptuously round, juicy texture! Throw in some uber-fresh acidity and a long, spicy, lip-smacking finish – and this is a slam dunk – even at the winery price of $40. At our fantastic price? Killer-diller!

title: Leefield Station Pinot Noir Marisco Vineyards Marlborough 2021
body_html:
94-POINT New Zealand Pinot for only $19!! 

What a PINOT! Stunning…and I mean, STUNNING levels of fruit, and savory notes, big yet soft, powerful while elegant – a delectable dichotomy of pure Pinot charm, grace, and lip-smacking pleasure! Just a ga-ga price, for a wine that falls somewhere between Premier Cru Burgundy (Gevrey-Chambertin? Pommard? Structured and meatier side for sho!), and Willamette Valley Pinot Noir in western Oregon. It’s a VIBE, no question. Smoky, sappy black cherries, roasted plums, cola and baking spices, and THE MUSHROOMS! Black trumpets, with some matsutake pine mushrooms, maybe even a shaving of Burgundy black truffle…just savory, umami deliciousness that sets a man’s heart aflame! It’s silky-smooth, with a perfectly integrated acidity – and unreal amounts of polish and sexy texture throughout. Terrifically drinkable, and again, THIS PRICE!

** All orders will be combined and shipped from our West Coast warehouse, so give it a little time, East Coasters! **

title: Pursuit Pinot Noir Russian River Valley 2020 (Magnum 1.5L)
body_html:
The END is HERE!!! Repent (or not) and REJOICE on the glorious wines, the unbeatable prices, and the incredible pedigree that is now infused into the heart of your wine collection! Hope you had as much fun as we did – everything’s getting packed up from our West Coast warehouse soon, so keep the faith, good people! Let’s end it all with a bang, shall we?

MAGNUMS! The perfect goodbye! When, I say WHEN have you ever seen MAGNUMS of Russian River Pinot Noir, crafted by THREE 100 point winemakers, for sale UNDER $40?! I daresay…never. And maybe, never again! This is our cherry on top, Rachmaninov’s third, Wu-Tang’s Triumph, and Heath’s Joker…it simply doesn’t get any better! Same classic wine – with the deep cherry cola, the spicy, earthy sasparilla, the flood of berries, and floral accents, the wild forest sensations…but you know, BIGGER. Don’t be shy with these baddies – they will continue to impress for years!

What. A. MONDAY! Peace, y’all!!

** All orders will be shipped from our West Coast warehouse, so give it a little time, East Coasters! **

title: Aileron Estates Cabernet Sauvignon Napa Valley 2019
body_html:
Alright…time for flight school. “Aileron” is that little wing that steers a plane. There. Back to the matter at hand. 95 POINTS! (Plus a totally solid 94-96 review from Jeb Dunnuck.) Oh BABY! In the realm of CULTY, NEW Cabernet Projects in our little valley – this tiny production of a single vineyard Atlas Peak cabernet sauvignon might be one of Napa’s best! From proprietor Shannon O’Shaughnessy – yes, as in the Howell Mountain stalwart for decades, O’Shaughnessy – comes the bound-to-fly Aileron Estates. They make one white, and one red, only a tiny amount of each, and like, super-duper rare. Seriously, I can only find two other shops in the USA that have this at the moment…and let’s just say…be glad you can get access to OUR PRICE! Seriously. 

Anywho. Forget about the amazing discount for a second. Sure, an extra wad of cash in your pocket is cool, but I want to do that thing where I shamelessly name-drop for like two seconds. Maayan Koschitzky. Philippe Melka. Mike Wolf. BOOM! That’s two…TWO…nigh-on INFALLIBLE winemakers, with TONS of 100-point scores between them who turn out CULT NAPA CABS the way I turn out perfectly gooey grilled cheese sandwiches at midnight. And then there’s maybe the very best vineyard manager in Napa history (Harlan, Bond, Scarecrow, Vine Hill Ranch, Beckstoffer…and soooo many more,) Mike Wolf. I mean, c’mon! Talk about an embarrassment of riches! Ok, I gotta speed up… we don’t have much, and will sell out…QUICK! 

Deep, rich, TOASTY, and soooo entrancing…this just sucks you into the glass with alluring power and roasted chocolate and coffee sensations. Elegant blue fruits – some cassis, some blueberry – accompanied by lilacs and candied violets, too. That first sip is straight FIRE! Liquid cashmere, buttery smooth, with a powerful kirsch sensation, then blackberry jam, graphite, toasted black peppercorns, vanilla creme brulee…DANG! Wave after wave hits (apologies to all my SoCal peeps for the trigger), and this just radiates with a warm, elegant, comforting happiness at every turn. This just tastes…well, expensive! And normally…it is! But for the fastest among you? NOT TODAY! Go get it, CAB LOVERS!

** I was fortunate enough to taste early samples of this fruit when Shannon first acquired the vineyard. It was fantastic even then, long before Maayan and Philippe came on board…glad to see it’s tasting even better several years in!!! **

title: Le Vieux Donjon Châteauneuf-du-Pape Rouge 2021
body_html:
Le Vieux Donjon!!!

It’s been YEARS since we got access to this icon of the Southern Rhone…YEARS! The last offer on this was the 2016 vintage – and we had it at a spectacular price back then, and y’all rightly SNATCHED up every last bottle…$50 for such a wine was a righteous steal! But TODAY! We’re dusting off the Last Bottle Time Machine and taking you way, way back… to a world where you could snag the incredible Le Vieux Donjon Châteauneuf-du-Pape Rouge for UNDER $40!!! 

HUZZAH!!! The supreme elegance of the cool and old-schooly 2021 CdP vintage is gorgeously revealed in this stunning creature – awash with crushed strawberries, hauntingly beautiful violets and lilac aromas, TONS of berries vibes and peppery spices, all culminating in a remarkably LUSH and velvety finish. Mostly grenache, then 10% each syrah and mourvèdre, with a splash of cinsault…this is the ONLY red Le Vieux Donjon makes…and gets all the best of the best to make it shine. It’s vibrant and lively with an amazingly integrated structure that is drinking SUPERB for such a young CdP…DANG! Get a lot of this…and drink it on the REGULAR! At our crazy BEST IN THE USA price? (Seriously, this is less than some big-name online retailer sells this same wine…in HALF BOTTLE!)

Fly…just fly! We had to battle like Rocky to secure this deal – the months of back and forth, the endless hours training in the Siberian wilderness, the sweat-filled montages with endless 80s tunes…wait, what? Sorry…it was just really tough to score this fantastic wine at such an incredible price – THAT is what I’m trying to say. But, of course, totally WORTH IT. Your chance is NOW, to make some of this 93 POINT beauty yours – and I’d advise you to not waste another moment! Go, go, go!!

** Y’all are just too funny with all your April Fool’s responses. Keep the feedback coming (on all matters), we love hearing from ya! **

title: DAOU Vineyards Pinot Noir Solomon Hills Vineyard Santa Maria Valley 2019
body_html:
POW, pow…Let’s drink some DAOU!

Sunday FUNDAY! Consider this your *official warning*! We really don’t even have enough for a sleepy, slow Sunday, even. 94 POINTS, people!

Oh man, what a Sunday! This winery was already a juggernaut in California…but once the sale went through – interest in the brand has never been higher. This is just a gorgeous, sumptuous, high-toned, drop-dead-stunning pinot noir that we pulled off in an 11th hour, Hail Mary miracle of a deal to bring this to you at a just cray-cray price! UNDER $50 for this Solomon Hills superstar is coup that would make Brutus blush crimson! Got some yet? It’s flying fast, I promise!

Jam-packed with hibiscus, cherry blossoms, lavender, and tomato leaf...this packs a wallop before it ever passes your lips! Spicy and tingly bright with bushels of red plums, baked clay, cherry extract, cedar, blood orange peel, licorice whips, pencil shavings, and…AND!!! A lingering sweet vanilla kiss send-off to tuck you to bed – this is velvety, and toasty without being heavy, nicely spiced and fresh from start to finish…if you need to understand the hype behind DAOU – look no further!

Just 10+ miles from the Pacific, in the western edge of the Santa Maria Valley – the Solomon Hills vineyard is a pinot icon of the Central Coast. Daniel Daou said this 2019 pinot is the BEST he’s ever made…and we got some!!! Hurry, hurry and you just might snag some for yourself! Who knows if we’ll be able to score such a deal again…so…while it lasts!!!

**So…what DOES one do in Phoenix on a Sunday in February?!?! Brunch? Spring training?**

title: Ego Bodegas Goru 38 Barrels Jumilla 2020
body_html:
This deal is so off the chain...we can't share it with everybody. Just you and a handful of others like you, to show you what Last Bottle is all about – great wines, crazy prices, best wine deals on the planet – all corked and bottled into a single steal of an offer! We built a separate website just for this moment. Take the Blue Pill. Join us. Drink the best ever quality-for-value wine of your life.  (We're not a cult, promise!)

Ok...Unimaginably low, UNDER $15 price, wicked high score, sultry and rich red wine sure to set your lips a’smacking!!! We know, we know, you’re like, “Dang, that’s like 65% off, holy moly, I should buy a case!” NO. ABSOLUTELY NOT. Buy 2. At least. 96 POINTS, people!

When a panel of top wine critics, industry veterans, and seasoned winemakers taste a wine – BLIND, mind you – and then rank it above over 1,200 other Spanish entries to put it into the TOP 1% of wines in its category…jump. Fly, run, do whatever you must ‘cause this…THIS… is how we send things off on the last Friday of 2023!!!

$14…FOURTEEN DOLLARS!!! This calls for one final James Brown dance around the warehouse for the year (I feel GOOD!!). Spain’s Jumilla region is on fire (a “winemaking revolution” according to some) and this monastrell (aka mourvèdre), syrah, and cabernet sauvignon blend is the sexiest, most OMG-fill-my-glass-again wine you’ve probably never tasted. Old vines, limestone soils, crazy long and sunny, and perfectly warm days combine to forge this rich, fleshy, round, and juicy wine that just ROCKS! Think crazy levels of drinkability, here! Mouth-watering blue and black fruits – like blueberries, blackberries, black cherries, plums – WOWZA! Ripe and saturated fruits, but then comes the dark cocoa, the black pepper, the candied violets, and wild, roasted herbs with salty, meaty olive tapenade and this cool and minty anise liqueur thing going in at the finish. For WHAT price?! I need to sit down. EVERYONE needs a few of these in their house – opening up a bottle of this is just NEVER the wrong call.

Have we mentioned the absurd price today? We did? OK. How about FREE SHIPPING on just 4 bottles?!?! Hope you got some!! 96 POINTS and sure to FLY!!!

title: DeLille Cellars Roofline Chardonnay Willamette Valley 2020
body_html:
DeLille Cellars has been hailed as the “Lafite Rothschild of Washington State” by Robert Parker…but Lafite doesn’t make a white quite like this!!! 

Chardonnay drinkers! HEY! If you like ‘em turned all the way up to 11, break out the Spinal Tap ‘cause today is your day! From Oregon no less! Over 50% OFF!

You always need a good, no-brainer, dry, fresh, and flavorful chard around. Are we right? This. Is. IT!!! A total steal too – UNDER $15! Yeeesh, this is good! A bushel full of crisp and ripe golden apples, and lemon freshness, then orange oil, the perfect touch of sweet Chantilly cream, some spicy candied ginger, and smoky, flinty finish! Tingly acidity, but a dash of nutty, buttery weight to keep this supremely balanced and thoroughly tasty…WOW! This will tickle the pants off many a chardonnay lover – Willamette Valley crispness with a sense of Cali-sunshine! This perfect ‘anytime’ chardonnay – especially at our extremely tempting price! Six of these beauties? Gone in a flash at my house, and I’m betting yours too!

DeLille Cellars has been crafting EPIC wines in Washington and Oregon for decades – one of the first five wineries in Washington to receive Robert Parker’s 5-star excellence rating – and they bring this old-world passion to their New World wines. It’s a powerful one-two combo, and we’re GOBSMACKED to get access to this 91 POINT, utterly fantastic chardonnay at the BEST PRICE ANYWHERE! Load up in due haste…this might not stick around for long!!!

title: Flaneur Wines Pinot Noir La Belle Promenade Vineyard Chehalem Mountains 2018
body_html:
“Hey, Coach Beard… what’s a “Flaneur”?” - Ted Lasso

A Wanderer!

Thanks, Ted Lasso, for continuing my education. And more besides! Ok, let’s get at it. On this fine Saturday (79 and sunny!) morning, we’re wandering up into the Chehalem Mountains of Oregon’s Willamette Valley for epic, aromatic, and oh-so-seductive PINOT NOIR! DOUBLE 93 POINTS, single vineyard goodness, ORGANICALLY FARMED, sustainably maintained, uber-dank! If you went GAGA for that Ribbon Ridge pinot vertical we offered up a couple of weeks back – these are their neighbors – and man, what a wine!!!

La Belle Promenade Vineyard sits high up in the coastal mountains at about 800 feet. Volcanic soils, cooling oceanic influences, and the long and slow ripening season make for one powerful black cherry BOMB! of epic proportions. Bracing acidity, beautiful balance, and this wonderfully smokey/meatiness endow this beauty with just gargantuan depth! Who doesn’t LOVE mulberries, right? And cola, and spicy herbs, raspberries, blueberries, bergamot tea, and this rich, fresh-pulled espresso aroma too – YOWZA! This is a treat! Did I mention it’s OVER 50% OFF?! Woooohoooooo!

Seriously, under $30 for a beauty of a single vineyard, AVA designated, Oregon monster of glorious pinosity – sign me up. But go like the Kerry wind in the Irish summer…this won’t be around long!!! Get a lot – this will sing for many a year to come!

title: Adega Cooperative Ponte de Lima Loureiro Vinho Verde 2022
body_html:
And not just any ol’ Vinho Verde – 95 POINT Vinho Verde from Ponte de Lima – that’s what!

This is one of those beautiful, aromatic, utterly compelling whites that we simply fall in love with instantly! We're like giddy, dizzy, foolhardy teenagers drinking this stuff today. This could seriously evolve into a game of Spin The Bottle if we're not careful. How can it possibly be so good at this cray-cray price? Regardless, yeah, we're throwing a case of this in the truck, STAT! TWELVE DOLLARS?! 95 POINTS! Just…go!

Ponte de Lima is a co-op winery, joining small growers throughout the Lima sub-region of Portugal’s Vinho Verde appellation. Unlike many a Vinho Verde, which can be sharp enough to scrape the ice off your windshield on a cold winter’s morning, this 100% loureiro grape version is decidedly friendlier – and super-classy too! Crisp and fresh, sunny and bright, with spectacular floral notes and metric tons of minerality – all with this lovely, slightly effervescent acidity and a peachy, pomelo, tangerine sort of creamy roundness that is irresistible! Lemon verbena? Possibly. Salty sea spray? You betcha. Just KILLER STUFF…and ONLY TWELVE DOLLARS!!! You will fall in love – certainly, at this price, it might be a going-steady kind of thing for a while. Get more than you think – it's that good!!! You won’t find this anywhere else in the USA!

title: Lafond Winery Pinot Noir SRH Sta. Rita Hills 2019
body_html:
BOOM! Serious pinot noir fans...you NEED to get as much as you possibly can of this one!! We scored an eye-popping deal on this 94-POINTER, from the historic Lafond Winery, which is a luxurious half-mile float up the Santa Ynez River from the iconic Sanford & Benedict vineyard, and only a quarter mile from the mythical Sea Smoke! Now, THOSE are neighbors to be proud of! This is the heart of the Sta. Rita Hills – where only the very best fruit is grown, and typically priced in the $75 to $150+ range…NOT TODAY! An absolute MUST if you love killer bargains on the highest-level Central Coast pinot!! UNDER $20?!?! Let’s go!!!

We did mention over 45% off, right? Crazy. Anyway, this is gorgeous, heady, and powerful stuff – made by long-time winemaker, Bruce McGuire, who’s been at the helm since 1981! 2019 was noted by Wine Spectator as “a benchmark vintage for California pinot, with wines showing power, finesse, and structure for cellarings”...and…yep, that just about hits the nail on the head with this fantastic beauty! Plums, violets and peonies, cranberry preserves, and Christmas spice, and this smoky, savory, scrumdiddlyumptious note that would make Ned Flanders’ mustache twitch with unending delight! Wonderfully balanced, with a touch of cola, and layers upon layers of complex, ripe fruits that saturate every last corner of your mouth. The grace. The power. The structure! Drop-dead gorgeous now or any time over the next DECADE! Wow! And the crazy PRICE? Forgetaboutit. 94 POINTS from Wine Enthusiast! Will go in a flash...

title: Santa Barbara Winery Chardonnay Reserve Santa Rita Hills Central Coast 2019
Stags' Leap Winery Cabernet Sauvignon Napa Valley 2019 (Half Bottle 375mL)
body_html:
STAGS’ LEAP, good people!!! 93 POINTS! The stellar 2019 VINTAGE! Utterly textbook, compelling, tremendous NAPA CAB for a price that is simply breathtaking. 45% OFF on these wee ones??? Time to get another case – seriously…I can’t stop cracking these open! Valentine’s Day picnic? You better believe it!

Blackberry preserves, black currants, boysenberry pie...DROOLING! Velvety notes of dark chocolate, and Tahitian vanilla too. And spice cake – heady, intoxicating, mind-reeling sensations of clove, allspice, fresh pipe tobacco, cedar, and sage...just...classic. This winery has been making Napa greats since the 1800s, and their 1970s-era wines are considered legendary (1974 petite sirah is one of my favorite Napa wines...EVER!). This 2019 is a real treat – an amazing vintage, and impeccably balanced through and through. At this price?!?!

I mean, we’re talking just over TWENTY BUCKS…for REAL?! You can still get these for a full $35 at the winery – but we worked out a deal and we’re just gonna run with it!!! Adorable (and spectacularly convenient) little half bottles, so rich, so full, so absolutely SILKY, juicy, and dark. Perfect for two glasses (and a wee bit of a topper) on your next date night. These are gonna FLY! Did you see the price? These marched out of our cool warehouse right after we unloaded them off the truck a few months back, and then again a few months later, but we got our guy on speed dial and snagged another drop of delightful and legendary Napa goodness at this incredible price. You're welcome. Better STOCK UP, folks, this well can’t run on forever! 

Bring on the WEEKEND!

title: Villa a Sesta Chianti Classico Il Palei 2017
body_html:
Imagine...a 94 POINT, unimaginably delicious Chianti Classico – for less than a movie ticket and a small popcorn!!! Are you kidding me? All from a serene, picture-perfect village of just 12 (TWELVE!) full-time residents in the glorious rolling hills of the famed Chianti Classico region. Heaven, right? That’s what’s been bottled here...a generously warm, unctuous, and pure ichor of Tuscan ambrosia...just for you. At 50% OFF?!?! 

Oh my, break out the fava beans (but let’s stick with a classic calf’s liver, ok Hannibal?) ‘cause this is THE wine to serve at your next sit-down dinner. Throw it in a decanter (not because it needs it, but because it looks cool, and you deserve something extra…), and then watch the magic happen. Your guests will make approving, somewhat unintelligible commentary at first – “Mmmm”, “hey” and the like. Then, straight-up glowing praise, declarations of your hosting supremacy, and unmatched valor in all things good taste. It’s going to be fun!!!  

Let’s just say...if you love sangiovese, Brunello, Montalcino Rossos, etc...and you aren’t a buyer for this, at 94 POINTS to boot, and our jaw-dropping, utterly rad price, well, we might have to come over and take your temperature. THIS Villa a Sesta Il Palei 2017 is spectacular – so finely tuned, mid-weight, purring with robust flavor, silky with a core of deep dark cherry, seemingly impossible for a wine priced as we have it today. DANG!! I loaded up my truck…default dinner wine for at least HALF of 2024!! 

Classic and classy with tart, sweet cherry fruit, dried cherry, sweet-turned earth, and dusty French oak – super fresh, clean, and pure. Plums, sandalwood, and spiced berry compote too! 100% sangiovese from the most glorious hilltop village imaginable. We promise it will take you back to the Tuscany of your dreams (we can’t wait to be back)! Astoundingly delicious at any price…but for UNDER $16!?!? YOWZA...this is good! Perfect for your table all year long! Drink this with deep satisfaction the moment it arrives, we beg you. OK, we’re outta here, grab it while it lasts!!

title: Rubissow Cabernet Sauvignon Reserve Mt. Veeder Napa Valley 2015
body_html:
Well, we went and did it again. RUBISSOW Reserve is in da HOUSE!

** if you love Mt. Veeder Cabernet (or you had the '14 and already know how dope this stuff is) -- PLEASE do not spend an extra moment doing anything that is not remotely related to getting some **

Did you see that price? We’re no math experts but that looks like over 50% OFF! And, oh lordy, the 2015 Napa Cabs in general are superb wines across the board. HUGE! If you've ever driven the steep hairpin lefts and rights of Mt. Veeder, you will eventually pass a sign or two demarcating the 45-acre Rubissow ranch, bought and planted over three decades ago. While much of the highly-regarded grapes are sold, we snagged a tiny bit of the awesome Reserve, made by Tim Milos, whose resume includes Opus One, Stag's Leap Wine Cellars, and Cliff Lede, to name but a few!! Truly an astounding deal...if only we had more!

Man! This is Napa Cabernet more like what your parents probably drank -- big, serious, powerful and ageable. We say this alot about this winery (‘cause it’s true) but we can’t help but think of wines like the old Mt. Veeder, Heitz and Mayacamas. The 2015 is fatter and perhaps even plusher than the ‘14, and it still packs in the rip-roaring cassis, blackberry jam, spicecake and coffee notes, with fresh acidity and a long, spicy, toasty finish. Gorgeous package, too, heavy glass and serious heft! Pure and clean, and singing of Cabernet with some mountain grip and persistence...this will easily cellar for another 10-20 years if you so choose...just a super-lucky small production, killer ‘15 we are stoked to bring you!!! While it lasts, that is...

title: Andrew Will Red Ciel du Cheval Vineyard Red Mountain Yakima Valley 2017
body_html:
Who's ready for the marathon tomorrow? Let's GO!!

Tomorrow will be hands down the best Holiday Marathon of all time! Set your alarms and get ready to rock at 9:00 AM sharp! 

"Across the board, these single vineyard bottlings are amongst the most superb wines made in the US today… Stylistically, there really are some similarities on both the nose and palate from the more recent releases with the wines of Cheval Blanc..."

High-toned violets and deeply warm and ripe berry notes – oooh, baby that earthy finishing spice! Just sexy all the way. If you love NAPA, or top-flight BORDEAUX, or the breathing of oxygen, the taste of water, the nourishment of food...any or all of those, really – you will LOVE this!!

title: Faire La Fete Brut Cremant de Limoux NV
body_html:
LIMOUX!!! Say it with me now...LEE-MOOOOOOO! Man, that’s fun to say. Even more fun to drink! UNDER $15 – for super-delicious, uber-fresh, and simply CRUSHABLE bubbly!!! 91 POINTS! I’m spinning...a whirling dervish of excitement and uncontainable vinous joy that only bubbles incite in me. LET’S GO!

Robb Report declared this as one of the Top 20 Wines Under $100...one delectable sip and you’ll understand. This ROCKS! Seriously...like chalky, minerally, flinty river rocks...a bubbly effervescent, supremely steely tingle of pure verve and intensity. And the citrus?!?!  Is that kaffir lime? Pomelo? Key lime pie? Whatever it is...I want more! Like Kylo Ren more. 70% chardonnay, 20% chenin blanc, 10% pinot noir – this drinks like Champagne at one-third the price! Load up on this – case purchases are really the only reasonable choice on an ultra-megasonic-teenage-warhead deal like this. I’m getting two, maybe three cases...it’s THAT good. 

Limoux is in southern France in the Pyrenean foothills, in the Languedoc region, and the area prides itself on being the first region to actually produce sparkling rose, some 200 years before Champagne (we can neither confirm nor deny this...). In any case, Faire La Fête (which loosely translates to “live it up”) is doing one heck of a bang-up job, because this CRUSHES most every other non-Champers bubbly we’ve had in some time. UBER-crisp, zingy, WHIP-SNAPPINGLY fresh citrus, crunchy green pear, and oh-so lively acidity....basically a complete no-brainer of bargain bubbly. PERIOD! UNDER $15!!! While it lasts!

title: Croce di Mezzo Brunello di Montalcino 2017
body_html:
BRUNELLO!!! We here at Last Bottle get straight up DELIRIOUS about Brunello (uh, yeah… when it's priced as we did it today?). Croce di Mezzo is BACK! With a vengeance that would even give Caesar pause – 56% OFF!!

That's right. We came, we saw, and we conquered! From the magical rolling hill paradise called Montalcino…all tucked up next to two of the greatest Brunello properties of all time – Bondi Santi and Fattoria Barbi (nowhere near those prices, don’t worry… we’re talking roughly 1/10th the price of Bondi Santi here!). But SO, SO GOOD! 

Bask in the Tuscan glow of cranberry tea, sun-dried hay, and baked clay earth. Plus strawberries, dried cherries, rose petals, blood orange, sage, clove, cinnamon, balsamic, saddle leather…it… Just. Keeps. Going! Silky texture, unbelievable perfume, savory, meaty, mouthwatering stuff! Make a lasagna bolognese STAT! 

Croce di Mezzo is a family-run “agriturismo” (def worth a stay or visit if you are heading to Tuscany/Montalcino…), and while production is small, the wines are gloriously classic, a touch old-school, and outrageously good for the money, even at the original price of $80. At $35?!?! This is an interrobang to end all interrobangs!!! Inconceivable! Best to move quickly considering our silly price, as we got as much as they could sell us, yet sadly, it will not be enough. While it lasts…!

title: Petroni Vineyards Sangiovese Poggio alla Pietra Vineyard Moon Mountain District 2013
body_html:
Can't stop...won't stop! Did you miss the 93 point 2014? Worry not! How about some 2013 vintage? One of the finest harvests in the North Bay maybe ever! In a perfect drinking window at 10 years of age and a staggering 76% off, how do we do it?! $20 bucks!!! ** Also…FREE GROUND SHIPPING ON ALL ORDERS for today’s offer (we’ll just add it to your Marathon haul)! Please also allow us several weeks to get to all states (up to 6 weeks). All orders will be shipped from our West Coast warehouse. **

$92. That’s what the world (and the winery) wants you to pay for Petroni’s stunning Poggio alla Pietra Sangiovese 2013. And, not gonna lie, up until about two months ago, I would have said…”who?” But when a handful of these showed up across our tasting bar, I gained a whole new appreciation for the potential of sangiovese on Sonoma’s iconic Moon Mountain. This...was…EPIC!

Vintner Lorenzo Petroni was born just outside of Florence, leaving at 17 for San Francisco with a few bucks in his pocket and a dream to make wines to rival Brunello in California. He eventually found the perfect plot in Sonoma’s Moon Mountain AVA and for the last 30 years, he’s made wines from vines that he smuggled in a suitcase over from Tuscany. Want to really have your mind blown?!?! Lorenzo convinced THE Consorzio del Vino Brunello di Montalcino to let him use the name “Brunello” on some of his bottlings! How’s that for a FLEX?

Poggio alla Pietra (the hill of stones) is Petroni’s estate vineyard, and these sangiovese grosso clones are thriving up here on Moon Mountain. 20% new oak, for 18 months rounds out all that percolating red fruit flavor – adding a smattering of spice that gives this an unreal depth. The tannins are silky soft now, but with plenty of life for another seven plus years…but trust me, pop a bottle of this as soon as it lands on your porch – and you’ll immediately be kicking yourself you didn’t get more! $20 bucks!

** P.S. – Y’all absolutely ROCKED our world for this year’s Holiday Marathon! We love every single one of you like family. Hope you LOVE your wines, and thanks for everything! **

title: Aldenalli Pinot Noir Sonoma Coast 2018
body_html:
93 POINT, killer-diller pinot noir from three supremely famous vineyards on the Sonoma Coast? That’ll do. But at this stellar price…and nearly 57% OFF?!?! Let the Pinot Madness begin!

BAM! Now that’s kicked up a notch (or two, or three…regardless, Emeril Lagasse rules)! Whatever are you ranting about, Last Bottle guy? Let me tell you…this is what happens when you pair up one of the founders of Kosta Browne (arguably California’s most famous contribution to pinot noir) and the culinary, hospitality, philanthropy mega-empire of the Lagasse family. I give you, ALDENALLI! 

This Sonoma Coast pinot noir is a cuvée of three vineyards: Campbell Ranch, Roger’s Creek, and Sunchase Vineyards. Single-vineyard cuvees from any of these three sites often fetch prices $75 and up…and nowhere near the ridonkulously discounted price of a mere $25! TWENTY FIVE, my friends! Inky, blue, and red fruits with the diversity of Sonoma Coast showing all its traits. Rich cherry cola, cool raspberries, and bramble leaves, minerally, spicy, with an elegant kiss of Tahitian vanilla – this is long, smooth, and crowd-pleasing, with enough complexity to satisfy the most discerning of pinot-files.

By now, y’all can probably guess how we came across this… 2018 vintage, fourteen months in barrel, and as you might imagine, a lot of this production was earmarked for sale at Emeril’s vast restaurant empire… to be released right around April 2020…hmm, I forget, was something going on around then?!?!

Anywho, we’re just thrilled to have pounced on this! We got as much as we could, but y’all have been rightly thirsty for quality Cali-pinot of late…so it may not be enough!!! But truly, this is some seriously pedigreed, enormously complex juice at a price like something from a dream of yesteryear. Dan Kosta and Alden Lagasse came about this partnership rather organically. If you didn’t already know, Emeril and Alden are huge supporters of education, helping to raise millions of dollars for children each year through the Emeril Lagasse Foundation. Dan met Alden at an event and became fast friends with a shared love of food and wine (our people!). They started AldenAlli in 2013, and the rest is history! Spectacular vineyards, great winemaking, awesome people… this is one of those wines we just love on every level. While it lasts!!

title: Ogier Gigondas Dentellis 2018
body_html:
In your deepest voice (Darth Vader, Ian McKellan, Michael Clarke Duncan for example), say it with us, ominously and with cavernous, wisdom-filled conviction: GIGONDAS (Gee-gaan-duhz). Again. Besides Picpoul (peek-pool), is it THE most awesome wine word out there?

HOLD ON to your hats, people, OGIER’s (oh-ghee-ay - sorry, this is a pronunciation kinda day I guess), slammin’ good, Rhone Valley blockbuster (91-93 POINTS!) is the rich, complex, mouth-soaking grenache/syrah/mourvedre blend of your dreams, priced only as we can do here in magic-land, where containers of vinous delicioso-ness arrive on the daily at the best prices to ever be seen. Just hit the warehouse and we are beyond stoked. 

$20. How is it possible? OGIER has a Rhone Valley history few can compare with – the name dates back to 800 A.D., and the family in “modern” times has been producing world-class wine from killer parcels all over this fantastically beautiful and ancient land for 160 years. 

** This stuff is hard to get. This is only the second time in 10 years we’ve ever been able to get any, and last time it sold out faster than an episode of Stranger Things (yes, sorry, we’re hooked at the moment). **

LET’S TALK WINE: There’s such a complex black cherry thing, here it makes a jaded old wine-slinger’s cheeks hurt with all the smiling. Sort of Chateauneuf-ish, with all the wonderful minerally, minty, savory spiced herbs too, but BIGGER, meatier and oh-so opulent…finishes long and savory, with a hint of chicory and baker's chocolate – not oaky, really, just packed with fruit and incense-y spices…fantastic. $20? OGIER? Gee-gone-daz?! Don’t miss!

title: Patrick Lesec Châteauneuf-du-Pape Cuvee Bargeton 2019
body_html:
OK OK OK hello, happy Tuesday, yes yes of COURSE you should do everything in your physical ability to snag this 92-94 POINT (plus Wine Advocate 92+!), 2019 vintage (so off the chain in Chateauneuf!!) RIGHT NOW, as we don’t have a lot and it is a staggering $55 OFF RETAIL (**poof goes the wine guys' brain**), but we LOVE this little ditty in the writeup from Jeb Dunnuck in his glowing, shimmering, dewy review (see whole writeup below):

“From a great blender”. NOW, don’t know about you, but our minds went instantly to A GREAT BLENDER, like the MOST massive Vitamix ever made, and in fact there is one (gotta love this Internet thing sometimes) filled with black cherries, raspberries, dark, ripe plums, licorice, lavender flowers, sweet thyme, kirsch, clove and so on and blended to smooth perfection. But seriously – WOW!! A colossal bang-for-the-buck ($35, c’mon!!!), hyper-deal, if you love the greatness of Chateauneuf like we do around here — there is simply not enough to satiate the beast. Hurry is all we can say, like you never hurried before…

Fact is, tho, Patrick Lesec IS a great blender in the Rhone Valley since 1993, making all manner of blockbusters from dozens of mucho-primo vineyard sites. This is 80% grenache, 15% mourvedre and 5% syrah from mostly older vines that range between 20 to 80 years of age (!) and are mostly sourced from the coveted "Cristia" lieu-dit (what's lieu-dit? HERE), which, he says breathlessly, is close to the holy temple of Chateauneuf Chateau Rayas, OH, AND …..

….this is….

…did we mention? $55 OFF!! The senses reel, do they not?! While it lasts..

title: Domaine Nicolas Girard Sancerre 2020
body_html:
SANCERRE! Say it like Adrock says: “KIIIICK IT!”


There’s never enough bone dry, flinty Sauvignon Blanc from one of the greatest places on earth to grow it to satisfy us, you, and everyone we’ve ever known…so best JUMP, cannonball, and can-opener into the Sancerre pool ASA-hella-P! 2020 is a “short” vintage (yield was down), so we got less than usual, just sayin’...


Just south of the village of Sancerre, brothers Anthony and Nicolas, are making beautiful, fresh, flinty sauvignon blanc from a wide range of terroir throughout the region. Many of you may have had this beautiful, fresh wine from us, so you’re already pros on the subject. The ‘20 is here and KICKING! So good! Killer-diller, unbeatable price as always!


TANGY. Savory, snappy, PUNCHY even. Zippy with pure, stony lemon, titillatingly fresh and zingy, with crispy green fruits and crunchy stone fruits along with the always mouth-watering citrus. Easy breezy and dangerously drinkable. Hey, we warned you!! While it lasts!

title: Domaine de la Denante Chardonnay Bourgogne Vieilles Vignes 2020
body_html:
TENSION. No, not that, the GOOD KIND, the White Burgundy kind! Acid! Minerality! Stoniness! TENSION!

2020 is a fine vintage for that salivary-gland-zapping freshness, oh yes. I’m looking at a small pile of boxes here, wondering why I don’t just buy it all myself - I mean, I’ll drink it. THIS PRICE! Drink it ALL summer with unfettered abandon – go! $16!!

We’ve been working with this Domaine for a few years now and the wines just continue to ROCK. Crazy-huge bang-for-the-buck. Chardonnay from the finest place on this planet to grow it, tongue-tinglingly zesty and terrifyingly easy to drink, stainless-steel fermented zippity-doo-dah freshness, 30+ year old chardonnay vines (V.V. or Vieilles Vignes, old vines), OVER 50% OFF retail, with extremely high crushability factor. YES! Bring it!

Zippy, clean and pure, as it should be. Starts out brisk, and as it soaks into your palate you get a hint of tropical fruit along with crunchy white fruits, tart/sweet citrus, kumquats and preserved lemon. Nicely balanced, and just lifts the spirits in one sip. We bought as much as we could at this absurdly excellent price….while it lasts….not going to say another word!

title: Andrew Will 2016 (2 Bottle Combo)
body_html:
We’ve seen it all now. Is…GOBSMACKED the word? Pandemonium, maybe? Dumbfounded? ANDREW WILL? 2-FER? For this…? From the ‘16 vintage? It’s anarchy. However…we did beef up the server…it’s gonna FLY, guys. WOW!!


**Dismally small QUANTITIES – please jump to the site with expeditious alacrity…


Ask yourself: is there some reason I don’t deserve to drink a mini-horizontal of 95 and 93+ point blockbuster Bordeaux-blend blood-sugar-sex-majik rockstar-ness from the masters at Andrew Will on Vashon Island for drastically, impossibly less than the rest of the world? Getting geeky here for a minute, Andrew Will is a legend in Washington, a small-production, dead-serious producer on Vashon Island (we encourage a Google of it!). It is one of those wines that savvy collectors keep to themselves and don’t talk about much -- more for them. Outrageously complex and powerful, with refined texture and the kind of thing easily mistaken for a $200+ Bordeaux wine.  This is, well, excuse us, a pretty manly-man/sexy wine. Like the Brad Pitt character in the “isn’t Brad Pitt still hot at 55 years old” movie "Once Upon a Time in Hollywood". Deep, powerful fruits, crushed flowers/violets that might remind you of Chateau Margaux, and a wine to age for decades, if you like!


The Two Blondes is probably the more sexy/approachable of the two, for the moment, a classic Right Bank style blend that is a little more opulent - but don’t think it’s not full of power, it is. The Ciel du Cheval has more minerality, it’s more tightly wound, in a $200 Bordeaux kind of way. This is a colossal no-brainer…while it lasts!

title: Guarachi Family Wines Pinot Noir Sonoma Coast 2014
Patz & Hall Pinot Noir Moses-Hall Carneros Napa Valley 2017
Aldenalli Pinot Noir Sonoma Coast 2016
body_html:
(Hear it? It’s angels singing…)


DAN KOSTA (yes, of Kosta Browne), and...wait for it...EMERIL LAGASSE. These two legends made a wine together. A TINY, miniscule, microscopic amount of brilliant, laser-focused, outrageously fine Sonoma Coast pinot noir. Inspired by Burgundy, and informed by Dan Kosta’s Sonoma winemaking legacy, this is a world-class, and incredibly hard to find bottle of brilliance that you need in your collection NOW. Goodness, where else would we even SEE this?? It’s...well…we’re just too stunned to ...ok breeeeeeathhhhee…


We’re BUSTIN’. Just wow. LASER-FOCUSED pinot fruit, from the fantastic 2016 vintage, crackling with plums and black cherry fruit, dancing spice notes, lightly oaked and packed with tastebud-stimulating freshness, this is just a thrill ride - a wine to put in the cellar (like you would a fine Burgundy) and enjoy for special occasions (here at Napa Headquarters that means NOW, damn the torpedoes, full steam ahead)!! 


Just mind-blowing to have a little (emphasis on LITTLE) -- if you love getting your hands on rarities like this, we hope you’ve already dropped 6 in your cart and are on your way!! WOW!! Good luck!!!

title: Ty Caton Cabernet Sauvignon Winemaker's Cuvee Estate Moon Mountain District 2017
body_html:
“Guess what! I got a fever….and the only prescription…is more Cabernet!" – not Bruce Dickinson

But seriously. $18?!! That should cure any ailment…but FLY, ZING, skip and seesaw your way to the app or whatever ASA-dang-P because…well, you know…! Cabernet like this, from the now much-coveted Moon Mountain appellation priced anything close to what we’re doing today is as rare as…a blue moon? A harvest moon? A blood moon?

OK. Loaded up? Sweet, good on ya. 

WHAT a rip-roaring deal. Bright red currants all day long, plummy, juicy goodness and pretty lavishly spicy/oaky, man we love the word LAVISH, oh lavish me with under-$20 cabernet, pleeeease…ah, sorry, packed with what I might call “grilled vanilla bean”, cedar, ripe as sunshine berries, toffee, a hint of mint and a muscley frame of mountain tannins that feels more like Mt. Veeder than Moon Mountain. This is a blend of 76% cabernet sauvignon, 13% syrah, 7% petite sirah, 4% merlot (whoa!), aged in 60% new French oak…and, um…$18. ** poof **

Ty Caton has been in the wine/vineyard biz most of his life (me too, come to think of it) and has some amazing vineyards to source from, including this coveted little patch of Moon Mountain (interesting history here) and, oh, is it already GONE? Grab it while we have it!!

title: Rotie Cellars Syrah Northern Blend Walla Walla Valley 2016
body_html:
*** POOF *** mind blown…

ROTIE. It’s a WILD, out-of-hand Last Bottle devotee favorite from way back, before they even started getting the FAT, wicked critical acclaim --- but because we’ve been fans for years, we scored a small batch of the new 95-POINT Northern Blend at by far the best price on this planet or any other! WOW! Not for the shy!!

Really, kids. Not for the shy. This may cause piloerection, cutis anserina, and horripilation (goosebumps) once you taste it --- just listen to what Jeb Dunnuck in his 95-point review said: “...this big, sexy, exotic effort just screams Syrah and is full-bodied, has terrific depth of fruit, polished tannins, and just overflowing amounts of character and charm.” 

MASSIVE, rich and packed with Northern Rhone-styled Syrah -- the tapenade, the smoked meat, the pure, blackberry extract -- long and spicy and incredibly savory. WHOA!! Rotie is a winery that is on fire, so grab this at our superb price today -- who knows if we’ll ever see it again??!!

While it lasts…!

title: Rudius Cabernet Sauvignon Napa Valley 2012
body_html:
OK, OK, OK -- one more!! Why not??!! This will be the last though - really, we need a beer or 3 and some Weezer turned up LOUD. This Friday sale stuff is crazy!! So let's finish up with a rockstar 2012 Cabernet from the lovely Napa Valley, shall we??!!! 92 POINTS, best price EVAH!

The savviest of you will already be all OVER this one -- for the uninitiated, this is made by Jeff Ames (he makes dozens of high-scoring wines like TOR for other clients) and his own project. Jeff trained under the esteemed Thomas Rivers Brown and the winemaking style is comparable -- LUSH! Dark! Rich! Especially in a vintage like 2012 here in Napa where all good wine-growing things converged perfectly. This is for the lovers of "big-gun" stuff --- irresistibly sexy and wonderful AS IS OUR PRICE TODAY! Of course! That's how we roll!!

Peace to all and thank you for playing along with our spur-of-the-moment blowout --- have a fantastic weekend!!

Free ground shipping on 3 or more bottles.

title: Meteor Cabernet Sauvignon Perseid Napa Valley 2007
body_html:
No -- you haven’t lost your grip on reality. This is actually happening…

ahhhh...Napa Cabernet collectors...it’s HIGHLY suggested you do anything in your power to get on the site and snag this --- crazy limited today. 2007!! WOW!!!

We’re just gobsmacked.  (quietly collects self) --- OK. Here’s what you need to know:

* A staggering $95 OFF current retail pricing. Yes, no typo. Hey, we know, we're floored, too. These kinds of things only happen because we’re at ground zero here in the lush, verdant Napa Valley!!  

* The superb 2007 vintage. Just now hitting its drinking window, with another decade or two ahead of it...still hard to fathom...

* Winemakers associated with this project: Andy Erickson (Screaming Eagle, Dalla Valle, more...), Dawnine Dyer (the best winemaker you don't really know), Tony Soter (Etude, Spottswoode, countless others), current winemaker and legend Philippe Melka (well, just too many to even name), a host of sommeliers and other industry titans...

* 93 POINTS Wine Advocate

OK - that's the condensed version, if you're not already on the checkout page --- we'll NEVER SAY how we pulled off an astounding score like this, but just go with it (while it lasts...). Here is more info from the most-excellent Vinography blog (Alder Yarrow).

Yes, it is drinking fantastically. It’s intentionally in a more "Bordeaux" style, but has plenty of Napa sauciness. Really just now coming into its own, drinking GORGEOUS. Lush, layered, deeply complex.  Another few years will only add to its complexity....but now you have violets, lavender, mocha, sweet tobacco, spicecake, cigar box….just crazy good. We're in a bit of disbelief, actually. This is one of those things...just carpe diem that stuff until it is gone...!

title: Txakoli Primo Zarautz Getariako Txakolina 2018
body_html:
CHAH-KUH-LEE!

CHAH-KUH-LEE!

TXAKOLI!

Belt it out with us!! Almost as fun to say as drink!! 92 POINTS, WAY over 50% OFF, killer cool-wind-in-the-breeze, mouth-watering, slam-dunk white for seafood from the cool northern coast of Spain!

Txakoli is all the rage with the cool kids right now and if you’ve been waiting to try it, there is NO BETTER time. Eleven bucks, 92 points, for real?? Yes! Primo’s vineyards are located in the beautiful Zarautz area (the winery was founded in 1953), between Bilbao and San Sebastien more or less, and this is CLASSIC. We’re very stoked to offer it! Pure, clean, racy, very slightly spritzy, and packed with truly mouth-watering acidity and lemon/lime freshness, this wine springs around your mouth like crazy, showing you bitter lemon, tart melon, almond...all in a light, piquant, salty-sea-air kind of way that just says: “let’s party”. Actually, it says: “Let’s RAGE”. Crazy quaffable.


We think it’s almost mandatory to have it with oysters, chilled shrimp, or some such, but hey, it’s just as zippy and fun poolside, beachside, inside, outside, upside and downside, too.

As it has a natural slight spritz (part of its irresistible charm), it is customary to pour it with a little OOOMPH to get the action going. Here’s a little vid to give you an idea, but as with all wines, do whatever you darn well want to. 

Txakoli! Drink it all summer with unabashed elation! $11!! Gonna go FAST...

title: Chateau La Tour Blanche Sauternes Premier Cru 2009 (Half Bottle 375mL)
body_html:
What’s your jam? Snoop? CCR? Led Zep? Cypress Hill? Johnny Cash? Pitbull? RATM? Tell us.

Wanna know what our jam is? 95-POINT ROCKSTAR-PRODUCER Chateau La Tour Blanche Sauternes with a FULL DECADE OF AGE, direct from the Chateau, at a melon-twisting, brain-rattling price -- 50% OFF RETAIL! Now THAT is a JAM!

Woo hoo! This really is crazy to have in our hot little hands. On a Monday after daylight savings no less! A LUXURIOUS, opulent, sublime bottle of classic dessert wine (conveniently in 375mL, which makes it handy to sneak into the movies or what have you) that will shake the foundations of your sweet wine soul. Rich, unctuously textured peaches, fresh sweet and dried apricots, marmalade, honeysuckle, orange flowers and so much more...this gives you something sexy at every sip --- spices, honey, vanilla bean, exotic fruits...just...WOW!!

** we forgot to mention --- HURRY! We don’t have much!!! **

La Tour Blanche has the interesting distinction of also being a school of Viticulture and Enology since 1909, and the property itself dates back to around mid 1700s. We scored an amazing parcel, direct from the Chateau, and at 10 years old it is SINGING --- roaringly delicious Semillon and Sauvignon Blanc, perfectly at peak, totally ready to bust out anytime for foie gras, cheese, ice cream, you name it! THE PRICE!! Unheard of!! Like we said, totally our jam!! While it lasts, that is...should go in flash!

title: Podere Del Visciolo Pian della Macchia Toscana 2015
body_html:
TUSCANY! 

Over 50% OFF?  Check.
Organic? Biodynamic? Check. Check. 
Killer vintage (2015)? Check.
New wine of one of our favorite, you-all-loved-it-and-begged-for-more Brunello producers, at a jaw-rattling price? CHECK, baby!! 

PIANCORNELLO, the producer of Podere Del Visciolo, is a feather in our caps, for sure. We discovered their sensationally delicious Brunello wines 3 years ago and have bought every single bottle they would give us ever since (and sold out in record time, FYI). This is their Rosso, basically, and it is such a massive over-deliverer of Tuscan ambrosia that we can scarcely grok it. This is a MUST for summer and all-the-time, happy-making drinking!!  

Fourteen bucks -- in American dollars! Boom! 80% Sangiovese, 10% Syrah and 10% Colorino --- but all sunshine and love, at least to your humble scribe. HOW, how do they do it --- it’s rich and dark and utterly POPPING with cherry fruit. We’re talking maraschino cherries, cherry juice, dried cherries, cherry pie!! Wow! A hint of sweet mint and sweet tobacco, too, but just exuberantly flowing with ripe, juicy, amazingly tasty fruit! Terrific balance, freshness and length...it just hits all the sweet spots...especially the price tag. Dang!! What else can we say? GET IT while you can!!

title: Domaine Bertrand Gigondas Stehelin 2016
body_html:
“Two thousand sixteen is one of the most consistently outstanding vintages for Gigondas that I have ever tasted...a slam dunk” -- Vinous

GEE-GONE-DAZ! Gigondas is SYRAH, gorgeously pure, beautifully balanced but packed flavor, from the brilliant Bertrand Stehelin, at the best price on Earth! Oh, and a DOUBLE 90-92 POINTER to boot!

2016 vintage -- so lush and fine. Hey don’t just take our word for it, here’s some professional critic love…”Smoke-accented aromas of black raspberry, licorice and succulent flowers, lifted by a spicy peppery nuance. Plush cherry, raspberry and licorice flavors show very good depth and take a sweeter turn with aeration. Rich yet energetic in style, finishing with repeating smokiness, velvety tannins and strong, floral-driven persistence…medium body, a rounded, layered, voluptuous texture, and solid mid-palate density, it’s going to benefit from short-term cellaring and keep for a decade or more.”

That’s what we are TALKIN’ about! We are talking PURPLE! Deep color, deep flavors of raspberry, orange peel, black cherry, woodsmoke, tasty spices and warm, toasty wood, with a surprisingly sexy, silky mouthfeel with fine tannin that reminds you this is serious stuff (just not at a serious price today!). Delicious, complex and shows different sides every time you take a sip. LOVE IT. This will age, too, if you can keep your hands off it for a few years, but honestly we doubt it once you get a bottle open and taste it yourself…while it lasts!

title: Tor Syrah Rogers La Herradura Vineyard Napa Valley 2014
body_html:
Isn’t life great? Here you are, sprawled on the couch, belly full, contemplating another glass of wine...oh sorry, that’s us!! Well, we hope you’re doing the same. Because…WOW!! TOR SYRAH! Can you say SAVORY, blockbuster, single-vineyard goodness? At the best price you will EVER see? Woo hoo!!

The savviest of you will already be all OVER this one -- for the uninitiated, this is made by the crazy-talented Jeff Ames (he makes dozens of high-scoring wines for TOR for other clients) and his own project called Rudius. Jeff trained under the esteemed Thomas Rivers Brown and the winemaking style is comparable -- LUSH! Dark! Rich! Sexy!

This beautiful 2014 (the price, c’mon!) is a knockout -- like diving into a warm, fresh-baked blackberry pie --- but that is just the beginning. Black cherry, smoky teriyaki, toasted peppercorn and cigar box…wow! Completely blew us away earlier today and this will age, too...at least another 5 years, but really, at our price, why wait?! DRINK IT!

………oh, and HAPPY THANKSGIVING from the Napa Valley!

title: Dolum Estates Proprietary Red Cask #11 Sonoma Mountain 2018
body_html:
Ok…technically, TECHNICALLY…this is a “Proprietor’s Red” and not a cabernet. But seriously...ONE single percent more and this is a cabernet sauvignon. And with 9% of this cabernet hailing from the 40+-year-old vines of their Oakville estate…shouldn’t that count for an extra 1%?! It does in my book at least. But let’s not waste time on such trivialities! 94 POINTS! Publisher’s Choice selection! And yes…all for less than one single Andrew Jackson. That’s insane by any measure – and this is just the sort of madness I embrace wholeheartedly! We burnt through our cache of the 2019 like wildfire – and then scored a bit more that sold out again – so DON’T WAIT. 

Did you do the math yet? You can buy this today, on the winery’s website for $82…but here? At ground zero of the world’s best wine deals? Try OVER 76% OFF!!! I mean, C’MON!!! Pick your jaw up off the floor, wipe that drool running down your chin, and add a case to your cart already!!

From the hillside slopes of Sonoma Mountain, and crafted by renowned Mountain Fruit specialist Chad Alexander, this is a masterful stroke worthy of Tiger Woods at Augusta National. Entrancing aromas of the flowery nature – lavender, lilacs, a bit of peony for good measure…just thrilling. Then rush after oncoming rush of blackberries, and black plums, with currants, and black cherries, raspberries, with a hearty splash of fresh roasted coffee, some damp forest floor accents, and a mineral, iron, and graphite twinge to give you this incredibly long and silky finish! Wowee, is this good! Nearly 6 years young makes this absolutely FIRING right now, with easily another decade-plus to enjoy the heck out of this fine, godly ambrosia. Why? Why are you still here? Go, good people, fly, leap, and bowl over your grandma if you must…just get this!

**Don’t worry, mom. Just like the 2019 Cask 11 you loved so much...I secured you a case. Spoiler alert: it’s your Mother’s Day present.**
